  945|       |    /// Normalize all line endings to Unix style (\n)
  946|       |    /// Handles Windows (\r\n), Unix (\n), and old Mac (\r) formats
  947|     32|    fn normalize_line_endings(s: &str) -> String {
  948|       |        // Order matters: replace \r\n first (Windows), then remaining \r (old Mac)
  949|     32|        let normalized = s.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace('\r', "\n");
  950|     32|        match normalized.strip_prefix('\u{FEFF}') {
  951|      1|            Some(stripped) => stripped.to_string(),
  952|     31|            None => normalized,
  953|       |        }
  954|     32|    }

 1027|       |    /// Calculate readability statistics for the given text
 1028|    280|    fn calculate_readability(text: &str) -> ReadabilityStats {
 1029|    280|        let words: Vec<&str> = text.split_whitespace().collect();
 1030|    280|        let word_count = words.len();
 1031|       |
 1032|    280|        if word_count == 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1032:12): [True: 2, False: 278]
  ------------------
 1033|      2|            return ReadabilityStats::default();
 1034|    278|        }
 1035|       |
 1036|       |        // Count sentences (periods, exclamation marks, question marks)
 1037|    278|        let sentence_count = text
 1038|    278|            .chars()
 1039|  4.45M|            .filter(|&c| c == '.' || c == '!' || c == '?')
                           ^278                    ^4.45M      ^4.45M
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1039:26): [True: 1.54k, False: 4.45M]
  |  Branch (1039:38): [True: 256, False: 4.45M]
  ------------------
 1040|    278|            .count()
 1041|    278|            .max(1); // At least 1 sentence
 1042|       |
 1043|       |        // Count syllables
 1044|  38.8k|        let syllable_count: usize = words.iter().map(|w| Self::count_syllables(w)).sum();
                          ^278            ^278    ^278         ^278                              ^278
 1045|       |
 1046|    278|        let words_f = word_count as f32;
 1047|    278|        let sentences_f = sentence_count as f32;
 1048|    278|        let syllables_f = syllable_count as f32;
 1049|       |
 1050|       |        // Flesch Reading Ease: 206.835 - 1.015*(words/sentences) - 84.6*(syllables/words)
 1051|    278|        let flesch_ease =
 1052|    278|            206.835 - 1.015 * (words_f / sentences_f) - 84.6 * (syllables_f / words_f);
 1053|       |
 1054|       |        // Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level: 0.39*(words/sentences) + 11.8*(syllables/words) - 15.59
 1055|    278|        let grade_level = 0.39 * (words_f / sentences_f) + 11.8 * (syllables_f / words_f) - 15.59;
 1056|       |
 1057|    278|        ReadabilityStats {
 1058|    278|            flesch_ease: flesch_ease.clamp(0.0, 100.0),
 1059|    278|            grade_level: grade_level.max(0.0),
 1060|    278|            word_count,
 1061|    278|            sentences: sentence_count,
 1062|    278|            syllables: syllable_count,
 1063|    278|        }
 1064|    280|    }

 2362|     11|    fn middle_ellipsize(text: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {
 2363|     11|        let total_chars = text.chars().count();
 2364|     11|        if total_chars <= max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2364:12): [True: 1, False: 10]
  ------------------
 2365|      1|            return text.to_string();
 2366|     10|        }
 2367|       |
 2368|     10|        if max_chars <= 3 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2368:12): [True: 2, False: 8]
  ------------------
 2369|      2|            return ".".repeat(max_chars);
 2370|      8|        }
 2371|       |
 2372|      8|        let file_name = text
 2373|      8|            .rsplit(['\\', '/'])
 2374|      8|            .next()
 2375|      8|            .filter(|segment| !segment.is_empty());
 2376|       |
 2377|      8|        if let Some(file_name) = file_name {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2377:16): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2378|      8|            let file_name_chars = file_name.chars().count();
 2379|      8|            if file_name_chars + 3 < max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2379:16): [True: 6, False: 2]
  ------------------
 2380|      6|                let prefix_chars = max_chars - file_name_chars - 3;
 2381|      6|                return format!("{}...{}", Self::take_chars(text, prefix_chars), file_name);
 2382|      2|            }
 2383|      0|        }
 2384|       |
 2385|      2|        let head_chars = (max_chars - 3) / 2;
 2386|      2|        let tail_chars = max_chars - 3 - head_chars;
 2387|      2|        format!(
 2388|       |            "{}...{}",
 2389|      2|            Self::take_chars(text, head_chars),
 2390|      2|            Self::take_last_chars(text, tail_chars)
 2391|       |        )
 2392|     11|    }

 2394|      3|    fn fit_middle_ellipsized_label(
 2395|      3|        ui: &egui::Ui,
 2396|      3|        prefix: &str,
 2397|      3|        value: &str,
 2398|      3|        max_width: f32,
 2399|      3|    ) -> String {
 2400|      3|        let full_label = format!("{prefix}{value}");
 2401|      3|        if max_width <= 0.0 || Self::measure_status_text(ui, &full_label) <= max_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2401:12): [True: 0, False: 3]
  |  Branch (2401:32): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------
 2402|      2|            return full_label;
 2403|      1|        }
 2404|       |
 2405|      1|        let value_chars = value.chars().count();
 2406|      1|        let mut best = format!("{prefix}...");
 2407|      1|        let mut low = 3;
 2408|      1|        let mut high = value_chars.max(3);
 2409|       |
 2410|      7|        while low <= high {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2410:15): [True: 6, False: 1]
  ------------------
 2411|      6|            let mid = (low + high) / 2;
 2412|      6|            let candidate = format!("{prefix}{}", Self::middle_ellipsize(value, mid));
 2413|      6|            if Self::measure_status_text(ui, &candidate) <= max_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2413:16): [True: 2, False: 4]
  ------------------
 2414|      2|                best = candidate;
 2415|      2|                low = mid + 1;
 2416|      4|            } else {
 2417|      4|                high = mid.saturating_sub(1);
 2418|      4|            }
 2419|       |        }
 2420|       |
 2421|      1|        best
 2422|      3|    }

 2433|     54|            .unwrap_or_default();

 3608|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
 3609|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
 3610|       |        ENV_LOCK
 3611|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
 3612|       |            .lock()
 3613|       |            .expect("env lock")
 3614|       |    }
 3615|       |
 3616|       |    struct EnvGuard {
 3617|       |        key: &'static str,
 3618|       |        original: Option<String>,
 3619|       |    }
 3620|       |
 3621|       |    impl EnvGuard {
 3622|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
 3623|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
 3624|       |            std::env::set_var(key, value);
 3625|       |            Self { key, original }
 3626|       |        }
 3627|       |    }
 3628|       |
 3629|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
 3630|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3631|       |            if let Some(value) = &self.original {
 3632|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, value);
 3633|       |            } else {
 3634|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
 3635|       |            }
 3636|       |        }
 3637|       |    }
 3638|       |
 3639|       |    struct CurrentDirGuard {
 3640|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
 3641|       |        original: PathBuf,
 3642|       |    }
 3643|       |
 3644|       |    impl CurrentDirGuard {
 3645|       |        fn set(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
 3646|       |            let lock = env_lock();
 3647|       |            let original = std::env::current_dir()?;
 3648|       |            std::env::set_current_dir(path)?;
 3649|       |            Ok(Self {
 3650|       |                _lock: lock,
 3651|       |                original,
 3652|       |            })
 3653|       |        }
 3654|       |    }
 3655|       |
 3656|       |    impl Drop for CurrentDirGuard {
 3657|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3658|       |            let _ = std::env::set_current_dir(&self.original);
 3659|       |        }
 3660|       |    }
 3661|       |
 3662|       |    /// Helper to set platform-appropriate config env var and return expected config subdir.
 3663|       |    fn set_config_env(temp_path: &std::path::Path) -> (EnvGuard, std::path::PathBuf) {
 3664|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
 3665|       |        {
 3666|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3667|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("MarkdownView");
 3668|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3669|       |        }
 3670|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
 3671|       |        {
 3672|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3673|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("Library/Application Support/MarkdownView");
 3674|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3675|       |        }
 3676|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
 3677|       |        {
 3678|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3679|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("mdmdview");
 3680|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3681|       |        }
 3682|       |    }
 3683|       |
 3684|       |    fn set_config_root_env(path: &std::path::Path) -> EnvGuard {
 3685|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
 3686|       |        {
 3687|       |            EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", path.to_string_lossy().as_ref())
 3688|       |        }
 3689|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
 3690|       |        {
 3691|       |            EnvGuard::set("HOME", path.to_string_lossy().as_ref())
 3692|       |        }
 3693|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
 3694|       |        {
 3695|       |            EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", path.to_string_lossy().as_ref())
 3696|       |        }
 3697|       |    }

 3704|       |            None
 3705|       |        }
 3706|       |
 3707|       |        fn set_string(&mut self, _key: &str, _value: String) {}

 3710|       |    }

 3727|       |    fn test_app_update_calls_update_impl() {
 3728|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3729|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 3730|       |        let _ = ctx.run(default_input(), |_| {});
 3731|       |        // SAFETY: eframe::Frame is unused by update(), so a dummy value is sufficient here.
 3732|       |        let mut frame = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<eframe::Frame>::zeroed();
 3733|       |        let frame = unsafe { frame.assume_init_mut() };

 3736|       |
 3737|       |    struct ForcedAppActions {
 3738|       |        actions: Vec<&'static str>,
 3739|       |    }
 3740|       |
 3741|       |    impl ForcedAppActions {
 3742|       |        fn new(actions: &[&'static str]) -> Self {
 3743|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3744|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3745|       |                for action in actions {
 3746|       |                    set.insert(*action);
 3747|       |                }
 3748|       |            });
 3749|       |            Self {
 3750|       |                actions: actions.to_vec(),
 3751|       |            }
 3752|       |        }
 3753|       |    }
 3754|       |
 3755|       |    impl Drop for ForcedAppActions {
 3756|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3757|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3758|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3759|       |                for action in &self.actions {
 3760|       |                    set.remove(action);
 3761|       |                }
 3762|       |            });
 3763|       |        }
 3764|       |    }
 3765|       |
 3766|       |    struct ForcedDialogPaths;
 3767|       |
 3768|       |    impl ForcedDialogPaths {
 3769|       |        fn new(open: Option<PathBuf>, save: Option<PathBuf>) -> Self {
 3770|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3771|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = open;
 3772|       |            });
 3773|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3774|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = save;
 3775|       |            });
 3776|       |            Self
 3777|       |        }
 3778|       |    }
 3779|       |
 3780|       |    impl Drop for ForcedDialogPaths {
 3781|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3782|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3783|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3784|       |            });
 3785|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3786|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3787|       |            });
 3788|       |        }
 3789|       |    }

 3792|       |    struct ForcedMermaidThemeSelection {
 3793|       |        previous: Option<MermaidTheme>,
 3794|       |    }
 3795|       |
 3796|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 3797|       |    impl ForcedMermaidThemeSelection {
 3798|       |        fn new(theme: MermaidTheme) -> Self {
 3799|       |            let previous = FORCED_MERMAID_THEME_SELECTION.with(|slot| slot.replace(Some(theme)));
 3800|       |            Self { previous }
 3801|       |        }
 3802|       |    }
 3803|       |
 3804|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 3805|       |    impl Drop for ForcedMermaidThemeSelection {
 3806|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3807|       |            FORCED_MERMAID_THEME_SELECTION.with(|slot| {
 3808|       |                slot.replace(self.previous);
 3809|       |            });
 3810|       |        }
 3811|       |    }
 3812|       |
 3813|       |    /// RAII guard that sets a thread-local `RefCell<bool>` flag to `true` on creation
 3814|       |    /// and restores it to `false` on drop. Used to inject errors in test paths.
 3815|       |    struct ForcedFlag {
 3816|       |        flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>,
 3817|       |    }
 3818|       |
 3819|       |    impl ForcedFlag {
 3820|       |        fn new(flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>) -> Self {
 3821|       |            flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = true);
 3822|       |            Self { flag }
 3823|       |        }
 3824|       |    }
 3825|       |
 3826|       |    impl Drop for ForcedFlag {
 3827|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3828|       |            self.flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = false);
 3829|       |        }
 3830|       |    }
 3831|       |
 3832|       |    fn force_load_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3833|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_LOAD_ERROR)
 3834|       |    }
 3835|       |
 3836|       |    fn force_scan_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3837|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_SCAN_ERROR)
 3838|       |    }
 3839|       |
 3840|       |    fn force_scan_entry_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3841|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_SCAN_ENTRY_ERROR)
 3842|       |    }
 3843|       |
 3844|       |    fn force_lossy_read_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3845|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_READ_LOSSY_ERROR)
 3846|       |    }

 3889|       |
 3890|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with a given cursor position into the raw editor.
 3891|       |    fn store_raw_editor_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context, cursor_pos: usize) {
 3892|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3893|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3894|       |        let cr = egui::text::CCursorRange::one(egui::text::CCursor::new(cursor_pos));
 3895|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(Some(cr));
 3896|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3897|       |    }
 3898|       |
 3899|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with no cursor range into the raw editor.
 3900|       |    fn store_raw_editor_no_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context) {
 3901|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3902|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3903|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(None);
 3904|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3905|       |    }

 3907|       |    /// Helper to set up app in raw write mode with the given buffer content.
 3908|       |    fn setup_raw_write_mode(app: &mut MarkdownViewerApp, content: &str) {
 3909|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 3910|       |        app.write_enabled = true;
 3911|       |        app.raw_buffer = content.to_string();
 3912|       |        app.current_content = app.raw_buffer.clone();
 3913|       |    }

 3922|       |    #[test]
 3923|       |    fn test_normalize_line_endings() {
 3924|       |        // Windows style (\r\n)
 3925|       |        assert_eq!(
 3926|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\r\nWorld"),
 3927|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3928|       |        );
 3929|       |
 3930|       |        // Unix style (\n) - no change
 3931|       |        assert_eq!(
 3932|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\nWorld"),
 3933|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3934|       |        );
 3935|       |
 3936|       |        // Old Mac style (\r)
 3937|       |        assert_eq!(
 3938|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\rWorld"),
 3939|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3940|       |        );
 3941|       |
 3942|       |        // Mixed line endings
 3943|       |        assert_eq!(
 3944|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\nB\nC\rD"),
 3945|       |            "A\nB\nC\nD"
 3946|       |        );
 3947|       |
 3948|       |        // Multiple blank lines with Windows endings
 3949|       |        assert_eq!(
 3950|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\n\r\nB"),
 3951|       |            "A\n\nB"
 3952|       |        );
 3953|       |
 3954|       |        // Empty string
 3955|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings(""), "");
 3956|       |
 3957|       |        // No line endings
 3958|       |        assert_eq!(
 3959|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Single line"),
 3960|       |            "Single line"
 3961|       |        );
 3962|       |    }
 3963|       |
 3964|       |    #[test]
 3965|       |    fn test_load_file_with_windows_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3966|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3967|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3968|       |
 3969|       |        // Write content with explicit Windows line endings
 3970|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\r\n\r\nParagraph text")?;
 3971|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3972|       |
 3973|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3974|       |
 3975|       |        // Should not contain any \r characters after normalization
 3976|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3977|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Line 1\nLine 2"));
 3978|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("\n\nParagraph"));
 3979|       |        Ok(())
 3980|       |    }
 3981|       |
 3982|       |    #[test]
 3983|       |    fn test_load_file_with_mixed_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3984|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3985|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3986|       |
 3987|       |        // Mix of \r\n (Windows), \n (Unix), and \r (old Mac)
 3988|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\nLine 3\rLine 4")?;
 3989|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3990|       |
 3991|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3992|       |
 3993|       |        // All should be normalized to \n
 3994|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3995|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content.lines().count(), 4);
 3996|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = app.current_content.lines().collect();
 3997|       |        assert_eq!(lines[0], "Line 1");
 3998|       |        assert_eq!(lines[1], "Line 2");
 3999|       |        assert_eq!(lines[2], "Line 3");
 4000|       |        assert_eq!(lines[3], "Line 4");
 4001|       |        Ok(())
 4002|       |    }

 4005|       |    fn test_load_file_strips_bom() -> Result<()> {
 4006|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4007|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4008|       |
 4009|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"\xEF\xBB\xBF# Heading\nContent")?;
 4010|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4011|       |
 4012|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;

 4017|       |    }

 4146|       |    fn test_spawn_file_loader_handles_error_path() {

 4150|       |
 4151|       |    #[test]
 4152|       |    fn test_spawn_file_loader_processes_request() -> Result<()> {
 4153|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4154|       |        writeln!(&temp, "hello")?;
 4155|       |
 4156|       |        let (request_tx, result_rx) = MarkdownViewerApp::spawn_file_loader();
 4157|       |        request_tx
 4158|       |            .send(FileLoadRequest {
 4159|       |                id: 99,
 4160|       |                path: temp.path().to_path_buf(),
 4161|       |            })
 4162|       |            .expect("send");
 4163|       |        let result = result_rx
 4164|       |            .recv_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))
 4165|       |            .expect("result");
 4166|       |        let (content, lossy) = result.content.expect("content");
 4167|       |        assert!(content.contains("hello"));
 4168|       |        assert!(!lossy);
 4169|       |        drop(request_tx);
 4170|       |        Ok(())
 4171|       |    }
 4172|       |

 4174|       |    fn test_spawn_file_loader_reports_missing_file() {
 4175|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 4176|       |        let missing_path = temp_dir.path().join("missing.md");
 4177|       |
 4178|       |        let (request_tx, result_rx) = MarkdownViewerApp::spawn_file_loader();
 4179|       |        request_tx
 4180|       |            .send(FileLoadRequest {
 4181|       |                id: 101,
 4182|       |                path: missing_path,
 4183|       |            })
 4184|       |            .expect("send");
 4185|       |        let result = result_rx
 4186|       |            .recv_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))
 4187|       |            .expect("result");
 4188|       |        assert!(result.content.is_err());
 4189|       |        drop(request_tx);
 4190|       |    }

 4207|       |    fn test_load_file() -> Result<()> {
 4208|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4209|       |
 4210|       |        // Create a temporary markdown file
 4211|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4212|       |        let content = "# Temporary File\n\nThis is a test markdown file.";
 4213|       |        temp_file.write_all(content.as_bytes())?;
 4214|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4215|       |
 4216|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4217|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), true)?;
 4218|       |
 4219|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content, content);
 4220|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(path));
 4221|       |        assert!(!app.parsed_elements.is_empty());
 4222|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_none());
 4223|       |
 4224|       |        Ok(())
 4225|       |    }

 4280|       |    #[test]
 4281|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_handles_mismatch_and_lossy() {
 4282|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4283|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4284|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4285|       |
 4286|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4287|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4288|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad {
 4289|       |            id: 1,
 4290|       |            path: path.clone(),
 4291|       |        });
 4292|       |
 4293|       |        result_tx
 4294|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4295|       |                id: 2,
 4296|       |                content: Ok(("ignored".to_string(), false)),
 4297|       |            })
 4298|       |            .expect("send mismatch");
 4299|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4300|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_some());
 4301|       |
 4302|       |        result_tx
 4303|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4304|       |                id: 1,
 4305|       |                content: Ok(("loaded".to_string(), true)),
 4306|       |            })
 4307|       |            .expect("send match");
 4308|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4309|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4310|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(path));
 4311|       |    }

 4337|       |    #[test]
 4338|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_sets_error_on_failure() {
 4339|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4340|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4341|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4342|       |
 4343|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4344|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4345|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad { id: 1, path });
 4346|       |
 4347|       |        result_tx
 4348|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4349|       |                id: 1,
 4350|       |                content: Err("boom".to_string()),
 4351|       |            })
 4352|       |            .expect("send match");
 4353|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4354|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4355|       |        assert!(app.error_message.as_deref().unwrap_or("").contains("boom"));
 4356|       |    }

 4405|       |    #[test]
 4406|       |    fn test_reload_current_file() -> Result<()> {
 4407|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4408|       |
 4409|       |        // Create a temporary markdown file
 4410|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4411|       |        let content1 = "# Title\n\nVersion 1";
 4412|       |        temp_file.write_all(content1.as_bytes())?;
 4413|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4414|       |
 4415|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4416|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), true)?;
 4417|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 1"));
 4418|       |
 4419|       |        // Update file content
 4420|       |        let content2 = "# Title\n\nVersion 2";
 4421|       |        temp_file.as_file_mut().set_len(0)?; // clear
 4422|       |        temp_file.write_all(content2.as_bytes())?;
 4423|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4424|       |
 4425|       |        // Reload and verify
 4426|       |        app.reload_current_file()?;
 4427|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 2"));
 4428|       |        Ok(())
 4429|       |    }

 4477|       |    #[test]
 4478|       |    fn test_window_state_change_detection() {
 4479|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4480|       |        app.last_window_pos = Some([10.0, 10.0]);
 4481|       |        app.last_window_size = Some([800.0, 600.0]);
 4482|       |        app.last_window_maximized = false;
 4483|       |
 4484|       |        let state = app.current_window_state().expect("state");
 4485|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4486|       |        app.last_persisted_state = Some(state);
 4487|       |        assert!(!app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4488|       |
 4489|       |        let mut moved = state;
 4490|       |        moved.pos = [12.0, 10.0];
 4491|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&moved));
 4492|       |
 4493|       |        let mut resized = state;
 4494|       |        resized.size = [820.0, 610.0];
 4495|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&resized));
 4496|       |
 4497|       |        let mut maximized = state;
 4498|       |        maximized.maximized = true;
 4499|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&maximized));
 4500|       |    }

 4539|       |        let complex_content = r#"# Complex Document
 4540|       |
 4541|       |## With Multiple Sections
 4542|       |
 4543|       |This has **bold** and *italic* text.
 4544|       |
 4545|       |```rust
 4546|       |fn main() {
 4547|       |    println!("Hello, world!");
 4548|       |}
 4549|       |```
 4550|       |
 4551|       |- List item 1
 4552|       |- List item 2
 4553|       |
 4554|       |> A blockquote
 4555|       |
 4556|       |[A link](https://example.com)
 4557|       |
 4558|       |---
 4559|       |
 4560|       |The end.
 4561|       |"#;
 4562|       |

 4567|       |        assert!(!app.parsed_elements.is_empty());

 4656|       |    #[test]
 4657|       |    fn test_fullscreen_toggle_flag() {
 4658|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4659|       |
 4660|       |        // Initially should be false
 4661|       |        assert!(!app.toggle_fullscreen);
 4662|       |
 4663|       |        // Simulate F11 key press (this would be set in handle_shortcuts)
 4664|       |        app.toggle_fullscreen = true;
 4665|       |        assert!(app.toggle_fullscreen);
 4666|       |
 4667|       |        // After handling, it should be reset to false
 4668|       |        app.toggle_fullscreen = false;
 4669|       |        assert!(!app.toggle_fullscreen);
 4670|       |    }
 4671|       |
 4672|       |    #[test]
 4673|       |    fn test_navigation_state_reset_on_load() {
 4674|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4675|       |
 4676|       |        // Set a navigation request
 4677|       |        app.nav_request = Some(NavigationRequest::Top);
 4678|       |        assert!(app.nav_request.is_some());
 4679|       |
 4680|       |        // Load content should reset navigation state
 4681|       |        app.load_content("# Test Content", Some("Test".to_string()));
 4682|       |        assert!(app.nav_request.is_none());
 4683|       |    }
 4684|       |
 4685|       |    #[test]
 4686|       |    fn test_page_navigation_calculations() {
 4687|       |        // Test the page size calculation logic used in navigation
 4688|       |        let viewport_height = 800.0f32;
 4689|       |        let page_size = viewport_height * 0.8;
 4690|       |        assert_eq!(page_size, 640.0);
 4691|       |
 4692|       |        // Test boundary conditions for Page Up
 4693|       |        let current_offset = 100.0f32;
 4694|       |        let new_offset_up = (current_offset - page_size).max(0.0);
 4695|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_up, 0.0); // Should clamp to 0
 4696|       |
 4697|       |        // Test Page Down calculation
 4698|       |        let new_offset_down = current_offset + page_size;
 4699|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_down, 740.0);
 4700|       |
 4701|       |        // Test that Page Up from near top goes to 0
 4702|       |        let near_top = 300.0f32;
 4703|       |        let from_near_top = (near_top - page_size).max(0.0);
 4704|       |        assert_eq!(from_near_top, 0.0);
 4705|       |    }
 4706|       |
 4707|       |    #[test]
 4708|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_clamps_offscreen_window() {
 4709|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1200.0, 900.0), egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0));
 4710|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0);
 4711|       |        let adjustment =
 4712|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4713|       |                .expect("should adjust window geometry");
 4714|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.expect("expected position adjustment");
 4715|       |        let size = adjustment.size.unwrap_or_else(|| outer.size());
 4716|       |        assert!(pos.x <= monitor.x - size.x + 1.0);
 4717|       |        assert!(pos.y <= monitor.y - size.y + 1.0);
 4718|       |        assert!(adjustment.size.is_none());
 4719|       |    }
 4720|       |
 4721|       |    #[test]
 4722|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_respects_min_size() {
 4723|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(-200.0, -100.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 100.0));
 4724|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0);
 4725|       |        let adjustment =
 4726|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4727|       |                .expect("should enforce minimum window size");
 4728|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.unwrap_or(outer.min);
 4729|       |        let size = adjustment.size.expect("expected size adjustment");
 4730|       |        assert!(size.x >= 600.0);
 4731|       |        assert!(size.y >= 400.0);
 4732|       |        assert!(pos.x >= 0.0);
 4733|       |        assert!(pos.y >= 0.0);
 4734|       |    }
 4735|       |
 4736|       |    #[test]
 4737|       |    fn test_is_valid_markdown_file() {
 4738|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4739|       |
 4740|       |        // Valid markdown extensions
 4741|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.md")));
 4742|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.markdown")));
 4743|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mdown")));
 4744|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mkd")));
 4745|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.txt")));
 4746|       |
 4747|       |        // Case insensitive
 4748|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.MD")));
 4749|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.Markdown")));
 4750|       |
 4751|       |        // Invalid extensions
 4752|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.pdf")));
 4753|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.docx")));
 4754|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.html")));
 4755|       |
 4756|       |        // No extension
 4757|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test")));
 4758|       |    }

 4760|       |    #[test]
 4761|       |    fn test_scan_directory() -> Result<()> {
 4762|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4763|       |        let dir_path = temp_dir.path();
 4764|       |
 4765|       |        // Create test files
 4766|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("zebra.md"), "# Z")?;
 4767|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("alpha.md"), "# A")?;
 4768|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("image.png"), "fake")?;
 4769|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("beta.markdown"), "# B")?;
 4770|       |
 4771|       |        // Create subdirectory (should be ignored)
 4772|       |        std::fs::create_dir(dir_path.join("subdir"))?;
 4773|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("subdir/nested.md"), "# N")?;
 4774|       |
 4775|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4776|       |        let files = app.scan_directory(dir_path)?;
 4777|       |
 4778|       |        // Should find 3 markdown files (alpha, beta, zebra)
 4779|       |        // Should NOT find image.png or nested.md
 4780|       |        assert_eq!(files.len(), 3);
 4781|       |
 4782|       |        // Should be sorted alphabetically
 4783|       |        assert!(files[0].ends_with("alpha.md"));
 4784|       |        assert!(files[1].ends_with("beta.markdown"));
 4785|       |        assert!(files[2].ends_with("zebra.md"));
 4786|       |
 4787|       |        Ok(())
 4788|       |    }

 4919|       |    #[test]
 4920|       |    fn test_queue_navigation() -> Result<()> {
 4921|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4922|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4923|       |
 4924|       |        let file1 = temp_dir.path().join("file1.md");
 4925|       |        let file2 = temp_dir.path().join("file2.md");
 4926|       |
 4927|       |        std::fs::write(&file1, "# File 1")?;
 4928|       |        std::fs::write(&file2, "# File 2")?;
 4929|       |
 4930|       |        // Load first file and queue second
 4931|       |        app.load_file(file1.clone(), true)?;
 4932|       |        app.pending_files.push_back(file2.clone());
 4933|       |
 4934|       |        assert!(app.can_navigate_forward());
 4935|       |        app.navigate_forward();
 4936|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(file2));
 4937|       |        assert!(app.pending_files.is_empty());
 4938|       |
 4939|       |        Ok(())
 4940|       |    }

 4980|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4981|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 4982|       |        app.write_enabled = false;
 4983|       |        app.raw_focus_requested = false;

 5028|       |    #[test]
 5029|       |    fn test_update_impl_rendered_view_runs() -> Result<()> {
 5030|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5031|       |        app.nav_request = Some(NavigationRequest::PageDown);
 5032|       |        app.toggle_fullscreen = true;
 5033|       |
 5034|       |        let mut temp_file = tempfile::Builder::new().suffix(".md").tempfile()?;
 5035|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"# Dropped\nContent")?;
 5036|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 5037|       |
 5038|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5039|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 5040|       |        let vp = input
 5041|       |            .viewports
 5042|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 5043|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 5044|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 5045|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 5046|       |            egui::pos2(1200.0, 900.0),
 5047|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 5048|       |        ));
 5049|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 5050|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 5051|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 5052|       |        ));
 5053|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 5054|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 5055|       |        input.hovered_files.push(egui::HoveredFile {
 5056|       |            path: Some(temp_file.path().to_path_buf()),
 5057|       |            ..Default::default()
 5058|       |        });
 5059|       |        input.dropped_files.push(egui::DroppedFile {
 5060|       |            path: Some(temp_file.path().to_path_buf()),
 5061|       |            ..Default::default()
 5062|       |        });
 5063|       |
 5064|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 5065|       |
 5066|       |        assert!(app.drag_hover);
 5067|       |        assert!(app.current_file.is_some());
 5068|       |        assert!(app.last_window_pos.is_some());
 5069|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_some());
 5070|       |        Ok(())
 5071|       |    }

 5126|       |    #[test]
 5127|       |    fn test_update_impl_releases_gpu_textures_when_minimized_and_restores_after() {
 5128|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5129|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5130|       |
 5131|       |        let mut minimized = default_input();
 5132|       |        let minimized_vp = minimized
 5133|       |            .viewports
 5134|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 5135|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 5136|       |        minimized_vp.minimized = Some(true);
 5137|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, minimized);
 5138|       |        assert!(app.gpu_textures_released);
 5139|       |
 5140|       |        let mut restored = default_input();
 5141|       |        let restored_vp = restored
 5142|       |            .viewports
 5143|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 5144|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 5145|       |        restored_vp.minimized = Some(false);
 5146|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, restored);
 5147|       |        assert!(!app.gpu_textures_released);
 5148|       |    }

 5216|       |        let output_path = temp_dir.path().join("shot.png");

 5218|       |            content_only: true,
 5219|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 5220|       |            font_source: Some("TestFont".to_string()),

 5222|       |        };
 5223|       |        let snapshot = ScreenshotSnapshot {
 5224|       |            content_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 5225|       |                egui::pos2(10.0, 5.0),
 5226|       |                egui::vec2(20.0, 10.0),
 5227|       |            )),
 5228|       |            stable_frames: 3,

 5230|       |            ..test_screenshot_snapshot(config)
 5231|       |        };
 5232|       |

 5234|       |        image.pixels[0] = Color32::from_rgb(10, 20, 30);

 5248|       |    #[test]
 5249|       |    fn test_update_screenshot_state_requests_and_scrolls() {
 5250|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 5251|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 5252|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 5253|       |            theme: ScreenshotTheme::Dark,
 5254|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().join("shot.png"))
 5255|       |        };
 5256|       |
 5257|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5258|       |        app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
 5259|       |
 5260|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5261|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 5262|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(100.0, 200.0),
 5263|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 100.0)),
 5264|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 5265|       |        };
 5266|       |        app.update_screenshot_state(&ctx, Some(1), Some(snapshot));
 5267|       |
 5268|       |        let target_offset = app
 5269|       |            .screenshot
 5270|       |            .as_ref()
 5271|       |            .and_then(|state| state.scroll_offset)
 5272|       |            .unwrap_or(0.0);
 5273|       |        let snapshot_ready = ScrollSnapshot {
 5274|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(100.0, 200.0),
 5275|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 100.0)),
 5276|       |            offset_y: target_offset,
 5277|       |        };
 5278|       |        app.update_screenshot_state(&ctx, Some(1), Some(snapshot_ready));
 5279|       |
 5280|       |        let state = app.screenshot.as_ref().expect("screenshot state");
 5281|       |        assert!(state.viewport_adjusted);
 5282|       |        assert!(state.requested);
 5283|       |        assert!(state.scroll_ready());
 5284|       |    }

 5470|       |

 5535|       |    fn test_handle_shortcuts_sets_flags() {
 5536|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5537|       |        app.load_content("Alpha\n\nBeta\n\nGamma", Some("Search".to_string()));
 5538|       |        app.search_query = "beta".to_string();
 5539|       |        app.current_file = Some(PathBuf::from("dummy.md"));
 5540|       |
 5541|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 5542|       |        input.events.extend([
 5543|       |            key_event(egui::Key::F, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5544|       |            key_event(egui::Key::R, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5545|       |            key_event(egui::Key::E, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5546|       |            key_event(egui::Key::Plus, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5547|       |            key_event(egui::Key::Minus, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5548|       |            key_event(egui::Key::Num0, egui::Modifiers::CTRL),
 5549|       |            key_event(egui::Key::ArrowLeft, egui::Modifiers::ALT),
 5550|       |            key_event(egui::Key::ArrowRight, egui::Modifiers::ALT),
 5551|       |            key_event(egui::Key::F3, egui::Modifiers::NONE),
 5552|       |            key_event(egui::Key::F3, egui::Modifiers::SHIFT),
 5553|       |            key_event(egui::Key::F11, egui::Modifiers::NONE),
 5554|       |            key_event(egui::Key::F5, egui::Modifiers::NONE),
 5555|       |        ]);
 5556|       |
 5557|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5558|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 5559|       |            app.handle_shortcuts(ctx);
 5560|       |        });
 5561|       |
 5562|       |        assert!(app.show_search);
 5563|       |        assert!(app.search_focus_requested);
 5564|       |        assert!(app.view_toggle_requested);
 5565|       |        assert!(app.write_toggle_requested);
 5566|       |        assert!(app.toggle_fullscreen);
 5567|       |        assert!(app.reload_requested);
 5568|       |        assert!(app.last_match_index.is_some());
 5569|       |    }

 5571|       |    #[test]
 5572|       |    fn test_handle_shortcuts_navigation_keys() {
 5573|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5574|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5575|       |
 5576|       |        let nav_cases = [
 5577|       |            (egui::Key::PageUp, NavigationRequest::PageUp),
 5578|       |            (egui::Key::PageDown, NavigationRequest::PageDown),
 5579|       |            (egui::Key::Home, NavigationRequest::Top),
 5580|       |            (egui::Key::End, NavigationRequest::Bottom),
 5581|       |            (egui::Key::ArrowUp, NavigationRequest::ScrollUp),
 5582|       |            (egui::Key::ArrowDown, NavigationRequest::ScrollDown),
 5583|       |        ];
 5584|       |        for (key, expected) in nav_cases {
 5585|       |            app.nav_request = None;
 5586|       |            let mut input = default_input();
 5587|       |            input.events.push(key_event(key, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 5588|       |            let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 5589|       |                app.handle_shortcuts(ctx);
 5590|       |            });
 5591|       |            assert_eq!(
 5592|       |                std::mem::discriminant(&app.nav_request),
 5593|       |                std::mem::discriminant(&Some(expected)),
 5594|       |                "failed for key {:?}",
 5595|       |                key,
 5596|       |            );
 5597|       |        }
 5598|       |    }
 5599|       |
 5600|       |    #[test]
 5601|       |    fn test_handle_shortcuts_escape_clears_search() {
 5602|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5603|       |        app.show_search = true;
 5604|       |        app.search_query = "alpha".to_string();
 5605|       |        app.last_query = "alpha".to_string();
 5606|       |        app.last_match_index = Some(0);
 5607|       |        app.pending_scroll_to_element = Some(1);
 5608|       |
 5609|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 5610|       |        input
 5611|       |            .events
 5612|       |            .push(key_event(egui::Key::Escape, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 5613|       |
 5614|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5615|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 5616|       |            app.handle_shortcuts(ctx);
 5617|       |        });
 5618|       |
 5619|       |        assert!(!app.show_search);
 5620|       |        assert!(app.search_query.is_empty());
 5621|       |        assert!(app.last_query.is_empty());
 5622|       |        assert!(app.last_match_index.is_none());
 5623|       |        assert!(app.pending_scroll_to_element.is_none());
 5624|       |    }
 5625|       |
 5626|       |    #[test]
 5627|       |    fn test_update_search_results_updates_matches() {
 5628|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5629|       |        app.load_content("Alpha\n\nBeta\n\nGamma", Some("Search".to_string()));
 5630|       |
 5631|       |        app.search_query = "beta".to_string();
 5632|       |        app.update_search_results("");
 5633|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_query, "beta");
 5634|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_match_index, Some(1));
 5635|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_scroll_to_element, Some(1));
 5636|       |
 5637|       |        app.search_query.clear();
 5638|       |        app.update_search_results("beta");
 5639|       |        assert!(app.last_query.is_empty());
 5640|       |    }

 5823|       |    fn test_restore_from_history_sets_state() {
 5824|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5825|       |        app.current_content = "first".to_string();
 5826|       |        app.current_file = Some(PathBuf::from("first.md"));
 5827|       |        app.title = "mdmdview - first".to_string();
 5828|       |        app.push_history();
 5829|       |
 5830|       |        app.current_content = "second".to_string();
 5831|       |        app.current_file = None;
 5832|       |        app.title = "mdmdview - second".to_string();
 5833|       |        app.push_history();
 5834|       |
 5835|       |        app.history_index = 0;
 5836|       |        app.restore_from_history();
 5837|       |
 5838|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(PathBuf::from("first.md")));
 5839|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content, "first");
 5840|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_scroll_to_element, Some(0));
 5841|       |    }

 5857|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5858|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 5859|       |        let good1 = temp_dir.path().join("good1.md");
 5860|       |        let good2 = temp_dir.path().join("good2.md");
 5861|       |        let bad = temp_dir.path().join("bad.pdf");
 5862|       |        std::fs::write(&good1, "# Good1")?;
 5863|       |        std::fs::write(&good2, "# Good2")?;
 5864|       |        std::fs::write(&bad, "nope")?;
 5865|       |
 5866|       |        app.handle_file_drop(vec![bad, good1.clone(), good2.clone()]);
 5867|       |
 5868|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(good1));
 5869|       |        assert!(app.pending_files.contains(&good2));
 5870|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_some());
 5871|       |        Ok(())
 5872|       |    }

 5900|       |    #[test]
 5901|       |    fn test_move_raw_cursor_lines_up_and_down() {
 5902|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5903|       |        setup_raw_write_mode(&mut app, "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3");
 5904|       |
 5905|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5906|       |        store_raw_editor_cursor(&ctx, 0);
 5907|       |
 5908|       |        app.move_raw_cursor_lines(&ctx, 1);
 5909|       |        let down_idx = app.raw_cursor.unwrap_or(0);
 5910|       |        assert!(down_idx > 0);
 5911|       |
 5912|       |        app.move_raw_cursor_lines(&ctx, -1);
 5913|       |        let up_idx = app.raw_cursor.unwrap_or(0);
 5914|       |        assert!(up_idx <= down_idx);
 5915|       |    }

 6072|       |    #[test]
 6073|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_clamps() {
 6074|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(-100.0, -100.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 100.0));
 6075|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0);
 6076|       |        let adjusted =
 6077|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor));
 6078|       |        assert!(adjusted.is_some());
 6079|       |
 6080|       |        let outer_ok = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0), egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0));
 6081|       |        let unchanged = MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(
 6082|       |            Some(outer_ok),
 6083|       |            Some(outer_ok),
 6084|       |            Some(monitor),
 6085|       |        );
 6086|       |        assert!(unchanged.is_none());
 6087|       |    }

 6185|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6186|       |        app.parsed_elements = vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 6187|       |            "Alpha".to_string(),
 6188|       |        )])];

 6190|       |        app.search_query = "Alpha".to_string();
 6191|       |        app.last_query = "Alpha".to_string();
 6192|       |        app.last_match_index = Some(0);
 6193|       |        app.pending_scroll_to_element = Some(1);
 6194|       |        let _actions = ForcedAppActions::new(&["search_close"]);
 6195|       |
 6196|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();

 6198|       |            app.render_search_dialog(ctx);
 6199|       |        });
 6200|       |

 6202|       |        assert!(app.search_query.is_empty());
 6203|       |        assert!(app.last_query.is_empty());

 6472|       |    fn test_middle_ellipsize_preserves_filename_when_possible() {
 6473|       |        let path = r"C:\language\mdmdview\wrk_docs\2026.02.26 - HLD - Munt.md";
 6474|       |
 6475|       |        let shortened = MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize(path, 40);
 6476|       |

 6480|       |    }

 6483|       |    fn test_middle_ellipsize_short_and_tiny_limits() {
 6484|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize("short", 10), "short");
 6485|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize("abcdef", 3), "...");

 6487|       |    }

 6559|       |    #[test]
 6560|       |    fn test_update_impl_welcome_open_button() -> Result<()> {
 6561|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6562|       |        app.parsed_elements.clear();
 6563|       |        app.current_content.clear();
 6564|       |        app.error_message = None;
 6565|       |
 6566|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 6567|       |        let file_path = temp_dir.path().join("welcome.md");
 6568|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, "# Welcome")?;
 6569|       |        let _forced = ForcedDialogPaths::new(Some(file_path.clone()), None);
 6570|       |        let _actions = ForcedAppActions::new(&["welcome_open"]);
 6571|       |
 6572|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6573|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, default_input());
 6574|       |
 6575|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(file_path));
 6576|       |        Ok(())
 6577|       |    }

 6707|       |    fn test_update_impl_raw_edit_updates_content() {
 6708|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6709|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 6710|       |        app.write_enabled = true;
 6711|       |        app.raw_focus_requested = true;
 6712|       |        app.raw_buffer = "Line".to_string();
 6713|       |        app.current_content = app.raw_buffer.clone();
 6714|       |
 6715|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6716|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6717|       |        input.focused = true;
 6718|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 6719|       |
 6720|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6721|       |        input.focused = true;
 6722|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Text("X".to_string()));
 6723|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 6724|       |
 6725|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains('X'));
 6726|       |    }

 6768|       |    #[test]
 6769|       |    fn test_save_screenshot_image_creates_parent_dir() -> Result<()> {
 6770|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 6771|       |        let nested = temp_dir.path().join("nested").join("shot.png");
 6772|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6773|       |            viewport_width: 40.0,
 6774|       |            viewport_height: 30.0,
 6775|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(nested.clone())
 6776|       |        };
 6777|       |        let snapshot = test_screenshot_snapshot(config);
 6778|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([40, 30], Color32::WHITE);
 6779|       |        MarkdownViewerApp::save_screenshot_image(&image, &snapshot)?;
 6780|       |        assert!(nested.exists());
 6781|       |        assert!(nested.with_extension("json").exists());
 6782|       |        Ok(())
 6783|       |    }

 6788|       |        let parent_file = temp_dir.path().join("parent_file");

 6790|       |        let output_path = parent_file.join("shot.png");
 6791|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6792|       |            viewport_width: 40.0,
 6793|       |            viewport_height: 30.0,
 6794|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(output_path)
 6795|       |        };
 6796|       |        let snapshot = test_screenshot_snapshot(config);
 6797|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([40, 30], Color32::WHITE);
 6798|       |        assert!(MarkdownViewerApp::save_screenshot_image(&image, &snapshot).is_err());
 6799|       |        Ok(())

 6819|       |    #[test]
 6820|       |    fn test_handle_screenshot_events_branches() {
 6821|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6822|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6823|       |
 6824|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6825|       |        input
 6826|       |            .events
 6827|       |            .push(key_event(egui::Key::A, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 6828|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6829|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6830|       |        });
 6831|       |
 6832|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE);
 6833|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6834|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 6835|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 6836|       |            image: std::sync::Arc::new(image),
 6837|       |        });
 6838|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6839|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6840|       |        });
 6841|       |
 6842|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 6843|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6844|       |            viewport_width: 40.0,
 6845|       |            viewport_height: 30.0,
 6846|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().to_path_buf())
 6847|       |        };
 6848|       |        app.screenshot = Some(ScreenshotState::new(config));
 6849|       |        let state = app.screenshot.as_mut().expect("screenshot state");
 6850|       |        state.done = true;
 6851|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE);
 6852|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6853|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 6854|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 6855|       |            image: std::sync::Arc::new(image),
 6856|       |        });
 6857|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6858|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6859|       |        });
 6860|       |    }

 6862|       |    #[test]
 6863|       |    fn test_screenshot_state_helpers() {
 6864|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6865|       |            viewport_width: 120.0,
 6866|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 6867|       |            wait_ms: 500,
 6868|       |            settle_frames: 2,
 6869|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(PathBuf::from("dummy.png"))
 6870|       |        };
 6871|       |        let mut state = ScreenshotState::new(config);
 6872|       |        assert!(!state.scroll_ready());
 6873|       |
 6874|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 6875|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(200.0, 400.0),
 6876|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 200.0)),
 6877|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 6878|       |        };
 6879|       |        assert!(state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6880|       |        assert!(!state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6881|       |
 6882|       |        state.update_stability(None, None);
 6883|       |        state.last_scroll_offset = Some(0.0);
 6884|       |        state.update_stability(Some(1), Some(10.0));
 6885|       |    }

 6888|       |    fn test_screenshot_is_stable_requires_no_pending() {
 6889|       |        assert!(MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 2, 2, false));
 6890|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 2, 2, true));
 6891|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(false, 2, 2, false));
 6892|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 1, 2, false));
 6893|       |    }

 6898|       |            viewport_width: 120.0,
 6899|       |            wait_ms: 500,
 6900|       |            settle_frames: 2,
 6901|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(PathBuf::from("dummy.png"))
 6902|       |        };
 6903|       |        let mut state = ScreenshotState::new(config);
 6904|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 6905|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(200.0, 400.0),
 6906|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 200.0)),
 6907|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 6908|       |        };
 6909|       |        assert!(!state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6910|       |        assert!(state.scroll_offset.is_none());

 7044|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_handles_nan_and_clamps() {
 7045|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7046|       |            egui::pos2(f32::NAN, f32::NAN),
 7047|       |            egui::vec2(f32::NAN, f32::NAN),
 7048|       |        );
 7049|       |        let adjusted = MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(
 7050|       |            Some(outer),
 7051|       |            Some(outer),
 7052|       |            Some(egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0)),
 7053|       |        );
 7054|       |        assert!(adjusted.is_some());
 7055|       |
 7056|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(5000.0, 4000.0));
 7057|       |        let adjusted = MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(
 7058|       |            Some(outer),
 7059|       |            Some(outer),
 7060|       |            Some(egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0)),
 7061|       |        );
 7062|       |        assert!(adjusted.is_some());
 7063|       |    }
 7064|       |

 7395|       |    #[test]
 7396|       |    fn test_update_impl_persists_window_state_when_due() {
 7397|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
 7398|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 7399|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp_dir.path());
 7400|       |
 7401|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7402|       |        app.last_persist_instant = std::time::Instant::now() - std::time::Duration::from_secs(2);
 7403|       |
 7404|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7405|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7406|       |        let vp = input
 7407|       |            .viewports
 7408|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7409|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7410|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 7411|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7412|       |            egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0),
 7413|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 7414|       |        ));
 7415|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7416|       |            egui::pos2(20.0, 20.0),
 7417|       |            egui::vec2(780.0, 580.0),
 7418|       |        ));
 7419|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7420|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 7421|       |
 7422|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7423|       |
 7424|       |        let path = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
 7425|       |        assert!(path.exists());
 7426|       |        assert!(app.last_persisted_state.is_some());
 7427|       |    }
 7428|       |
 7429|       |    #[test]
 7430|       |    fn test_update_impl_fullscreen_skips_size_persist() {
 7431|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7432|       |        app.last_window_size = None;
 7433|       |
 7434|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7435|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7436|       |        let vp = input
 7437|       |            .viewports
 7438|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7439|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7440|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 7441|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7442|       |            egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0),
 7443|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 7444|       |        ));
 7445|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7446|       |            egui::pos2(20.0, 20.0),
 7447|       |            egui::vec2(780.0, 580.0),
 7448|       |        ));
 7449|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(true);
 7450|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 7451|       |
 7452|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7453|       |
 7454|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_none());
 7455|       |    }
 7456|       |
 7457|       |    #[test]
 7458|       |    fn test_update_impl_maximized_skips_size_and_pos_tracking() {
 7459|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7460|       |        app.last_window_size = None;
 7461|       |        app.last_window_pos = None;
 7462|       |
 7463|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7464|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7465|       |        let vp = input
 7466|       |            .viewports
 7467|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7468|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7469|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0));
 7470|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7471|       |            egui::pos2(-8.0, -8.0),
 7472|       |            egui::vec2(1936.0, 1096.0),
 7473|       |        ));
 7474|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7475|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7476|       |            egui::vec2(1920.0, 1048.0),
 7477|       |        ));
 7478|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7479|       |        vp.maximized = Some(true);
 7480|       |
 7481|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7482|       |
 7483|       |        // Pos and size should NOT be updated when maximized
 7484|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_none());
 7485|       |        assert!(app.last_window_pos.is_none());
 7486|       |        // But maximized flag should be tracked
 7487|       |        assert!(app.last_window_maximized);
 7488|       |    }
 7489|       |
 7490|       |    #[test]
 7491|       |    fn test_update_impl_maximized_preserves_pre_maximize_geometry() {
 7492|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7493|       |        // Simulate pre-maximize state: window at (50,50) with size 900x700
 7494|       |        app.last_window_pos = Some([50.0, 50.0]);
 7495|       |        app.last_window_size = Some([900.0, 700.0]);
 7496|       |        app.last_window_maximized = false;
 7497|       |
 7498|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7499|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7500|       |        let vp = input
 7501|       |            .viewports
 7502|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7503|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7504|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0));
 7505|       |        // Maximized window: outer extends beyond screen, inner fills it
 7506|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7507|       |            egui::pos2(-8.0, -8.0),
 7508|       |            egui::vec2(1936.0, 1096.0),
 7509|       |        ));
 7510|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7511|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7512|       |            egui::vec2(1920.0, 1048.0),
 7513|       |        ));
 7514|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7515|       |        vp.maximized = Some(true);
 7516|       |
 7517|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7518|       |
 7519|       |        // Pre-maximize geometry must be preserved, not overwritten with maximized dimensions
 7520|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_window_pos, Some([50.0, 50.0]));
 7521|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_window_size, Some([900.0, 700.0]));
 7522|       |        assert!(app.last_window_maximized);
 7523|       |    }
 7524|       |

 7526|       |    fn test_update_impl_reload_with_file_succeeds() -> Result<()> {
 7527|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7528|       |        let mut temp_file = tempfile::Builder::new().suffix(".md").tempfile()?;
 7529|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"# Reloaded")?;
 7530|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 7531|       |        app.current_file = Some(temp_file.path().to_path_buf());
 7532|       |        app.reload_requested = true;
 7533|       |
 7534|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7535|       |        let input = default_input();
 7536|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7537|       |
 7538|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_none());
 7539|       |        Ok(())
 7540|       |    }

 7544|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7545|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new()?;
 7546|       |        let _cwd = CurrentDirGuard::set(temp_dir.path())?;
 7547|       |        app.current_content = "Saved".to_string();
 7548|       |        let filename = format!("save_parentless_{}.md", std::process::id());

 7586|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 7587|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 7588|       |            image: std::sync::Arc::new(image),
 7589|       |        });

 7591|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 7592|       |        });

 7616|       |    fn test_update_screenshot_state_requested_done_skips_repaint() {
 7617|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 7618|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 7619|       |            viewport_width: 200.0,
 7620|       |            viewport_height: 100.0,
 7621|       |            wait_ms: 1000,
 7622|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().join("shot.png"))
 7623|       |        };
 7624|       |        let mut state = ScreenshotState::new(config);
 7625|       |        state.requested = true;
 7626|       |        state.done = true;
 7627|       |
 7628|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7629|       |        app.screenshot = Some(state);
 7630|       |
 7631|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7632|       |        app.update_screenshot_state(&ctx, None, None);
 7633|       |
 7634|       |        let state = app.screenshot.as_ref().expect("screenshot state");
 7635|       |        assert!(state.requested);
 7636|       |        assert!(state.done);
 7637|       |    }

 7650|       |    fn test_count_syllables_basic_words() {
 7651|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("hello"), 2);
 7652|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("world"), 1);
 7653|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("computer"), 3);
 7654|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("the"), 1);
 7655|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("a"), 1);
 7656|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables(""), 0);
 7657|       |        // Silent e
 7658|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("make"), 1);
 7659|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("time"), 1);
 7660|       |    }

 7691|       |
 7692|       |    #[test]
 7693|       |    fn test_poll_readability_discards_stale_result() {
 7694|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7695|       |        // Set current request ID to 5
 7696|       |        app.readability_request_id = 5;
 7697|       |        // Send a stale result with ID 3 (mismatches)
 7698|       |        let _ = app.readability_tx.send(ReadabilityRequest {
 7699|       |            id: 3,
 7700|       |            content: "Stale content here.".to_string(),
 7701|       |        });
 7702|       |        // Give the background thread time to process
 7703|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100));
 7704|       |        app.poll_readability();
 7705|       |        // Stale result should be discarded
 7706|       |        assert!(app.readability_stats.is_none());
 7707|       |
 7708|       |        // Now send a matching result
 7709|       |        let _ = app.readability_tx.send(ReadabilityRequest {
 7710|       |            id: 5,
 7711|       |            content: "Matching content here.".to_string(),
 7712|       |        });
 7713|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100));
 7714|       |        app.poll_readability();
 7715|       |        // Matching result should be accepted
 7716|       |        assert!(app.readability_stats.is_some());
 7717|       |    }

 7723|       |        // The error path prints to stderr and returns defunct channels.

 7725|       |    }

 7753|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7754|       |
 7755|       |        // Test each Flesch score bracket
 7756|       |        let brackets: &[(f32, &str)] = &[
 7757|       |            (95.0, "Very Easy"),
 7758|       |            (85.0, "Easy"),
 7759|       |            (75.0, "Fairly Easy"),
 7760|       |            (65.0, "Standard"),
 7761|       |            (55.0, "Fairly Difficult"),
 7762|       |            (40.0, "Difficult"),
 7763|       |            (20.0, "Very Difficult"),
 7764|       |        ];
 7765|       |
 7766|       |        for &(flesch, _expected_level) in brackets {
 7767|       |            let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7768|       |            app.current_content = "Test.".to_string();
 7769|       |            app.parsed_elements = vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 7770|       |                "Test.".to_string(),
 7771|       |            )])];
 7772|       |            app.readability_stats = Some(ReadabilityStats {
 7773|       |                flesch_ease: flesch,
 7774|       |                grade_level: 5.0,
 7775|       |                word_count: 10,
 7776|       |                sentences: 2,
 7777|       |                syllables: 15,
 7778|       |            });
 7779|       |
 7780|       |            let _actions = ForcedAppActions::new(&["status_hover"]);
 7781|       |            let _ = ctx.run(default_input(), |ctx| {
 7782|       |                app.render_status_bar(ctx);
 7783|       |            });
 7784|       |        }
 7785|       |    }

 7913|       |    fn test_toggle_theme() {
 7914|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7915|       |        assert!(app.settings.dark_mode); // default is dark
 7916|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7917|       |        app.toggle_theme(&ctx);
 7918|       |        assert!(!app.settings.dark_mode); // now light
 7919|       |        app.toggle_theme(&ctx);
 7920|       |        assert!(app.settings.dark_mode); // back to dark
 7921|       |    }

 7949|       |    fn test_render_mermaid_theme_menu_applies_selection_and_persists() -> Result<()> {
 7950|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
 7951|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new()?;
 7952|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp_dir.path());
 7953|       |
 7954|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7955|       |        app.settings.dark_mode = false;
 7956|       |        app.settings.mermaid_theme = MermaidTheme::Auto;
 7957|       |
 7958|       |        let _forced = ForcedMermaidThemeSelection::new(MermaidTheme::Neutral);
 7959|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7960|       |        let _ = ctx.run(default_input(), |ctx| {
 7961|       |            CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {

   25|       |//! Full Test Runner for mdmdview
   26|       |//!
   27|       |//! Runs all quality checks, tests, Mermaid visual comparisons, D2 visual
   28|       |//! regression tests, and coverage in a single invocation, then generates
   29|       |//! a self-contained HTML report.
   30|       |//!
   31|       |//! Phases (run sequentially, fast-to-slow):
   32|       |//! 1. Code quality checks (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy)
   33|       |//! 2. Unit + D2 tests (cargo test --release --lib --tests --workspace)
   34|       |//! 3. Mermaid visual tests (screenshot comparison against reference images)
   35|       |//!    3b. D2 visual tests (SVG rasterization comparison against d2 CLI)
   36|       |//! 4. Coverage analysis (cargo llvm-cov, optional)
   37|       |//!
   38|       |//! Usage:
   39|       |//!   cargo run --release --bin full_test
   40|       |//!
   41|       |//! Options:
   42|       |//!   --skip-quality           Skip fmt/clippy checks
   43|       |//!   --skip-coverage          Skip coverage analysis
   44|       |//!   --skip-mermaid           Skip Mermaid visual tests
   45|       |//!   --skip-d2-visual         Skip D2 visual regression tests
   46|       |//!   --update-d2-references   Regenerate D2 reference PNGs from d2 CLI
   47|       |//!   --quick                  Exclude ignored tests
   48|       |//!   --help, -h               Show this help message
   49|       |
   50|       |use chrono::Local;
   51|       |use std::io::Write;
   52|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   53|       |use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
   54|       |use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   55|       |
   56|       |const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");
   57|       |
