  970|       |/// Rasterize an SVG string to a PNG file using usvg/resvg.
  971|       |/// Uses a shared fontdb to avoid rebuilding system fonts per fixture (~100ms each).
  972|      0|fn rasterize_svg_to_png(
  973|      0|    svg: &str,
  974|      0|    output: &Path,
  975|      0|    fontdb: &std::sync::Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  976|      0|) -> Result<(), String> {
  977|      0|    let opt = usvg::Options {
  978|      0|        fontdb: std::sync::Arc::clone(fontdb),
  979|      0|        ..Default::default()
  980|      0|    };
  981|      0|    let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
  982|      0|        .map_err(|e| format!("usvg parse failed: {e}"))?;
  983|       |
  984|      0|    let size = tree.size();
  985|      0|    let w = size.width().ceil() as u32;
  986|      0|    let h = size.height().ceil() as u32;
  987|      0|    if w == 0 || h == 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (987:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (987:18): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  988|      0|        return Err(format!("SVG has zero dimensions: {w}x{h}"));
  989|      0|    }
  990|       |
  991|      0|    let mut pixmap =
  992|      0|        tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(w, h).ok_or_else(|| format!("Failed to create pixmap {w}x{h}"))?;
  993|       |    // Fill with white background so transparent regions don't cause false diffs.
  994|      0|    pixmap.fill(tiny_skia::Color::WHITE);
  995|      0|    let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  996|      0|    resvg::render(&tree, tiny_skia::Transform::identity(), &mut pmut);
  997|       |
  998|       |    // Convert to image::RgbaImage and save as PNG.
  999|      0|    let rgba_data = pixmap.data().to_vec();
 1000|      0|    let img = image::RgbaImage::from_raw(w, h, rgba_data)
 1001|      0|        .ok_or_else(|| "Failed to create RgbaImage from pixmap".to_string())?;
 1002|      0|    img.save(output)
 1003|      0|        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to save PNG: {e}"))?;
 1004|       |
 1005|      0|    Ok(())
 1006|      0|}

 1458|       |/// Extract a number from nested JSON structure.
 1459|       |/// Looks for section_key, then within that section finds value_key and extracts the number.
 1460|     12|fn extract_json_number(json: &str, section_key: &str, value_key: &str) -> Option<usize> {
 1461|     12|    let section_start = json.find(section_key)?;
                      ^11                                   ^1
 1462|     11|    let section = &json[section_start..];
 1463|     11|    let brace_start = section.find('{')?;
                                                     ^0
 1464|     11|    let section_content = &section[brace_start..];
 1465|     11|    let brace_end = section_content.find('}')?;
                                                           ^0
 1466|     11|    let inner = &section_content[..brace_end];
 1467|       |
 1468|     11|    let value_start = inner.find(value_key)?;
                      ^10                                ^1
 1469|     10|    let after_key = &inner[value_start + value_key.len()..];
 1470|     10|    let after_colon = after_key.trim_start().strip_prefix(':')?;
                                                                            ^0
 1471|     10|    let num_str = after_colon.trim_start();
 1472|       |
 1473|     10|    let end = num_str
 1474|     34|        .find(|c: char| !c.is_ascii_digit())
                       ^10
 1475|     10|        .unwrap_or(num_str.len());
 1476|     10|    num_str[..end].parse().ok()

 2176|       |    fn make_result(name: &str, passed: bool) -> TestResult {
 2177|       |        TestResult {
 2178|       |            name: name.to_string(),
 2179|       |            passed,
 2180|       |            duration: Duration::ZERO,
 2181|       |            output: String::new(),
 2182|       |        }
 2183|       |    }
 2184|       |
 2185|       |    fn make_suite(
 2186|       |        passed: usize,
 2187|       |        failed: usize,
 2188|       |        skipped: usize,
 2189|       |        tests: Vec<TestResult>,
 2190|       |    ) -> SuiteResults {
 2191|       |        SuiteResults {
 2192|       |            mode: "tests".to_string(),
 2193|       |            total: passed + failed + skipped,
 2194|       |            passed,
 2195|       |            failed,
 2196|       |            skipped,
 2197|       |            duration: Duration::from_secs(5),
 2198|       |            tests,
 2199|       |        }
 2200|       |    }

 2208|       |        assert!(!options.quick);
 2209|       |        assert!(!options.skip_quality);
 2210|       |        assert!(!options.skip_coverage);
 2211|       |        assert!(!options.skip_mermaid);
 2212|       |        assert!(!options.skip_d2_visual);
 2213|       |        assert!(!options.update_d2_references);
 2214|       |    }

 2244|       |            "full_test".to_string(),
 2245|       |            "--quick".to_string(),
 2246|       |            "--help".to_string(),
 2247|       |        ];

 2268|       |    #[test]
 2269|       |    fn test_parse_test_output_counts_statuses() {
 2270|       |        let stdout = "\
 2271|       |test alpha ... ok\n\
 2272|       |test beta ... FAILED\n\
 2273|       |test gamma ... ignored\n\
 2274|       |not a test line\n";
 2275|       |        let duration = Duration::from_millis(3);
 2276|       |
 2277|       |        let result = parse_test_output("tests", stdout, "", duration);
 2278|       |
 2279|       |        assert_eq!(result.mode, "tests");
 2280|       |        assert_eq!(result.total, 3);
 2281|       |        assert_eq!(result.passed, 1);
 2282|       |        assert_eq!(result.failed, 1);
 2283|       |        assert_eq!(result.skipped, 1);
 2284|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests.len(), 2);
 2285|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests[0].name, "alpha");
 2286|       |        assert!(result.tests[0].passed);
 2287|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests[1].name, "beta");
 2288|       |        assert!(!result.tests[1].passed);
 2289|       |    }
 2290|       |
 2291|       |    #[test]
 2292|       |    fn test_parse_test_output_from_stderr() {
 2293|       |        let stdout = "test alpha ... ok\n";
 2294|       |        let stderr = "note: running tests\ntest delta ... ok\n";
 2295|       |
 2296|       |        let results = parse_test_output("tests", stdout, stderr, Duration::from_secs(1));
 2297|       |
 2298|       |        assert_eq!(results.total, 2);
 2299|       |        assert_eq!(results.passed, 2);
 2300|       |        assert!(results.tests.iter().any(|t| t.name == "alpha" && t.passed));
 2301|       |        assert!(results.tests.iter().any(|t| t.name == "delta" && t.passed));
 2302|       |    }
 2303|       |

 2347|       |    fn test_categorize_tests_groups_correctly() {
 2348|       |        let tests = vec![
 2349|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_heading", true),
 2350|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_paragraph", true),
 2351|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_code", false),
 2352|       |            make_result("app::tests::test_open", true),
 2353|       |            make_result("app::tests::test_close", false),
 2354|       |        ];
 2355|       |
 2356|       |        let categories = categorize_tests(&tests);
 2357|       |
 2358|       |        assert_eq!(categories.len(), 2);
 2359|       |
 2360|       |        // Failures first
 2361|       |        let first = &categories[0];
 2362|       |        assert!(first.failed > 0);
 2363|       |
 2364|       |        let mr_cat = categories
 2365|       |            .iter()
 2366|       |            .find(|c| c.name == "markdown_renderer::tests")
 2367|       |            .unwrap();
 2368|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.total, 3);
 2369|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.passed, 2);
 2370|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.failed, 1);
 2371|       |
 2372|       |        let app_cat = categories.iter().find(|c| c.name == "app::tests").unwrap();
 2373|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.total, 2);
 2374|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.passed, 1);
 2375|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.failed, 1);
 2376|       |    }

 2409|       |    fn test_extract_json_number_missing_key() {

 2411|       |        assert_eq!(extract_json_number(json, "\"lines\"", "\"covered\""), None);
 2412|       |    }
 2413|       |
 2414|       |    // -- Coverage parsing tests --------------------------------------
 2415|       |
 2416|       |    #[test]
 2417|       |    fn test_parse_coverage_json_extracts_totals() {
 2418|       |        let json = r#"{"data":[{"totals":{"lines":{"count":1000,"covered":800},"functions":{"count":100,"covered":90},"branches":{"count":200,"covered":150},"regions":{"count":500,"covered":400}}}]}"#;
 2419|       |        let result = parse_coverage_json(json, Duration::from_secs(30)).unwrap();
 2420|       |        assert_eq!(result.lines_covered, 800);
 2421|       |        assert_eq!(result.lines_total, 1000);
 2422|       |        assert_eq!(result.functions_covered, 90);
 2423|       |        assert_eq!(result.functions_total, 100);
 2424|       |        assert_eq!(result.branches_covered, 150);
 2425|       |        assert_eq!(result.branches_total, 200);
 2426|       |        assert_eq!(result.regions_covered, 400);
 2427|       |        assert_eq!(result.regions_total, 500);
 2428|       |    }
 2429|       |
 2430|       |    #[test]
 2431|       |    fn test_parse_coverage_json_no_totals() {
 2432|       |        let json = r#"{"data":[]}"#;
 2433|       |        assert!(parse_coverage_json(json, Duration::ZERO).is_none());
 2434|       |    }

 2437|       |
 2438|       |    #[test]
 2439|       |    fn test_html_escape_special_chars() {
 2440|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("hello"), "hello");
 2441|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("<script>"), "&lt;script&gt;");
 2442|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("a & b"), "a &amp; b");
 2443|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("\"quoted\""), "&quot;quoted&quot;");
 2444|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("it's"), "it&#39;s");
 2445|       |    }

 2473|       |    #[test]
 2474|       |    fn test_build_report_path_increments_on_collision() {
 2475|       |        let temp = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 2476|       |        let dir = temp.path().join("tests").join("results");
 2477|       |        std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
 2478|       |
 2479|       |        // Create existing report
 2480|       |        std::fs::write(dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report.html"), "r1").unwrap();
 2481|       |        std::fs::write(dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report (2).html"), "r2").unwrap();
 2482|       |
 2483|       |        // Verify collision increment logic
 2484|       |        let three = dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report (3).html");
 2485|       |        assert!(!three.exists());
 2486|       |    }

 2494|       |

 2510|       |
 2511|       |    // -- Image diff tests --------------------------------------------
 2512|       |
 2513|       |    #[test]
 2514|       |    fn test_diff_images_identical() {
 2515|       |        let img = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2516|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&img, &img, 60);
 2517|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 0);
 2518|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 0);
 2519|       |    }
 2520|       |
 2521|       |    #[test]
 2522|       |    fn test_diff_images_within_tolerance() {
 2523|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2524|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([150, 100, 100, 255]));
 2525|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2526|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 0); // delta=50 is within tolerance=60
 2527|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 50);
 2528|       |    }
 2529|       |
 2530|       |    #[test]
 2531|       |    fn test_diff_images_exceeds_tolerance() {
 2532|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([0, 0, 0, 255]));
 2533|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([255, 255, 255, 255]));
 2534|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2535|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 100); // all 10x10 pixels differ
 2536|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 255);
 2537|       |    }
 2538|       |
 2539|       |    #[test]
 2540|       |    fn test_diff_images_mixed() {
 2541|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2542|       |        let mut b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2543|       |        // Make one pixel different by more than tolerance
 2544|       |        b.put_pixel(0, 0, image::Rgba([200, 100, 100, 255]));
 2545|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2546|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 1);
 2547|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 100);
 2548|       |    }
 2549|       |
 2550|       |    // -- Pad to common size tests ------------------------------------
 2551|       |
 2552|       |    #[test]
 2553|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_same_dimensions() {
 2554|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2555|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([64, 64, 64, 255]));
 2556|       |        let (pa, pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2557|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (10, 10));
 2558|       |        assert_eq!(pb.dimensions(), (10, 10));
 2559|       |    }
 2560|       |
 2561|       |    #[test]
 2562|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_different_dimensions() {
 2563|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 20, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2564|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(20, 10, image::Rgba([64, 64, 64, 255]));
 2565|       |        let (pa, pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2566|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (20, 20));
 2567|       |        assert_eq!(pb.dimensions(), (20, 20));
 2568|       |    }
 2569|       |
 2570|       |    #[test]
 2571|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_preserves_content() {
 2572|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2573|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(4, 4, image::Rgba([200, 200, 200, 255]));
 2574|       |        let (pa, _pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2575|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (4, 4));
 2576|       |        // Center pixel of padded a should be original color
 2577|       |        assert_eq!(pa.get_pixel(1, 1).0, [100, 100, 100, 255]);
 2578|       |        // Corner should be white (padding)
 2579|       |        assert_eq!(pa.get_pixel(0, 0).0, [255, 255, 255, 255]);
 2580|       |    }

 2628|       |    }

 2636|       |    // -- Run full test orchestration tests ---------------------------
 2637|       |
 2638|       |    #[test]
 2639|       |    fn test_run_full_test_calls_runner() {
 2640|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2641|       |            quick: true,
 2642|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2643|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2644|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2645|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2646|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2647|       |        };
 2648|       |        let mut calls = Vec::new();
 2649|       |        let runner = |include_ignored: bool| {
 2650|       |            calls.push(include_ignored);
 2651|       |            make_suite(1, 0, 0, vec![make_result("t", true)])
 2652|       |        };
 2653|       |
 2654|       |        let outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2655|       |
 2656|       |        assert_eq!(calls, vec![false]); // quick mode = not include_ignored
 2657|       |        assert!(!outcome.any_failed);
 2658|       |    }
 2659|       |
 2660|       |    #[test]
 2661|       |    fn test_run_full_test_detects_test_failures() {
 2662|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2663|       |            quick: true,
 2664|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2665|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2666|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2667|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2668|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2669|       |        };
 2670|       |        let runner = |_include_ignored: bool| {
 2671|       |            make_suite(
 2672|       |                1,
 2673|       |                1,
 2674|       |                0,
 2675|       |                vec![make_result("t", true), make_result("f", false)],
 2676|       |            )
 2677|       |        };
 2678|       |
 2679|       |        let outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2680|       |        assert!(outcome.any_failed);
 2681|       |    }
 2682|       |
 2683|       |    #[test]
 2684|       |    fn test_run_full_test_full_mode_includes_ignored() {
 2685|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2686|       |            quick: false,
 2687|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2688|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2689|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2690|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2691|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2692|       |        };
 2693|       |        let mut calls = Vec::new();
 2694|       |        let runner = |include_ignored: bool| {
 2695|       |            calls.push(include_ignored);
 2696|       |            make_suite(1, 0, 0, vec![make_result("t", true)])
 2697|       |        };
 2698|       |
 2699|       |        let _outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2700|       |        assert_eq!(calls, vec![true]); // full mode = include_ignored
 2701|       |    }

 2706|       |    fn test_generate_html_report_basic_structure() {

 2708|       |            2,
 2709|       |            1,
 2710|       |            0,
 2711|       |            vec![
 2712|       |                make_result("app::tests::test_a", true),
 2713|       |                make_result("app::tests::test_b", true),
 2714|       |                make_result("app::tests::test_c", false),
 2715|       |            ],
 2716|       |        );
 2717|       |
 2718|       |        let html = generate_html_report(
 2719|       |            Some(&tests),
 2720|       |            &[],
 2721|       |            None,
 2722|       |            None,
 2723|       |            None,
 2724|       |            "abc123 (main)",
 2725|       |            Local::now(),
 2726|       |            "Full Suite",
 2727|       |            Duration::from_secs(100),
 2728|       |        );
 2729|       |

 2732|       |        assert!(html.contains("abc123 (main)"));

 2734|       |        assert!(html.contains("Test Breakdown"));
 2735|       |        assert!(html.contains("Failed Tests"));
 2736|       |        assert!(html.contains("test_c"));
 2737|       |        assert!(html.contains("</html>"));
 2738|       |    }

 2895|       |    // -- D2 visual HTML report test -----------------------------------
 2896|       |
 2897|       |    #[test]
 2898|       |    fn test_generate_html_report_with_d2_visual() {
 2899|       |        let d2_visual = D2VisualResults {
 2900|       |            cases: vec![
 2901|       |                D2VisualCaseResult {
 2902|       |                    case_name: "001-simple-connection".to_string(),
 2903|       |                    passed: true,
 2904|       |                    message: "ok".to_string(),
 2905|       |                    diff_percent: 5.2,
 2906|       |                    diff_pixels: 800,
 2907|       |                    max_delta: 45,
 2908|       |                    duration: Duration::from_millis(200),
 2909|       |                },
 2910|       |                D2VisualCaseResult {
 2911|       |                    case_name: "020-simple-container".to_string(),
 2912|       |                    passed: false,
 2913|       |                    message: "diff_exceeds_threshold".to_string(),

C:\language\mdmdview\src\d2_renderer.rs:
    1|       |//! D2 diagram renderer.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
    4|       |//! then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
    5|       |//! work (D2 compilation + SVG rasterization) runs on a dedicated worker
    6|       |//! thread; the main thread only handles the cheap GPU texture upload.
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Key components:
    9|       |//! - `D2Renderer` struct: holds font database, caches, and worker channels
   10|       |//! - `LruCache<K, V>`: generic LRU cache (shared module in lru_cache.rs)
   11|       |//! - `render_d2_to_svg()`: static method calling into the mdmdview-d2 crate
   12|       |//! - `rasterize_svg()`: pure function: SVG string -> RGBA bytes (runs on worker)
   13|       |//! - `poll_results()`: drains worker results, uploads textures to GPU (main thread)
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! This module is conditionally compiled behind `#[cfg(feature = "d2")]`.
   16|       |//! It integrates with `MarkdownRenderer` following the same public API pattern
   17|       |//! as `PikchrRenderer` (render_block, begin_frame, has_pending, release_gpu_textures).
   18|       |
   19|       |use std::cell::RefCell;
   20|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
   21|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   22|       |use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   23|       |
   24|       |use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, Sender, TrySendError};
   25|       |
   26|       |use crate::lru_cache::{hash_str, LruCache};
   27|       |use crate::ThemeColors;
   28|       |
   29|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   30|       |// Cache capacity constants
   31|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   32|       |
   33|       |const D2_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   34|       |const D2_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   35|       |const D2_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 32;
   36|       |/// Trailing-edge debounce cooldown for resize. During window resize, suppress
   37|       |/// rasterization enqueue and show the last rendered texture scaled to fit.
   38|       |/// Only enqueue a new rasterization job once the width stabilizes for this
   39|       |/// duration.
   40|       |const RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
   41|       |/// Maximum rasterized dimension (width or height) in pixels.
   42|       |/// Matches the cap used by pikchr_renderer and mermaid_renderer.
   43|       |const MAX_RASTER_SIDE: u32 = 4096;
   44|       |/// Rasterization supersample factor. D2 diagrams benefit from higher pixel
   45|       |/// density for crisp text and clean lines, same as pikchr_renderer.
   46|       |const D2_SUPERSAMPLE: f32 = 2.0;
   47|       |/// Bounded channel capacity for job and result channels. With debounce
   48|       |/// suppressing enqueue during resize, at most one job per diagram is
   49|       |/// enqueued after the debounce expires. A document with ~6-8 diagrams
   50|       |/// fits within 8 slots. The bounded channel provides natural backpressure
   51|       |/// if the worker falls behind.
   52|       |const CHANNEL_CAPACITY: usize = 8;
   53|       |
   54|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   59|       |type JobReceiver = Receiver<RasterJob>;
   60|       |type ResultSender = Sender<RasterResult>;
   61|       |type ResultReceiver = Receiver<RasterResult>;

  164|       |/// D2 diagram renderer.
  165|       |///
  166|       |/// Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
  167|       |/// then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
  168|       |/// runs on a dedicated worker thread; the main thread only uploads the
  169|       |/// resulting RGBA data to the GPU (~0.1-1ms).
  170|       |pub(crate) struct D2Renderer {
  171|       |    /// LRU cache: texture_key (String) -> (TextureHandle, [width, height])
  172|       |    textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, D2TextureEntry>>,
  173|       |    /// SVG cache: svg_cache_key (u128: code_hash << 1 | dark_mode) -> SVG string
  174|       |    svg_cache: RefCell<LruCache<u128, String>>,
  175|       |    /// Error cache: code_hash (u64) -> error message.
  176|       |    /// Errors don't depend on dark_mode, only on source syntax.
  177|       |    errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  178|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize suppression.
  179|       |    debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, DiagramDebounce>>,
  180|       |    /// Maps code_hash -> texture_key of the most recently rasterized texture.
  181|       |    /// Used to display a stale texture scaled to fit during debounce cooldown.
  182|       |    latest_texture_key: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  183|       |    /// Job sender. Wrapped in Option so Drop can close the channel
  184|       |    /// before joining the worker thread (same pattern as Pikchr/Mermaid).
  185|       |    job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  186|       |    /// Result receiver. Polled each frame in poll_results().
  187|       |    result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  188|       |    /// Worker thread handle. Joined on drop for clean shutdown.
  189|       |    worker_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
  190|       |    /// Set of code_hashes with in-flight jobs. Keyed by code_hash (not
  191|       |    /// texture_key) to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes
  192|       |    /// while a job is already pending for the same diagram.
  193|       |    pending: RefCell<HashSet<u64>>,
  194|       |    /// Tracks the currently desired width bucket per diagram (for stale discard).
  195|       |    wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,
  196|       |}

  198|       |impl D2Renderer {
  199|       |    /// Create a new D2Renderer, loading system fonts once at startup.
  200|       |    /// Without system fonts, usvg's default empty fontdb silently drops all
  201|       |    /// text labels in rasterized SVGs. Takes ~50-200ms (one-time cost).
  202|       |    /// Spawns a dedicated worker thread for background rasterization.
  203|       |    /// Same pattern as pikchr_renderer.rs and mermaid_renderer.rs.
  204|    480|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
  205|    480|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  206|    480|        db.load_system_fonts();
  207|    480|        let fontdb = Arc::new(db);
  208|       |
  209|    480|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  210|    480|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  211|       |
  212|       |        // Clone fontdb for the worker thread
  213|    480|        let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
  214|    480|        let worker_handle = std::thread::Builder::new()
  215|    480|            .name("d2-raster".into())
  216|    480|            .spawn(move || {
  217|    480|                Self::worker_loop(worker_fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
  218|    480|            })
  219|    480|            .expect("failed to spawn d2 raster worker");
  220|       |
  221|    480|        Self {
  222|    480|            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(D2_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  223|    480|            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(D2_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY)),

  239|       |    /// Main loop for the worker thread. Receives jobs, processes them, and
  240|       |    /// sends results back. Uses `try_send` to avoid blocking if the result
  241|       |    /// channel is full (prevents shutdown deadlock). Same pattern as Pikchr
  242|       |    /// and Mermaid renderers.
  243|    484|    fn worker_loop(
  244|    484|        fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  245|    484|        job_rx: JobReceiver,
  246|    484|        result_tx: ResultSender,
  247|    484|    ) {
  248|    490|        while let Ok(job) = job_rx.recv() {
                                   ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (248:19): [True: 6, False: 484]
  ------------------
  249|      6|            let result = Self::process_job(&fontdb, job);
  250|      6|            // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
  251|      6|            // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
  252|      6|            // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
  253|      6|            // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable --
  254|      6|            // the main thread still has the code_hash in the pending set, so
  255|      6|            // render_block will show the stale texture or placeholder and the
  256|      6|            // pending entry will be leaked (harmless, cleared on next
  257|      6|            // release_gpu_textures or app restart).
  258|      6|            let _ = result_tx.try_send(result);
  259|      6|        }
  260|       |        // job_rx disconnected (main thread dropped job_tx) -- exit
  261|    484|    }

  320|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  321|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  322|       |    ///
  323|       |    /// Parses via usvg (using the shared fontdb with system fonts), and
  324|       |    /// rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia. D2 SVGs include proper font references,
  325|       |    /// so no font-family injection is needed (unlike Pikchr).
  326|       |    /// Rasterizes at `D2_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  327|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  328|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  329|     10|    fn rasterize_svg(
  330|     10|        fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  331|     10|        svg: &str,
  332|     10|        width_bucket: u32,
  333|     10|        scale_bucket: u32,
  334|     10|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  335|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  336|     10|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  337|     10|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  338|     10|            ..Default::default()
  339|     10|        };
  340|     10|        let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
                          ^8
  341|     10|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^2                                ^2
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  344|      8|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();
  345|      8|        let (w, h) = (size.width().max(1), size.height().max(1));
  346|       |
  347|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  348|      8|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  349|      8|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (349:30): [True: 6, False: 2]
  ------------------
  350|      6|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  351|       |        } else {
  352|      2|            base_scale
  353|       |        };
  354|      8|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  355|       |
  356|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  357|      8|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  358|      8|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  359|       |
  360|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  361|      8|        let raster_scale = scale * D2_SUPERSAMPLE;
  362|      8|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  363|      8|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  364|       |
  365|       |        // Clamp to reasonable maximum
  366|      8|        if raster_w > MAX_RASTER_SIDE || raster_h > MAX_RASTER_SIDE {
                                                       ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (366:12): [True: 2, False: 6]
  |  Branch (366:42): [True: 0, False: 6]
  ------------------
  367|      2|            let clamp_scale = (MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_w as f32)
  368|      2|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  369|      2|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  370|      2|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  371|      6|        }
  372|       |
  373|       |        // Rasterize: D2 SVGs include background fills, so no separate fill needed.
  374|      8|        let mut pixmap = tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(raster_w, raster_h)
  375|      8|            .ok_or_else(|| "Pixmap allocation failed".to_string())?;
                                         ^0                         ^0          ^0
  376|      8|        let transform = tiny_skia::Transform::from_scale(raster_scale, raster_scale);
  377|      8|        let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  378|      8|        resvg::render(&tree, transform, &mut pmut);
  379|       |
  380|      8|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  381|      8|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  382|      8|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  383|      8|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  384|      8|        })
  385|     10|    }
  386|       |
  387|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  388|       |    // Main-thread polling
  389|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  390|       |
  391|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  392|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  393|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  394|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  395|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  396|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  397|     45|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {
  398|     45|        let mut any_processed = false;
  399|       |
  400|       |        loop {
  401|     50|            match self.result_rx.try_recv() {
  402|      5|                Ok(result) => {
  403|      5|                    any_processed = true;
  404|       |
  405|       |                    // Remove from pending set
  406|      5|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&result.code_hash);
  407|       |
  408|       |                    // Always cache SVG (content-addressed, width-independent)
  409|      5|                    if let Some(ref svg) = result.svg {
                                              ^4
  ------------------
  |  Branch (409:28): [True: 4, False: 1]
  ------------------
  410|      4|                        let sk = svg_cache_key(result.code_hash, result.dark_mode);
  411|      4|                        self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(sk, svg.clone());
  412|      4|                    }
                                  ^1
  413|       |
  414|       |                    // Always cache errors (prevents infinite retry loops)
  415|      5|                    if let Some(ref err) = result.error {
                                              ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (415:28): [True: 1, False: 4]
  ------------------
  416|      1|                        self.errors
  417|      1|                            .borrow_mut()
  418|      1|                            .insert(result.code_hash, err.clone());
  419|      1|                        continue; // No RGBA to upload
  420|      4|                    }
  421|       |
  422|       |                    // Stale result check: skip GPU upload if width bucket changed since enqueue
  423|      4|                    if self.is_stale_result(&result.texture_key, result.code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (423:24): [True: 2, False: 2]
  ------------------
  424|      2|                        continue;
  425|      2|                    }
  426|       |
  427|       |                    // Upload RGBA to GPU texture
  428|      2|                    if let (Some(rgba), Some(raster_size), Some(display_size)) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (428:28): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  429|      2|                        (result.rgba, result.raster_size, result.display_size)
  430|       |                    {
  431|      2|                        let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(raster_size, &rgba);
  432|      2|                        let texture = ctx.load_texture(
  433|      2|                            result.texture_key.clone(),
  434|      2|                            image,
  435|       |                            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  436|       |                        );
  437|      2|                        let entry = D2TextureEntry {
  438|      2|                            texture,
  439|      2|                            display_size,
  440|      2|                        };
  441|      2|                        self.textures
  442|      2|                            .borrow_mut()
  443|      2|                            .insert(result.texture_key.clone(), entry);
  444|      2|                        self.latest_texture_key
  445|      2|                            .borrow_mut()
  446|      2|                            .insert(result.code_hash, result.texture_key.clone());
  447|       |                        // Update debounce state
  448|      1|                        if let Some(width_bucket) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (448:32): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  449|      2|                            Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(&result.texture_key)
  450|      1|                        {
  451|      1|                            self.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
  452|      1|                                result.code_hash,
  453|      1|                                DiagramDebounce {
  454|      1|                                    last_rasterized_bucket: width_bucket,
  455|      1|                                    last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
  456|      1|                                    bucket_changed_at: None,
  457|      1|                                },
  458|      1|                            );
  459|      1|                        }
  460|      0|                    }
  461|       |                }
  462|     43|                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
  463|       |                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
  464|       |                    // Worker thread has died (panic or unexpected exit).
  465|       |                    // Clear all pending entries so render_block can detect the dead
  466|       |                    // worker via try_send(Disconnected) and show an error instead of
  467|       |                    // showing "Rendering..." forever.
  468|      2|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  469|      2|                    break;
  470|       |                }
  471|       |            }
  472|       |        }
  473|       |
  474|     45|        if any_processed {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (474:12): [True: 5, False: 40]
  ------------------
  475|      5|            ctx.request_repaint();
  476|     40|        }
  477|     45|        any_processed
  478|     45|    }
  479|       |
  480|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  481|       |    // Public API
  482|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  483|       |
  484|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  485|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  486|     45|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  487|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  488|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  489|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  490|     45|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  491|       |
  492|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  493|     45|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:12): [True: 1, False: 44]
  ------------------
  494|      1|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  495|     44|        }
  496|     45|    }
  497|       |
  498|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  499|     62|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  500|     62|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  501|     62|    }
  502|       |
  503|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  504|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  505|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  506|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  507|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  508|      3|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  509|      3|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  510|      3|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  511|      3|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  512|      3|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  513|      3|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  514|      3|    }
  515|       |
  516|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a D2 code block.
  517|       |    ///
  518|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  519|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  520|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  521|       |    ///
  522|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  523|     14|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  524|     14|        &self,
  525|     14|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  526|     14|        code: &str,
  527|     14|        ui_scale: f32,
  528|     14|        code_font_size: f32,
  529|     14|    ) -> bool {
  530|     14|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  531|     14|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  532|     14|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  533|       |
  534|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  535|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  536|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  537|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  538|     14|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  539|     14|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (539:16): [True: 1, False: 13]
  ------------------
  540|      1|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  541|      1|            return true;
  542|     13|        }
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  545|     13|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  546|     13|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  547|     13|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  548|     13|        let texture_key = format!(
  549|       |            "d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  550|     13|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  551|       |        );
  552|       |
  553|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  554|     13|        self.wanted_bucket
  555|     13|            .borrow_mut()
  556|     13|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  557|       |
  558|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  559|       |        {
  560|     13|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  561|     13|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (561:20): [True: 1, False: 12]
  ------------------
  562|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  563|      1|                return true;
  564|     12|            }
  565|       |        }
  566|       |
  567|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  568|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  569|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  570|     12|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
                                                                                    ^6
  571|      6|            (
  572|      6|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  573|      6|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  574|      6|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  575|      6|            )
  576|      6|        });
  577|     12|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
                                   ^6               ^6         ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (577:16): [True: 6, False: 6]
  ------------------
  578|      6|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (578:16): [True: 5, False: 1]
  ------------------
  579|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  580|      5|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (580:20): [True: 3, False: 2]
  ------------------
  581|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  582|      3|                    let changed_at = Instant::now();
  583|      3|                    self.debounce.borrow_mut().entry(code_hash).and_modify(|d| {
  584|      3|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  585|      3|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(changed_at);
  586|      3|                    });
  587|      2|                }
  588|      5|                let changed_at = self
  589|      5|                    .debounce
  590|      5|                    .borrow()
  591|      5|                    .get(&code_hash)
  592|      5|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  593|      5|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  594|      5|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  595|      5|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  596|      5|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (596:20): [True: 4, False: 1]
  ------------------
  597|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  598|      2|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (598:28): [True: 2, False: 2]
  ------------------
  599|      4|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  600|       |                    {
  601|      2|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (601:32): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  602|      1|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  603|      1|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  604|      1|                            return true;
  605|      1|                        }
  606|      2|                    }
  607|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  608|      1|                }
  609|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  610|      1|            }
  611|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  612|      6|        }
  613|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  614|       |
  615|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  616|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  617|     11|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (617:12): [True: 1, False: 10]
  ------------------
  618|      1|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  619|     10|        }
  620|       |
  621|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  622|     10|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  623|     10|        let job = RasterJob {
  624|     10|            code_hash,
  625|     10|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  626|     10|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (626:22): [True: 9, False: 1]
  ------------------
  627|      9|                Some(code.to_string())
  628|       |            } else {
  629|      1|                None
  630|       |            },
  631|     10|            svg: cached_svg,
  632|     10|            dark_mode,
  633|     10|            width_bucket,
  634|     10|            scale_bucket,
  635|       |        };
  636|       |
  637|     10|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  638|      8|            Ok(()) => {
  639|      8|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  640|      8|            }
  641|      1|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  642|      1|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  643|      1|            }
  644|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  645|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  646|      1|                self.errors
  647|      1|                    .borrow_mut()
  648|      1|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  649|      1|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  650|      1|                return true;
  651|       |            }
  652|       |        }
  653|       |
  654|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  655|      9|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  656|     14|    }
  657|       |
  658|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  659|       |    // Helpers
  660|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  661|       |
  662|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  663|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  664|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  665|     12|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  666|     12|        &self,
  667|     12|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  668|     12|        code_hash: u64,
  669|     12|        available_width: f32,
  670|     12|    ) -> bool {
  671|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  672|     12|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
                                  ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (672:16): [True: 3, False: 9]
  ------------------
  673|      3|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (673:20): [True: 1, False: 2]
  ------------------
  674|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  675|      1|                return true;
  676|      2|            }
  677|      9|        }
  678|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  679|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  680|     11|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  681|     11|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  682|     11|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  683|     11|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  684|     11|        );
  685|     11|        ui.painter().text(
  686|     11|            rect.center(),
  687|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  688|       |            "Rendering...",
  689|     11|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  690|     11|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  691|       |        );
  692|     11|        true
  693|     12|    }
  694|       |
  695|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  696|       |    /// Key format: `d2:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  697|     14|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  698|     14|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^10                     ^4
  699|     10|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  700|     10|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  701|     14|    }
  702|       |
  703|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  704|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  705|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  706|      8|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  707|      8|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  708|      8|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  709|      8|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  710|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  711|      2|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  712|       |            //                                   release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  713|      2|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  714|       |        }
  715|      8|    }
  716|       |
  717|       |    /// Render D2 source code to an SVG string.
  718|       |    ///
  719|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  720|       |    /// Calls into the mdmdview-d2 crate pipeline.
  721|     11|    fn render_d2_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  722|     11|        let options = mdmdview_d2::RenderOptions {
  723|     11|            dark_mode,
  724|     11|            ..Default::default()
  725|     11|        };
  726|       |
  727|     11|        mdmdview_d2::render_d2_to_svg(code, &options)
  728|     11|            .map(|result| result.svg)
  729|     11|            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
                                       ^2^2
  730|     11|    }
  731|       |
  732|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  733|      3|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &D2TextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  734|      3|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  735|      3|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (735:24): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------
  736|      2|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  737|       |        } else {
  738|      1|            1.0
  739|       |        };
  740|      3|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  741|      3|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  742|      3|    }
  743|       |
  744|       |    /// Display a D2 error block with the error message and original source code.
  745|      2|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  746|      2|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  747|      2|        egui::Frame::none()
  748|      2|            .fill(tc.box_bg)
  749|      2|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  750|      2|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  751|      2|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  752|      2|                ui.label(
  753|      2|                    egui::RichText::new("D2 rendering error:")
  754|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  755|      2|                        .size(code_font_size),
  756|       |                );
  757|      2|                ui.label(
  758|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(error)
  759|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::LIGHT_GRAY)
  760|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  761|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  762|       |                );
  763|      2|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  764|      2|                ui.label(
  765|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  766|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  767|      2|                        .color(tc.code_fallback_text)
  768|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  769|       |                );
  770|      2|            });
  771|      2|    }
  772|       |}
  773|       |
  774|       |impl Drop for D2Renderer {
  775|    499|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  776|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  777|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  778|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  779|    499|        self.job_tx.take();
  780|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  781|    499|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
                                  ^480
  ------------------
  |  Branch (781:16): [True: 480, False: 19]
  ------------------
  782|    480|            let _ = handle.join();
  783|    480|        }
                      ^19
  784|    499|    }
  785|       |}
  786|       |
  787|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  788|       |// Unit tests
  789|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  790|       |
  791|       |#[cfg(test)]
  792|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  793|       |mod tests {
  794|       |    use super::*;
  795|       |
  796|       |    fn test_raw_input(width: f32, height: f32) -> egui::RawInput {
  797|       |        egui::RawInput {
  798|       |            screen_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
  799|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
  800|       |                egui::vec2(width, height),
  801|       |            )),
  802|       |            ..Default::default()
  803|       |        }
  804|       |    }
  805|       |
  806|       |    fn test_renderer_with_channels(
  807|       |        job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  808|       |        result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  809|       |    ) -> D2Renderer {
  810|       |        D2Renderer {
  811|       |            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  812|       |            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  813|       |            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  814|       |            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  815|       |            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  816|       |            job_tx,
  817|       |            result_rx,
  818|       |            worker_handle: None,
  819|       |            pending: RefCell::new(HashSet::new()),
  820|       |            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  821|       |        }
  822|       |    }
  823|       |
  824|       |    fn insert_test_texture(
  825|       |        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  826|       |        renderer: &D2Renderer,
  827|       |        key: &str,
  828|       |        display_size: [u32; 2],
  829|       |    ) {
  830|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], egui::Color32::WHITE);
  831|       |        let texture =
  832|       |            ui.ctx()
  833|       |                .load_texture(key.to_string(), image, egui::TextureOptions::default());
  834|       |        renderer.textures.borrow_mut().insert(
  835|       |            key.to_string(),
  836|       |            D2TextureEntry {
  837|       |                texture,
  838|       |                display_size,
  839|       |            },
  840|       |        );
  841|       |    }
  842|       |
  843|       |    #[test]
  844|       |    fn test_render_simple_diagram() {
  845|       |        let svg = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", false);
  846|       |        assert!(
  847|       |            svg.is_ok(),
  848|       |            "Simple D2 diagram should render: {:?}",
  849|       |            svg.err()
  850|       |        );
  851|       |        let svg = svg.unwrap();
  852|       |        assert!(svg.contains("<svg"));
  853|       |    }
  854|       |
  855|       |    #[test]
  856|       |    fn test_render_dark_mode_produces_different_svg() {
  857|       |        let light = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", false).unwrap();
  858|       |        let dark = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", true).unwrap();
  859|       |        assert_ne!(light, dark);
  860|       |    }
  861|       |
  862|       |    #[test]
  863|       |    fn test_render_invalid_syntax() {
  864|       |        // D2 should error on truly invalid syntax
  865|       |        let result = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("}{}{", false);
  866|       |        assert!(result.is_err());
  867|       |    }
  868|       |
  869|       |    #[test]
  870|       |    fn test_render_empty_input() {
  871|       |        let result = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("", false);
  872|       |        // Empty input may produce an error or empty SVG depending on the crate
  873|       |        // Just verify it doesn't panic
  874|       |        let _ = result;
  875|       |    }
  876|       |
  877|       |    #[test]
  878|       |    fn test_svg_cache_key_differs_by_dark_mode() {
  879|       |        let key_light = svg_cache_key(12345, false);
  880|       |        let key_dark = svg_cache_key(12345, true);
  881|       |        assert_ne!(key_light, key_dark);
  882|       |    }
  883|       |
  884|       |    #[test]
  885|       |    fn test_svg_cache_key_differs_by_code_hash() {
  886|       |        let key1 = svg_cache_key(12345, false);
  887|       |        let key2 = svg_cache_key(67890, false);
  888|       |        assert_ne!(key1, key2);
  889|       |    }
  890|       |
  891|       |    #[test]
  892|       |    fn test_texture_key_includes_all_components() {
  893|       |        let key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}", 12345u64, 640, 100, 0);
  894|       |        assert!(key.contains("d2:"));
  895|       |        assert!(key.contains(":w640:"));
  896|       |        assert!(key.contains(":s100:"));
  897|       |        assert!(key.contains(":dm0"));
  898|       |    }
  899|       |
  900|       |    #[test]
  901|       |    fn test_width_bucketing() {
  902|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(641.0));
  903|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(655.0));
  904|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(656.0));
  905|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), 640);
  906|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(672.0), 672);
  907|       |    }
  908|       |
  909|       |    #[test]
  910|       |    fn test_scale_bucketing() {
  911|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.0), 100);
  912|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.25), 125);
  913|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.5), 150);
  914|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(2.0), 200);
  915|       |    }
  916|       |
  917|       |    #[test]
  918|       |    fn test_renderer_new_creates_empty_caches() {
  919|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
  920|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
  921|       |    }
  922|       |
  923|       |    #[test]
  924|       |    fn test_supersample_constant_is_reasonable() {
  925|       |        const { assert!(D2_SUPERSAMPLE >= 1.0, "Supersample must be >= 1.0") };
  926|       |        const {
  927|       |            assert!(
  928|       |                D2_SUPERSAMPLE <= 4.0,
  929|       |                "Supersample > 4.0 would waste memory"
  930|       |            )
  931|       |        };
  932|       |    }

  935|       |    // Stale result and texture key parsing tests

  937|       |
  938|       |    #[test]
  939|       |    fn test_parse_width_from_texture_key() {
  940|       |        assert_eq!(
  941|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:0000000000003039:w640:s100:dm0"),
  942|       |            Some(640)
  943|       |        );
  944|       |        assert_eq!(
  945|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:w1024:s150:dm1"),
  946|       |            Some(1024)
  947|       |        );
  948|       |        assert_eq!(
  949|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:w0:s100:dm0"),
  950|       |            Some(0)
  951|       |        );
  952|       |        // No :w prefix
  953|       |        assert_eq!(
  954|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:s100:dm0"),
  955|       |            None
  956|       |        );
  957|       |    }

  963|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
  964|       |    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
  965|       |        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  966|       |        db.load_system_fonts();
  967|       |        Arc::new(db)
  968|       |    }
  969|       |
  970|       |    #[test]
  971|       |    fn test_render_block_basic_and_pending_placeholder() {
  972|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
  973|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  974|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
  975|       |        let code = "a -> b";
  976|       |        let mut rendered_first = false;
  977|       |        let mut rendered_second = false;
  978|       |
  979|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
  980|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
  981|       |                rendered_first = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
  982|       |                rendered_second = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
  983|       |            });
  984|       |        });
  985|       |
  986|       |        assert!(rendered_first);
  987|       |        assert!(rendered_second);
  988|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&hash_str(code)));
  989|       |    }
  990|       |
  991|       |    #[test]
  992|       |    fn test_render_block_reports_cached_error() {
  993|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
  994|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
  995|       |        let code = "a -> b";
  996|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  997|       |        renderer
  998|       |            .errors
  999|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1000|       |            .insert(code_hash, "boom".to_string());
 1001|       |
 1002|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1003|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1004|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1005|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1006|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1007|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1008|       |            });
 1009|       |        });
 1010|       |
 1011|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1012|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1013|       |    }
 1014|       |
 1015|       |    #[test]
 1016|       |    fn test_render_block_enqueues_cached_svg() {
 1017|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1018|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1019|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1020|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1021|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1022|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1023|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, false),
 1024|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1025|       |        );
 1026|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1027|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, true),
 1028|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1029|       |        );
 1030|       |
 1031|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1032|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1033|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1034|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1035|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1036|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1037|       |            });
 1038|       |        });
 1039|       |
 1040|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1041|       |        let request = job_rx
 1042|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1))
 1043|       |            .expect("request");
 1044|       |        assert!(request.svg.is_some());
 1045|       |        assert!(request.code.is_none());
 1046|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1047|       |    }
 1048|       |
 1049|       |    #[test]
 1050|       |    fn test_render_block_queue_full_keeps_rendering() {
 1051|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1052|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1053|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx.clone()), result_rx);
 1054|       |        job_tx
 1055|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1056|       |                code_hash: 1,
 1057|       |                texture_key: "d2:full:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1058|       |                code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1059|       |                svg: None,
 1060|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1061|       |                width_bucket: 224,
 1062|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1063|       |            })
 1064|       |            .expect("fill queue");
 1065|       |
 1066|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1067|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1068|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1069|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1070|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1071|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, "a -> b", 1.0, 14.0);
 1072|       |            });
 1073|       |        });
 1074|       |
 1075|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1076|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1077|       |        drop(job_rx);
 1078|       |    }

 1080|       |    #[test]
 1081|       |    fn test_render_block_disconnected_queue_sets_error() {

 1083|       |        drop(job_rx);
 1084|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1085|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1086|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1087|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1088|       |
 1089|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1090|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1091|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1092|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1093|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1094|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1095|       |            });
 1096|       |        });

 1101|       |
 1102|       |    #[test]
 1103|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_and_uses_cached_texture() {
 1104|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(2);
 1105|       |        let (job_tx, _job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1106|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1107|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1108|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1109|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1110|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(999);
 1111|       |        let result_tx = RefCell::new(Some(result_tx));
 1112|       |        let texture_key = RefCell::new(String::new());
 1113|       |
 1114|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1115|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1116|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1117|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1118|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1119|       |                let width_bucket = bucket_width(ui.available_width().max(1.0));
 1120|       |                let key = format!(
 1121|       |                    "d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
 1122|       |                    code_hash,
 1123|       |                    width_bucket,
 1124|       |                    100,
 1125|       |                    ui.visuals().dark_mode as u8
 1126|       |                );
 1127|       |                *texture_key.borrow_mut() = key.clone();
 1128|       |                renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
 1129|       |                if let Some(tx) = result_tx.borrow_mut().take() {
 1130|       |                    tx.send(RasterResult {
 1131|       |                        code_hash,
 1132|       |                        texture_key: key,
 1133|       |                        dark_mode: ui.visuals().dark_mode,
 1134|       |                        svg: Some(
 1135|       |                            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1136|       |                                .to_string(),
 1137|       |                        ),
 1138|       |                        rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1139|       |                        raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1140|       |                        display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1141|       |                        error: None,
 1142|       |                    })
 1143|       |                    .expect("send result");
 1144|       |                }
 1145|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1146|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1147|       |            });
 1148|       |        });
 1149|       |
 1150|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1151|       |        let texture_key = texture_key.into_inner();
 1152|       |        assert!(renderer.textures.borrow_mut().get(&texture_key).is_some());
 1153|       |        assert!(renderer
 1154|       |            .svg_cache
 1155|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1156|       |            .get(&svg_cache_key(code_hash, ctx.style().visuals.dark_mode))
 1157|       |            .is_some());
 1158|       |        assert_eq!(
 1159|       |            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash),
 1160|       |            Some(&texture_key)
 1161|       |        );
 1162|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1163|       |    }
 1164|       |
 1165|       |    #[test]
 1166|       |    fn test_begin_frame_result_missing_raster_fields_skips_texture_upload() {
 1167|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1168|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1169|       |        let code_hash = hash_str("a -> b");
 1170|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1171|       |        result_tx
 1172|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1173|       |                code_hash,
 1174|       |                texture_key: "d2:missing-fields:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1175|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1176|       |                svg: Some(
 1177|       |                    "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"></svg>"
 1178|       |                        .to_string(),
 1179|       |                ),
 1180|       |                rgba: None,
 1181|       |                raster_size: None,
 1182|       |                display_size: None,
 1183|       |                error: None,
 1184|       |            })
 1185|       |            .expect("send result");
 1186|       |
 1187|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1188|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1189|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1190|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |_ui| {
 1191|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1192|       |            });
 1193|       |        });
 1194|       |
 1195|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.textures.borrow().len(), 0);
 1196|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1197|       |    }
