 1248|       |    #[test]
 1249|       |    fn test_begin_frame_discards_stale_result() {
 1250|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1251|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1252|       |        let code_hash = 11;
 1253|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1254|       |        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, 128);
 1255|       |        result_tx
 1256|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1257|       |                code_hash,
 1258|       |                texture_key: "d2:stale:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1259|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1260|       |                svg: Some(
 1261|       |                    "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1262|       |                        .to_string(),
 1263|       |                ),
 1264|       |                rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1265|       |                raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1266|       |                display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1267|       |                error: None,
 1268|       |            })
 1269|       |            .expect("send result");
 1270|       |
 1271|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1272|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1273|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1274|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |_ui| {
 1275|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1276|       |            });
 1277|       |        });
 1278|       |
 1279|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.textures.borrow().len(), 0);
 1280|       |        assert!(renderer
 1281|       |            .svg_cache
 1282|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1283|       |            .get(&svg_cache_key(code_hash, false))
 1284|       |            .is_some());
 1285|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1286|       |    }

 1289|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_with_unparseable_texture_key() {
 1290|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1291|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1292|       |        let code_hash = 19;

 1296|       |            .send(RasterResult {

 1300|       |                svg: Some(
 1301|       |                    "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1302|       |                        .to_string(),
 1303|       |                ),
 1304|       |                rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1305|       |                raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1306|       |                display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1307|       |                error: None,
 1308|       |            })
 1309|       |            .expect("send result");

 1316|       |            });
 1317|       |        });

 1347|       |    fn test_render_block_debounce_without_stale_texture_enqueues_job() {
 1348|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1349|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1350|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1351|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1352|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1353|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1354|       |            code_hash,
 1355|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1356|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1357|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1358|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1359|       |            },
 1360|       |        );
 1361|       |
 1362|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1363|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1364|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1365|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1366|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1367|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1368|       |            });
 1369|       |        });
 1370|       |
 1371|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1372|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 1373|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1374|       |        assert_eq!(
 1375|       |            renderer
 1376|       |                .debounce
 1377|       |                .borrow()
 1378|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1379|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1380|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1381|       |        );
 1382|       |    }

 1384|       |    #[test]
 1385|       |    fn test_render_block_uses_stale_texture_during_debounce() {
 1386|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1387|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1388|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1389|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1390|       |        let stale_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w224:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1391|       |        renderer
 1392|       |            .latest_texture_key
 1393|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1394|       |            .insert(code_hash, stale_key.clone());
 1395|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1396|       |            code_hash,
 1397|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1398|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1399|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1400|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1401|       |            },
 1402|       |        );
 1403|       |
 1404|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1405|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1406|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1407|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1408|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1409|       |                insert_test_texture(ui, &renderer, &stale_key, [320, 160]);
 1410|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1411|       |            });
 1412|       |        });
 1413|       |
 1414|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1415|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1416|       |        assert_eq!(
 1417|       |            renderer
 1418|       |                .debounce
 1419|       |                .borrow()
 1420|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1421|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1422|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1423|       |        );
 1424|       |    }

 1604|       |    #[test]
 1605|       |    fn test_rasterize_svg_uses_base_scale_when_width_bucket_is_zero_and_clamps() {
 1606|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1607|       |        let svg = "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 5000 5000\"><rect width=\"5000\" height=\"5000\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>";
 1608|       |
 1609|       |        let output = D2Renderer::rasterize_svg(&fontdb, svg, 0, 100).expect("rasterize");
 1610|       |
 1611|       |        assert_eq!(output.display_size, [5000, 5000]);
 1612|       |        assert!(output.raster_size[0] <= MAX_RASTER_SIDE as usize);
 1613|       |        assert!(output.raster_size[1] <= MAX_RASTER_SIDE as usize);
 1614|       |    }

 1639|       |    fn test_process_job_returns_raster_error_for_invalid_cached_svg() {
 1640|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1641|       |        let code_hash = hash_str("cached-error");
 1642|       |        let result = D2Renderer::process_job(
 1643|       |            &fontdb,
 1644|       |            RasterJob {
 1645|       |                code_hash,
 1646|       |                texture_key: format!("d2:{:016x}:w224:s100:dm0", code_hash),
 1647|       |                code: None,
 1648|       |                svg: Some("<svg".to_string()),
 1649|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1650|       |                width_bucket: 224,
 1651|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1652|       |            },

 1654|       |

 1659|       |
 1660|       |    #[test]
 1661|       |    fn test_worker_processes_job() {
 1662|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1663|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1664|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1665|       |
 1666|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1667|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1668|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1669|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1670|       |            code_hash,
 1671|       |            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
 1672|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1673|       |            svg: None,
 1674|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1675|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1676|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1677|       |        };
 1678|       |
 1679|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1680|       |        // Drop sender so worker_loop exits after processing the one job.
 1681|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1682|       |
 1683|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1684|       |
 1685|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1686|       |        assert!(result.rgba.is_some(), "Worker should produce RGBA data");
 1687|       |        assert!(
 1688|       |            result.raster_size.is_some(),
 1689|       |            "Worker should produce raster_size"
 1690|       |        );
 1691|       |        assert!(
 1692|       |            result.display_size.is_some(),
 1693|       |            "Worker should produce display_size"
 1694|       |        );
 1695|       |        assert!(result.error.is_none(), "Worker should not produce an error");
 1696|       |        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "Worker should produce SVG on success");
 1697|       |    }
 1698|       |
 1699|       |    #[test]
 1700|       |    fn test_worker_caches_svg_on_success() {
 1701|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1702|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1703|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1704|       |
 1705|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1706|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1707|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1708|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1709|       |            code_hash,
 1710|       |            texture_key,
 1711|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1712|       |            svg: None,
 1713|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1714|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1715|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1716|       |        };
 1717|       |
 1718|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1719|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1720|       |
 1721|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1722|       |
 1723|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1724|       |        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "SVG should be cached on success");
 1725|       |        let svg = result.svg.unwrap();
 1726|       |        assert!(
 1727|       |            svg.contains("<svg"),
 1728|       |            "Cached SVG should contain <svg tag, got: {}",
 1729|       |            &svg[..svg.len().min(200)]
 1730|       |        );
 1731|       |    }
 1732|       |
 1733|       |    #[test]
 1734|       |    fn test_worker_caches_error() {
 1735|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1736|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1737|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1738|       |
 1739|       |        let code = "}{}{";
 1740|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1741|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1742|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1743|       |            code_hash,
 1744|       |            texture_key,
 1745|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1746|       |            svg: None,
 1747|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1748|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1749|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1750|       |        };
 1751|       |
 1752|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1753|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1754|       |
 1755|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1756|       |
 1757|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1758|       |        assert!(
 1759|       |            result.error.is_some(),
 1760|       |            "Worker should produce an error for invalid code"
 1761|       |        );
 1762|       |        assert!(
 1763|       |            result.rgba.is_none(),
 1764|       |            "Worker should not produce RGBA for invalid code"
 1765|       |        );
 1766|       |        assert!(
 1767|       |            result.svg.is_none(),
 1768|       |            "Worker should not produce SVG for invalid code"
 1769|       |        );
 1770|       |    }

 1789|       |        assert!(
 1790|       |            !renderer.is_stale_result(&fresh_key, code_hash),
 1791|       |            "Width 640 vs wanted 640 should be fresh"

 1880|       |    #[test]
 1881|       |    fn test_queue_full_does_not_block() {
 1882|       |        let (tx, _rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(2);
 1883|       |
 1884|       |        // Fill the channel with 2 dummy jobs
 1885|       |        for i in 0..2 {
 1886|       |            tx.send(RasterJob {
 1887|       |                code_hash: i,
 1888|       |                texture_key: format!("d2:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:{}", i),
 1889|       |                code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1890|       |                svg: None,
 1891|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1892|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 1893|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1894|       |            })
 1895|       |            .unwrap();
 1896|       |        }
 1897|       |
 1898|       |        // Third send should fail with Full, not block
 1899|       |        let result = tx.try_send(RasterJob {
 1900|       |            code_hash: 99,
 1901|       |            texture_key: "d2:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:99".to_string(),
 1902|       |            code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1903|       |            svg: None,
 1904|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1905|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1906|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1907|       |        });
 1908|       |        assert!(
 1909|       |            matches!(result, Err(TrySendError::Full(_))),
 1910|       |            "try_send on full channel should return Err(Full), got {:?}",
 1911|       |            result.err()
 1912|       |        );
 1913|       |    }

 1928|       |        );

 1932|       |    fn test_pending_dedup_prevents_double_enqueue() {
 1933|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1934|       |        let code_hash = 42u64;
 1935|       |
 1936|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1937|       |
 1938|       |        // The dedup check in render_block uses contains()
 1939|       |        assert!(
 1940|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash),
 1941|       |            "Pending set should contain the inserted code_hash"
 1942|       |        );
 1943|       |
 1944|       |        // Inserting again is a no-op on HashSet
 1945|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1946|       |        assert_eq!(
 1947|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().len(),
 1948|       |            1,
 1949|       |            "HashSet dedup: inserting same hash twice should keep len at 1"
 1950|       |        );
 1951|       |    }

 1953|       |    #[test]
 1954|       |    fn test_worker_try_send_full_does_not_block() {
 1955|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1956|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1957|       |        // Result channel with capacity 1
 1958|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(1);
 1959|       |
 1960|       |        // Fill the result channel with a dummy result
 1961|       |        result_tx
 1962|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1963|       |                code_hash: 0,
 1964|       |                texture_key: "dummy".to_string(),
 1965|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1966|       |                svg: None,
 1967|       |                rgba: None,
 1968|       |                raster_size: None,
 1969|       |                display_size: None,
 1970|       |                error: None,
 1971|       |            })
 1972|       |            .unwrap();
 1973|       |
 1974|       |        // Submit a job that will produce a result the worker can't send (channel full)
 1975|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1976|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1977|       |        job_tx
 1978|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1979|       |                code_hash,
 1980|       |                texture_key: format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash),
 1981|       |                code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1982|       |                svg: None,
 1983|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1984|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 1985|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1986|       |            })
 1987|       |            .unwrap();
 1988|       |        // Drop job_tx so worker_loop exits after processing the one job
 1989|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1990|       |
 1991|       |        // Run worker_loop on a spawned thread with a timeout to detect hangs
 1992|       |        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {
 1993|       |            D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1994|       |        });
 1995|       |
 1996|       |        // Wait up to 10 seconds for the worker to finish (D2 can be slower than pikchr)
 1997|       |        let start = Instant::now();
 1998|       |        loop {
 1999|       |            if handle.is_finished() {
 2000|       |                break;
 2001|       |            }
 2002|       |            if start.elapsed() > Duration::from_secs(10) {
 2003|       |                panic!("worker_loop blocked for >10s — try_send likely blocked instead of dropping the result");
 2004|       |            }
 2005|       |            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
 2006|       |        }
 2007|       |        handle.join().unwrap();
 2008|       |
 2009|       |        // The result channel should still contain the original dummy result
 2010|       |        let dummy = result_rx.try_recv().unwrap();
 2011|       |        assert_eq!(
 2012|       |            dummy.code_hash, 0,
 2013|       |            "Original dummy result should still be in the channel"
 2014|       |        );
 2015|       |    }
 2016|       |
 2017|       |    #[test]

 2051|       |    #[test]
 2052|       |    fn test_svg_cache_key_consistency() {
 2053|       |        let code_hash = 12345u64;
 2054|       |
 2055|       |        // Same inputs should produce the same key across multiple calls
 2056|       |        let key_a = svg_cache_key(code_hash, false);
 2057|       |        let key_b = svg_cache_key(code_hash, false);
 2058|       |        assert_eq!(key_a, key_b, "svg_cache_key should be deterministic");
 2059|       |
 2060|       |        let key_dark_a = svg_cache_key(code_hash, true);
 2061|       |        let key_dark_b = svg_cache_key(code_hash, true);
 2062|       |        assert_eq!(
 2063|       |            key_dark_a, key_dark_b,
 2064|       |            "svg_cache_key (dark) should be deterministic"
 2065|       |        );
 2066|       |
 2067|       |        // Different dark_mode should produce different keys
 2068|       |        assert_ne!(
 2069|       |            key_a, key_dark_a,
 2070|       |            "Different dark_mode should produce different keys"
 2071|       |        );
 2072|       |    }

C:\language\mdmdview\src\emoji_assets.rs:
    1|       |// emoji_assets.rs -- Procedural vector fallback icons for a small subset of emoji.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// When a Twemoji PNG is unavailable (see emoji_catalog.rs), this module can
    4|       |// generate simple rasterised shapes for a handful of commonly used emoji.
    5|       |// The fallback set is intentionally small; most emoji are served as embedded
    6|       |// PNGs from the catalog.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// Public API:
    9|       |//   make_image(emoji, size) -> Option<ColorImage>  -- render a fallback icon
   10|       |//   has_fallback(emoji)     -> bool                 -- cheap membership check
   11|       |
   12|       |use egui::Color32 as C;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Emoji codepoints that have procedural vector fallbacks.
   15|       |/// Both `make_image` and `has_fallback` derive from this list, keeping them
   16|       |/// automatically in sync.
   17|       |const FALLBACK_EMOJI: &[&str] = &[
   18|       |    "\u{2705}",  // white check mark
   19|       |    "\u{1f389}", // tada / party popper
   20|       |    "\u{1f680}", // rocket
   21|       |    "\u{2764}",  // red heart
   22|       |    "\u{1f496}", // sparkling heart (shares heart renderer)
   23|       |    "\u{2b50}",  // star
   24|       |    "\u{1f525}", // fire
   25|       |];
   26|       |
   27|       |/// Draw a simple vector fallback icon for a supported emoji.
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// Returns `None` for emoji not in `FALLBACK_EMOJI`.
   30|     14|pub fn make_image(emoji: &str, size: usize) -> Option<egui::ColorImage> {
   31|     14|    let mut img = egui::ColorImage::new([size, size], C::TRANSPARENT);
   32|     14|    match emoji {
   33|     14|        "\u{2705}" => {
   34|      1|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(34, 139, 34));
   35|      1|            draw_check(&mut img, size, C::WHITE);
   36|      1|        }
   37|     13|        "\u{1f389}" => {
   38|      1|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   39|      1|            confetti(&mut img, size);
   40|      1|        }
   41|     12|        "\u{1f680}" => {
   42|      2|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(60, 60, 170));
   43|      2|            rocket(
   44|      2|                &mut img,
   45|      2|                size,
   46|      2|                C::from_rgb(230, 230, 230),
   47|      2|                C::from_rgb(255, 110, 60),
   48|      2|            );
   49|      2|        }
   50|     10|        "\u{2764}" | "\u{1f496}" => {
                                   ^9
   51|      2|            heart(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(220, 20, 60));
   52|      2|        }
   53|      8|        "\u{2b50}" => {
   54|      1|            star(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   55|      1|        }
   56|      7|        "\u{1f525}" => {
   57|      1|            flame(&mut img, size);
   58|      1|        }
   59|      6|        _ => return None,
   60|       |    }
   61|      8|    Some(img)
   62|     14|}
   63|       |
   64|       |/// Returns true if the emoji has a procedural vector fallback in `make_image`.
   65|       |///
   66|       |/// This is a lightweight check that avoids allocating a ColorImage.
   67|     11|pub fn has_fallback(emoji: &str) -> bool {
   68|     11|    FALLBACK_EMOJI.contains(&emoji)
   69|     11|}

   71|      4|fn draw_circle(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize, color: C) {
   72|      4|    let cx = (size as i32) / 2;
   73|      4|    let cy = cx;
   74|      4|    let r = (size as i32) / 2 - 2;
   75|    136|    for y in 0..size as i32 {
                           ^4
   76|  5.82k|        for x in 0..size as i32 {
                               ^136
   77|  5.82k|            let dx = x - cx;
   78|  5.82k|            let dy = y - cy;
   79|  5.82k|            if dx * dx + dy * dy <= r * r {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (79:16): [True: 3.77k, False: 2.05k]
  ------------------
   80|  3.77k|                img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = color;
   81|  3.77k|            }
                          ^2.05k
   82|       |        }
   83|       |    }
   84|      4|}

   86|      1|fn draw_check(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize, color: C) {
   87|      1|    let s = size as i32;
   88|     40|    let mut plot = |x: i32, y: i32| {
                      ^1
   89|     40|        img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = color;
   90|     40|    };
   91|      8|    for i in 0..s / 3 {
                           ^1
   92|      8|        plot(s / 3 - i, s * 2 / 3 + i);
   93|      8|        plot(s / 3 - i + 1, s * 2 / 3 + i);
   94|      8|    }
   95|     12|    for i in 0..s / 2 {
                           ^1
   96|     12|        plot(s / 3 + i, s * 2 / 3 - i);
   97|     12|        plot(s / 3 + i + 1, s * 2 / 3 - i);
   98|     12|    }
   99|      1|}

  101|      1|fn confetti(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize) {
  102|      1|    let dots = [
  103|      1|        C::from_rgb(255, 80, 80),
  104|      1|        C::from_rgb(80, 180, 255),
  105|      1|        C::from_rgb(120, 220, 120),
  106|      1|        C::from_rgb(220, 120, 220),
  107|      1|    ];
  108|      1|    let s = size as i32;
  109|      4|    for (i, col) in dots.iter().enumerate() {
                                  ^1   ^1     ^1
  110|      4|        let x = (s / 4) * ((i as i32) + 1);
  111|      4|        let y = (s / 5) * ((i as i32) + 1);
  112|      4|        if x < s {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (112:12): [True: 3, False: 1]
  ------------------
  113|      3|            img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = *col;
  114|      3|        }
                      ^1
  115|       |    }
  116|      1|}

  118|      2|fn rocket(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize, body: C, flame: C) {
  119|      2|    let s = size as i32;
  120|       |    // body
  121|     44|    for y in s / 4..s * 3 / 4 {
                           ^2
  122|    768|        for x in s / 3..s * 2 / 3 {
                               ^44
  123|    768|            img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = body;
  124|    768|        }
  125|       |    }
  126|       |    // nose
  127|     14|    for i in 0..s / 6 {
                           ^2
  128|    116|        for x in s / 2 - i..=s / 2 + i {
                               ^14
  129|    116|            let y = s / 4 - i;
  130|    116|            img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = body;
  131|    116|        }
  132|       |    }
  133|       |    // flame
  134|     14|    for i in 0..s / 6 {
                           ^2
  135|    116|        for x in s / 2 - i..=s / 2 + i {
                               ^14
  136|    116|            let y = s * 3 / 4 + i;
  137|    116|            img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = flame;
  138|    116|        }
  139|       |    }
  140|      2|}

  142|      2|fn heart(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize, color: C) {
  143|      2|    let s = size as i32;
  144|     48|    for y in 0..s {
                           ^2
  145|  1.15k|        for x in 0..s {

  147|  1.15k|            let dy = y - s / 3;
  148|  1.15k|            let a = (dx * dx + dy * dy - s * s / 16) < 0;

  157|      1|fn star(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize, color: C) {
  158|      1|    let s = size as i32;
  159|     24|    for i in 0..s {
                           ^1
  160|     24|        let y = i;
  161|     24|        let x1 = s / 2;
  162|     24|        let x2 = s / 2 - i / 2;
  163|     24|        let x3 = s / 2 + i / 2;
  164|     24|        img[(x2 as usize, y as usize)] = color;
  165|     24|        img[(x1 as usize, y as usize)] = color;
  166|     24|        img[(x3 as usize, y as usize)] = color;
  167|     24|    }
  168|      1|}
  169|       |
  170|      1|fn flame(img: &mut egui::ColorImage, size: usize) {
  171|      1|    let s = size as i32;
  172|      1|    let base = C::from_rgb(255, 140, 0);
  173|      1|    let tip = C::from_rgb(255, 220, 120);
  174|     24|    for y in 0..s {
                           ^1
  175|    576|        for x in 0..s {
                               ^24
  176|    576|            let dx = (x - s / 2) as f32 / (s as f32 / 3.5);
  177|    576|            let dy = (y - s) as f32 / (s as f32 / 1.5);
  178|    576|            let v = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  179|    576|            if v < 1.0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (179:16): [True: 161, False: 415]
  ------------------
  180|    161|                let t = 1.0 - v;
  181|    161|                img[(x as usize, y as usize)] = C::from_rgb(
  182|    161|                    (base.r() as f32 * t + tip.r() as f32 * (1.0 - t)) as u8,
  183|    161|                    (base.g() as f32 * t + tip.g() as f32 * (1.0 - t)) as u8,
  184|    161|                    (base.b() as f32 * t + tip.b() as f32 * (1.0 - t)) as u8,
  185|    161|                );
  186|    415|            }
  187|       |        }
  188|       |    }

C:\language\mdmdview\src\emoji_catalog.rs:
    1|       |// emoji_catalog.rs -- Embedded Twemoji PNG lookup and shortcode-to-emoji mapping.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// The catalog embeds 80 Twemoji PNG files at compile time via `include_bytes!`.
    4|       |// Two lookup paths are provided:
    5|       |//   image_bytes_for(emoji)       -> Option<&[u8]>   -- codepoint to PNG bytes
    6|       |//   emoji_for_shortcode(code)    -> Option<&str>     -- shortcode to codepoint
    7|       |//   shortcode_map()              -> &HashMap         -- full shortcode table
    8|       |//
    9|       |// Adding a new emoji requires:
   10|       |//   1. Place the PNG in assets/emoji/<hex>.png
   11|       |//   2. Add the codepoint to the `emoji_png!` invocation in `image_bytes_for`
   12|       |//   3. Add the shortcode mapping in `shortcode_map`
   13|       |//   4. Add the codepoint to `ALL_EMOJI_CODEPOINTS` in tests
   14|       |
   15|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Generates a match expression that maps Unicode literals to embedded PNG bytes.
   18|       |/// Eliminates the repetitive `Some(include_bytes!(concat!(...)))` boilerplate.
   19|       |macro_rules! emoji_png {
   20|       |    ( $input:expr; $( $unicode:literal => $file:literal ),+ $(,)? ) => {
   21|       |        match $input {
   22|       |            $( $unicode => Some(include_bytes!(concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/assets/emoji/", $file))), )+
   23|       |            _ => None,
   24|       |        }
   25|       |    };
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Returns the embedded PNG bytes for a known emoji character.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Uses pattern matching only -- cannot panic regardless of input.
   31|       |/// Unknown emoji characters return `None`.

C:\language\mdmdview\src\image_decode.rs:
    1|       |// Image decoding module for raster (PNG, JPEG, etc.) and SVG images.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// Provides functions to decode image bytes into egui `ColorImage` values
    4|       |// with optional background blending. SVG images are rendered via usvg/resvg
    5|       |// and automatically scaled to fit within MAX_IMAGE_SIDE limits. Raster images
    6|       |// are validated against size and pixel count limits before decoding.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// The primary entry point is `bytes_to_color_image_guess`, which tries raster
    9|       |// decoding first and falls back to SVG.
   10|       |

  272|       |    fn encode_png(rgba: &RgbaImage) -> Vec<u8> {
  273|       |        let mut bytes = Vec::new();
  274|       |        let mut cursor = Cursor::new(&mut bytes);
  275|       |        image::DynamicImage::ImageRgba8(rgba.clone())
  276|       |            .write_to(&mut cursor, ImageOutputFormat::Png)
  277|       |            .expect("encode png");
  278|       |        bytes
  279|       |    }

   26|       |// Automatically lower the test binary's process priority on Windows.
   27|       |// The full test suite (~986 tests, ~8 minutes) saturates all CPU cores,
   28|       |// making remote desktop sessions unresponsive. Setting BELOW_NORMAL priority
   29|       |// lets tests use idle CPU without starving interactive applications.
   30|       |// This runs via the C runtime .CRT$XCU initializer, before main().
   31|       |#[cfg(all(test, windows))]
   32|       |mod test_priority {
   33|       |    extern "system" {
   34|       |        fn GetCurrentProcess() -> isize;
   35|       |        fn SetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize, dwPriorityClass: u32) -> i32;
   36|       |        fn GetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize) -> u32;
   37|       |    }
   38|       |    const BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS: u32 = 0x00004000;
   39|       |
   40|       |    #[used]
   41|       |    #[link_section = ".CRT$XCU"]
   42|       |    static SET_BELOW_NORMAL: unsafe extern "C" fn() = {
   43|      1|        unsafe extern "C" fn init() {
   44|      1|            SetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess(), BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS);
   45|      1|        }
   46|       |        init
   47|       |    };
   48|       |
   49|       |    #[test]
   50|      1|    fn test_process_priority_is_below_normal() {
   51|      1|        let priority = unsafe { GetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess()) };
   52|      1|        assert_eq!(
   53|       |            priority, BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS,
   54|       |            "Test process should be running at BELOW_NORMAL priority, got 0x{:08X}",
   55|       |            priority
   56|       |        );
   57|      1|    }
   58|       |}
   59|       |
   60|       |pub use app::{MarkdownViewerApp, APP_TITLE_PREFIX};
   61|       |pub use markdown_renderer::{MarkdownElement, MarkdownRenderer};
   62|       |pub use sample_files::{SampleFile, SAMPLE_FILES};

   28|       |/// Generic LRU cache that evicts the least-recently-used entry when capacity
   29|       |/// is reached. The `get` method updates access order (true LRU, not FIFO).
   30|       |///

  144|       |#[cfg(not(test))]
  145|       |fn load_fonts_from_dir(ctx: &egui::Context, dir: &Path) -> Result<(), String> {
  146|       |    let mut font_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = std::fs::read_dir(dir)
  147|       |        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read font dir {dir:?}: {e}"))?
  148|       |        .filter_map(|entry| entry.ok().map(|e| e.path()))
  149|       |        .filter(|path| {
  150|       |            path.extension()
  151|       |                .and_then(|ext| ext.to_str())
  152|       |                .map(|ext| matches!(ext.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str(), "ttf" | "otf"))
  153|       |                .unwrap_or(false)
  154|       |        })
  155|       |        .collect();
  156|       |
  157|       |    if font_paths.is_empty() {
  158|       |        return Err(format!("No font files found in {dir:?}"));
  159|       |    }
  160|       |
  161|       |    font_paths.sort_by(|a, b| a.to_string_lossy().cmp(&b.to_string_lossy()));
  162|       |
  163|       |    let mut fonts = egui::FontDefinitions::default();
  164|       |    let mut prop_names = Vec::new();
  165|       |    let mut mono_names = Vec::new();
  166|       |    let mut used_names = std::collections::HashSet::new();
  167|       |
  168|       |    for path in font_paths {
  169|       |        let bytes =
  170|       |            std::fs::read(&path).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read font {path:?}: {e}"))?;
  171|       |        let name = path
  172|       |            .file_stem()
  173|       |            .and_then(|stem| stem.to_str())
  174|       |            .map(|stem| stem.to_string())
  175|       |            .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("font-{}", prop_names.len() + mono_names.len()));
  176|       |        let base = format!("test-{name}");
  177|       |        let mut name = base.clone();
  178|       |        let mut counter = 1usize;
  179|       |        while used_names.contains(&name) {
  180|       |            name = format!("{base}-{counter}");
  181|       |            counter += 1;
  182|       |        }
  183|       |        used_names.insert(name.clone());
  184|       |
  185|       |        fonts
  186|       |            .font_data
  187|       |            .insert(name.clone(), egui::FontData::from_owned(bytes));
  188|       |
  189|       |        let lower = name.to_ascii_lowercase();
  190|       |        if lower.contains("mono") || lower.contains("code") {
  191|       |            mono_names.push(name);
  192|       |        } else {
  193|       |            prop_names.push(name);
  194|       |        }
  195|       |    }
  196|       |
  197|       |    if prop_names.is_empty() {
  198|       |        prop_names = mono_names.clone();
  199|       |    }
  200|       |    if mono_names.is_empty() {
  201|       |        mono_names = prop_names.clone();
  202|       |    }
  203|       |
  204|       |    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Proportional) {
  205|       |        for name in prop_names.iter().rev() {
  206|       |            family.insert(0, name.clone());
  207|       |        }
  208|       |    }
  209|       |    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Monospace) {
  210|       |        for name in mono_names.iter().rev() {
  211|       |            family.insert(0, name.clone());
  212|       |        }
  213|       |    }
  214|       |
  215|       |    ctx.set_fonts(fonts);
  216|       |    Ok(())
  217|       |}

  219|       |/// Application entry point
  220|       |#[cfg(not(test))]
  221|       |fn main() -> Result<(), eframe::Error> {
  222|       |    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
  223|       |    env_logger::init();
  224|       |
  225|       |    let cli = match parse_cli_args() {
  226|       |        Ok(opts) => opts,
  227|       |        Err(err) => {
  228|       |            eprintln!("{err}");
  229|       |            return Ok(());
  230|       |        }
  231|       |    };
  232|       |
  233|       |    let screenshot_enabled = cli.screenshot;
  234|       |    let resolved_theme = if screenshot_enabled {
  235|       |        Some(cli.theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light))
  236|       |    } else {
  237|       |        cli.theme
  238|       |    };
  239|       |    let screenshot_zoom = cli.zoom.unwrap_or(1.0);
  240|       |
  241|       |    let default_width = if screenshot_enabled { 1280.0 } else { 1000.0 };
  242|       |    let default_height = if screenshot_enabled { 720.0 } else { 700.0 };
  243|       |    let window_width = cli.width.unwrap_or(default_width);
  244|       |    let window_height = cli.height.unwrap_or(default_height);
  245|       |
  246|       |    let mut viewport = egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
  247|       |        .with_title(APP_TITLE_PREFIX.to_string())
  248|       |        .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(window_width, window_height))
  249|       |        .with_min_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(
  250|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 600.0 },
  251|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 400.0 },
  252|       |        ))
  253|       |        .with_icon(create_app_icon())
  254|       |        .with_resizable(!screenshot_enabled)
  255|       |        .with_maximize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  256|       |        .with_minimize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  257|       |        .with_drag_and_drop(!screenshot_enabled)
  258|       |        // Start hidden to avoid white flash before dark theme is applied
  259|       |        .with_visible(screenshot_enabled);
  260|       |
  261|       |    if !screenshot_enabled {
  262|       |        if let Some(ws) = load_window_state() {
  263|       |            if let Some(ws) = sanitize_window_state(ws) {
  264|       |                viewport = viewport
  265|       |                    .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(ws.size[0], ws.size[1]))
  266|       |                    .with_position(egui::pos2(ws.pos[0], ws.pos[1]))
  267|       |                    .with_maximized(ws.maximized);
  268|       |            }
  269|       |        }
  270|       |    }
  271|       |
  272|       |    let native_options = eframe::NativeOptions {
  273|       |        viewport,
  274|       |        // Disable eframe's built-in window persistence - we handle it ourselves via
  275|       |        // load_window_state/save_window_state to avoid conflicts
  276|       |        persist_window: false,
  277|       |        ..Default::default()
  278|       |    };
  279|       |
  280|       |    let initial_file = cli.initial_file.clone();
  281|       |    let test_fonts = cli.test_fonts.clone();
  282|       |    let screenshot_config = if screenshot_enabled {
  283|       |        let theme = match resolved_theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light) {
  284|       |            ThemeChoice::Light => ScreenshotTheme::Light,
  285|       |            ThemeChoice::Dark => ScreenshotTheme::Dark,
  286|       |        };
  287|       |        Some(ScreenshotConfig {
  288|       |            output_path: cli
  289|       |                .screenshot_output
  290|       |                .clone()
  291|       |                .expect("screenshot output required"),
  292|       |            viewport_width: window_width,
  293|       |            viewport_height: window_height,
  294|       |            content_only: cli.content_only,
  295|       |            scroll_ratio: cli.scroll,
  296|       |            wait_ms: cli.wait_ms.unwrap_or(2000),
  297|       |            settle_frames: cli.settle_frames.unwrap_or(3),
  298|       |            zoom: screenshot_zoom,
  299|       |            theme,
  300|       |            font_source: test_fonts
  301|       |                .as_ref()
  302|       |                .map(|path| path.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
  303|       |        })
  304|       |    } else {
  305|       |        None
  306|       |    };
  307|       |
  308|       |    eframe::run_native(
  309|       |        APP_TITLE_PREFIX,
  310|       |        native_options,
  311|       |        Box::new(move |cc| {
  312|       |            if let Some(theme) = resolved_theme {
  313|       |                let visuals = match theme {
  314|       |                    ThemeChoice::Light => egui::Visuals::light(),
  315|       |                    ThemeChoice::Dark => egui::Visuals::dark(),
  316|       |                };
  317|       |                cc.egui_ctx.set_visuals(visuals);
  318|       |            }
  319|       |
  320|       |            configure_egui_style(&cc.egui_ctx);
  321|       |            if let Some(font_dir) = test_fonts.as_ref() {
  322|       |                if let Err(err) = load_fonts_from_dir(&cc.egui_ctx, font_dir) {
  323|       |                    eprintln!("{err}");
  324|       |                }
  325|       |            }
  326|       |
  327|       |            let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
  328|       |            // Apply saved theme ONLY when no explicit --theme flag was passed
  329|       |            if resolved_theme.is_none() {
  330|       |                app.apply_saved_theme(&cc.egui_ctx);
  331|       |            }
  332|       |            if screenshot_zoom != 1.0 {
  333|       |                app.set_zoom_scale(screenshot_zoom);
  334|       |            }
  335|       |            if let Some(config) = screenshot_config {
  336|       |                app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
  337|       |            }
  338|       |
  339|       |            if let Some(file_path) = initial_file {
  340|       |                if file_path.exists() && file_path.is_file() {
  341|       |                    if let Err(e) = app.load_file(file_path, true) {
  342|       |                        eprintln!("Failed to load file: {e}");
  343|       |                    }
  344|       |                } else {
  345|       |                    eprintln!("File not found: {}", file_path.display());
  346|       |                }
  347|       |            }
  348|       |
  349|       |            Box::new(app)
  350|       |        }),
  351|       |    )
  352|       |}

  428|       |/// Configure egui styling for optimal markdown display
  429|      2|fn configure_egui_style(ctx: &egui::Context) {
  430|      2|    let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  431|       |
  432|      2|    style.spacing.item_spacing = egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0);
  433|      2|    style.spacing.window_margin = egui::Margin::same(8.0);
  434|      2|    style.spacing.menu_margin = egui::Margin::same(6.0);
  435|       |

  438|       |

  440|      2|    style.visuals.menu_rounding = egui::Rounding::same(4.0);

  443|      2|    if style.visuals.dark_mode {

  450|      2|    ctx.set_style(style);
  451|      2|    configure_font_fallbacks(ctx);
  452|      2|}
  453|       |
  454|       |/// Configure font fallbacks for better symbol coverage.
  455|       |/// Adds platform-specific symbol fonts as fallbacks so characters like ? ? ?
  456|       |/// render instead of showing tofu boxes.
  457|      4|fn install_symbol_fallback(fonts: &mut egui::FontDefinitions, font_data: Vec<u8>) {
  458|      4|    let font_name = "symbol_fallback".to_string();
  459|      4|    fonts
  460|      4|        .font_data
  461|      4|        .insert(font_name.clone(), egui::FontData::from_owned(font_data));
  462|       |
  463|      4|    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Proportional) {
                              ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (463:12): [True: 3, False: 1]
  ------------------
  464|      3|        family.push(font_name.clone());
  465|      3|    }
                  ^1
  466|      4|    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Monospace) {
                              ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (466:12): [True: 3, False: 1]
  ------------------
  467|      3|        family.push(font_name);
  468|      3|    }
                  ^1
  469|      4|}
  470|       |
  471|      3|fn configure_font_fallbacks_from_data(ctx: &egui::Context, font_data: Option<Vec<u8>>) {
  472|      3|    let mut fonts = egui::FontDefinitions::default();
  473|      3|    if let Some(font_data) = font_data {
                              ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (473:12): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------

  475|      2|    }
                  ^1
  476|      3|    ctx.set_fonts(fonts);
  477|      3|}
  478|       |
  479|      2|fn configure_font_fallbacks(ctx: &egui::Context) {
  480|       |    // Platform-specific symbol font paths
  481|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  482|      2|    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  483|      2|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\seguisym.ttf", // Segoe UI Symbol
  484|      2|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\segoeui.ttf",  // Segoe UI (backup)
  485|      2|    ];
  486|       |
  487|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
  488|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  489|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Apple Symbols.ttf",
  490|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial Unicode.ttf",
  491|       |    ];
  492|       |
  493|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
  494|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  495|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf",
  496|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/freefont/FreeSans.ttf",
  497|       |    ];
  498|       |
  499|       |    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos", target_os = "linux")))]
  500|       |    let symbol_font_paths: &[&str] = &[];
  501|       |
  502|       |    // Load the first available symbol font as fallback
  503|      2|    let font_data = symbol_font_paths.iter().find_map(|p| std::fs::read(p).ok());
  504|      2|    configure_font_fallbacks_from_data(ctx, font_data);
  505|      2|}

  507|       |#[cfg(test)]
  508|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  509|       |mod tests {
  510|       |    use super::*;
  511|       |    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
  512|       |
  513|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
  514|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
  515|       |        ENV_LOCK
  516|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
  517|       |            .lock()
  518|       |            .expect("env lock")
  519|       |    }
  520|       |
  521|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  522|       |        key: &'static str,
  523|       |        original: Option<String>,
  524|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
  525|       |    }
  526|       |
  527|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  528|       |        fn unset(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  529|       |            let lock = env_lock();
  530|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  531|       |            std::env::remove_var(key);
  532|       |            Self {
  533|       |                key,
  534|       |                original,
  535|       |                _lock: lock,
  536|       |            }
  537|       |        }
  538|       |    }
  539|       |
  540|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  541|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
  542|       |            if let Some(prev) = &self.original {
  543|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, prev);
  544|       |            } else {
  545|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
  546|       |            }
  547|       |        }
  548|       |    }
  549|       |

  554|       |        assert_eq!(icon.height, 32);
  555|       |        assert_eq!(icon.rgba.len(), 32 * 32 * 4); // RGBA format
  556|       |    }

  560|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  561|       |        let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  562|       |        style.visuals.dark_mode = true;
  563|       |        ctx.set_style(style);
  564|       |
  565|       |        configure_egui_style(&ctx);
  566|       |
  567|       |        let style = ctx.style();
  568|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.item_spacing, egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0));
  569|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.window_margin, egui::Margin::same(8.0));
  570|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.menu_margin, egui::Margin::same(6.0));
  571|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  572|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.menu_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  573|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  574|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.panel_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  575|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.faint_bg_color, egui::Color32::from_gray(20));

  834|       |    fn test_env_guard_restores_previous_value() {
  835|       |        let key = "MDMDVIEW_TEST_GUARD_RESTORE";
  836|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  837|       |        {
  838|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "orig");
  839|       |            let guard = EnvGuard {
  840|       |                key,
  841|       |                original: Some("orig".to_string()),
  842|       |                _lock,
  843|       |            };
  844|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "overwritten");
  845|       |            drop(guard);
  846|       |        }
  847|       |        assert_eq!(std::env::var(key).ok().as_deref(), Some("orig"));
  848|       |        // Clean up
  849|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  850|       |        std::env::remove_var(key);
  851|       |    }
  852|       |
  853|       |    #[test]
  854|       |    fn test_env_guard_drop_removes_unset_key() {
  855|       |        let key = "MDMDVIEW_TEST_GUARD_UNSET";
  856|       |        std::env::remove_var(key);
  857|       |        {
  858|       |            let _guard = EnvGuard::unset(key);
  859|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "temp");
  860|       |        }
  861|       |        assert!(std::env::var(key).is_err());
  862|       |    }

  217|       |/// Generic LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with configurable capacity.
  218|       |///
  219|       |/// This cache evicts the least recently accessed entries when capacity is exceeded.
  220|       |/// Used for `emoji_textures` and `image_failures` to prevent unbounded memory growth.
  221|       |///
  222|       |/// # Performance Note
  223|       |///
  224|       |/// The `touch()`, `insert()` (for existing keys), and `remove()` operations use
  225|       |/// `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n) where n is the cache capacity. This is acceptable
  226|       |/// because:
  227|       |/// - Cache capacities are small and bounded (256-512 entries max)
  228|       |/// - These operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1)
  229|       |/// - For these cache sizes, the constant factors of a more complex O(1) data structure
  230|       |///   (e.g., intrusive linked list with HashMap) would likely outweigh the O(n) cost
  231|       |struct LruCache<K, V> {
  232|       |    entries: HashMap<K, V>,
  233|       |    order: VecDeque<K>,
  234|       |    capacity: usize,
  235|       |}
  236|       |
  237|       |impl<K, V> LruCache<K, V>
  238|       |where
  239|       |    K: Eq + std::hash::Hash + Clone,
  240|       |    V: Clone,
  241|       |{

  244|    951|        Self {
  245|    951|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  246|    951|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  247|    951|            capacity,
  248|    951|        }
  249|    951|    }
  250|       |

  437|       |#[derive(Clone)]
  438|       |struct ImageCacheEntry {
  439|       |    texture: egui::TextureHandle,
  440|       |    size: [u32; 2],
  441|       |    modified: Option<SystemTime>,
  442|       |    /// Approximate memory size in bytes (width * height * 4 for RGBA).
  443|       |    byte_size: usize,
  444|       |    /// True when the texture has been replaced with a 2x2 placeholder to save memory.
  445|       |    degraded: bool,
  446|       |}
  447|       |
  448|       |impl ImageCacheEntry {
  449|       |    /// Estimate texture memory size: width * height * 4 bytes (RGBA).
  450|     77|    fn estimate_bytes(size: [u32; 2]) -> usize {
  451|     77|        (size[0] as usize) * (size[1] as usize) * 4
  452|     77|    }
  453|       |}
  454|       |
  455|       |/// Cache for image textures with entry count and memory size limits.
  456|       |///
  457|       |/// Entry count is capped at `capacity` (safety valve).  Memory budget is
  458|       |/// enforced separately by `enforce_budget()`, which degrades far-from-viewport
  459|       |/// entries to tiny placeholders instead of evicting them.
  460|       |///
  461|       |/// # Performance Note
  462|       |///
  463|       |/// The `remove()` operation uses `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n).
  464|       |/// This is acceptable because the cache capacity is bounded (256 entries max)
  465|       |/// and these operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1).
  466|       |struct ImageCache {
  467|       |    entries: HashMap<String, ImageCacheEntry>,
  468|       |    order: VecDeque<String>,
  469|       |    capacity: usize,
  470|       |    /// Total approximate bytes of all cached textures.
  471|       |    total_bytes: usize,
  472|       |    /// Maximum total bytes before degradation triggers.
  473|       |    max_bytes: usize,
  474|       |    /// Distance of each cached image from the viewport center (in pixels).
  475|       |    /// Updated each frame by the renderer.  When non-empty, enforce_budget()
  476|       |    /// degrades the entry with the greatest distance first.
  477|       |    viewport_distances: HashMap<String, f32>,
  478|       |    /// Estimated bytes of all in-flight restore reloads (enqueued but not yet
  479|       |    /// completed).  Used by `would_restore_fit` to prevent over-committing the
  480|       |    /// budget when multiple degraded images are enqueued in the same frame.
  481|       |    pending_restore_bytes: usize,
  482|       |}
  483|       |
  484|       |impl ImageCache {
  485|    495|    fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
  486|    495|        Self {
  487|    495|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  488|    495|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  489|    495|            capacity: capacity.max(1),
  490|    495|            total_bytes: 0,
  491|    495|            max_bytes: MAX_IMAGE_TEXTURE_CACHE_BYTES,
  492|    495|            viewport_distances: HashMap::new(),
  493|    495|            pending_restore_bytes: 0,
  494|    495|        }
  495|    495|    }
  496|       |
  497|     65|    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<ImageCacheEntry> {
  498|     65|        self.entries.get(key).cloned()
  499|     65|    }
  500|       |
  501|     72|    fn insert(&mut self, key: String, entry: ImageCacheEntry) {
  502|     72|        let new_bytes = entry.byte_size;
  503|       |
  504|       |        // If key already exists, update in-place and adjust total_bytes.
  505|     72|        if let Some(old_entry) = self.entries.get(&key) {
                                  ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (505:16): [True: 2, False: 70]
  ------------------
  506|      2|            self.total_bytes = self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(old_entry.byte_size);
  507|      2|            self.entries.insert(key, entry);
  508|      2|            self.total_bytes += new_bytes;
  509|      2|            return;
  510|     70|        }
  511|       |
  512|       |        // Entry-count safety valve: if at capacity, remove the oldest entry.
  513|     70|        if self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (513:12): [True: 4, False: 66]
  ------------------
  514|      4|            if let Some(old_key) = self.order.front().cloned() {
                                      ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (514:20): [True: 3, False: 1]
  ------------------
  515|      3|                self.order.pop_front();
  516|      3|                self.viewport_distances.remove(&old_key);
  517|      3|                if let Some(old_entry) = self.entries.remove(&old_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (517:24): [True: 3, False: 0]
  ------------------
  518|      3|                    self.total_bytes = self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(old_entry.byte_size);
  519|      3|                }
                              ^0
  520|      1|            }
  521|     66|        }
  522|       |
  523|     70|        self.order.push_back(key.clone());
  524|     70|        self.total_bytes += new_bytes;
  525|     70|        self.entries.insert(key, entry);
  526|     72|    }
  527|       |
  528|       |    /// Replace viewport distances wholesale.  Called once per frame by the
  529|       |    /// renderer after it has collected all image Y positions.
  530|      9|    fn update_viewport_distances(&mut self, distances: HashMap<String, f32>) {
  531|      9|        self.viewport_distances = distances;
  532|      9|    }
  533|       |
  534|      3|    fn remove(&mut self, key: &str) {
  535|      3|        if let Some(entry) = self.entries.remove(key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (535:16): [True: 3, False: 0]

  537|      3|        }
                      ^0
  538|      3|        self.order.retain(|entry| entry != key);
  539|      3|        self.viewport_distances.remove(key);
  540|      3|    }

  553|       |    /// Degrade an entry to a 2x2 neutral grey placeholder texture.
  554|       |    /// Preserves `size` (layout dimensions) and `modified` timestamp.
  555|       |    /// No-op if the key is not found or already degraded.
  556|     19|    fn degrade(&mut self, key: &str, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  557|     19|        let entry = match self.entries.get_mut(key) {
                          ^18
  558|     18|            Some(e) => e,
  559|      1|            None => return,
  560|       |        };
  561|     18|        if entry.degraded {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (561:12): [True: 1, False: 17]
  ------------------
  562|      1|            return;
  563|     17|        }
  564|     17|        let old_bytes = entry.byte_size;
  565|     17|        let placeholder = egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::from_rgb(128, 128, 128));
  566|     17|        entry.texture = ctx.load_texture(
  567|     17|            format!("degraded:{key}"),
  568|     17|            placeholder,
  569|       |            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  570|       |        );
  571|     17|        entry.byte_size = 2 * 2 * 4;
  572|     17|        entry.degraded = true;
  573|     17|        self.total_bytes = self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(old_bytes) + entry.byte_size;
  574|     19|    }
  575|       |
  576|       |    /// Degrade far-from-viewport entries to keep memory within budget and
  577|       |    /// create headroom for near-viewport degraded entries to restore.
  578|       |    ///
  579|       |    /// Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance, from old documents).
  580|       |    /// Phase 2: Degrade current-document entries furthest-first until under budget.
  581|       |    /// Phase 3: Proactively degrade far non-degraded entries to make room for
  582|       |    ///   near-viewport degraded entries. Without this, enforce_budget and
  583|       |    ///   would_restore_fit deadlock: enforce_budget stops because under budget,
  584|       |    ///   but would_restore_fit blocks restores because there's no headroom.
  585|      7|    fn enforce_budget(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context, viewport_height: f32) {
  586|       |        // Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance) first.
  587|       |        // These are leftovers from a previous document — safe to degrade before
  588|       |        // touching current-document entries.
  589|      7|        if self.total_bytes > self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (589:12): [True: 4, False: 3]
  ------------------
  590|      4|            let orphans: Vec<String> = self
  591|      4|                .entries
  592|      4|                .iter()
  593|     13|                .filter(|(_, e)| !e.degraded)
                               ^4
  594|     13|                .filter(|(k, _)| !self.viewport_distances.contains_key(k.as_str()))
                               ^4
  595|      4|                .map(|(k, _)| k.clone())
                                            ^2^2
  596|      4|                .collect();
  597|      4|            for key in &orphans {
                              ^2
  598|      2|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  599|      2|                if self.total_bytes <= self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (599:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  600|      1|                    break;
  601|      1|                }
  602|       |            }
  603|      3|        }
  604|       |
  605|       |        // Build sorted candidate list (non-degraded entries with distances,
  606|       |        // sorted furthest-first). Used by both Phase 2 and Phase 3.
  607|      7|        let mut candidates: Vec<(String, f32)> = self
  608|      7|            .entries
  609|      7|            .iter()
  610|     17|            .filter(|(_, e)| !e.degraded)
                           ^7
  611|     14|            .filter_map(|(k, _)| self.viewport_distances.get(k).map(|&d| (k.clone(), d)))
                           ^7
  612|      7|            .collect();
  613|     12|        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
                      ^7         ^7
  614|       |
  615|       |        // Phase 2: Degrade current-document entries furthest-first until under budget.
  616|      7|        if self.total_bytes > self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (616:12): [True: 3, False: 4]
  ------------------
  617|      7|            for (key, _) in &candidates {
                                          ^3
  618|     24|                let non_degraded_count = self.entries.values().filter(|e| !e.degraded).count();
                                  ^7                   ^7           ^7       ^7                      ^7
  619|      7|                if non_degraded_count <= 1 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (619:20): [True: 0, False: 7]
  ------------------
  620|      0|                    break;
  621|      7|                }
  622|      7|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  623|      7|                if self.total_bytes <= self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (623:20): [True: 3, False: 4]
  ------------------
  624|      3|                    break;
  625|      4|                }
  626|       |            }
  627|      4|        }
  628|       |
  629|       |        // Phase 3: Proactive headroom — degrade far non-degraded entries to make
  630|       |        // room for near-viewport degraded entries that want to restore.
  631|       |        // Compute how many bytes near-viewport degraded entries would need.
  632|      7|        let reload_zone = viewport_height * 2.0;
  633|      7|        let needed: usize = self
  634|      7|            .entries
  635|      7|            .iter()
  636|      7|            .filter(|(_, e)| e.degraded)
  637|     10|            .filter(|(k, _)| {
                           ^7
  638|     10|                self.viewport_distances
  639|     10|                    .get(k.as_str())
  640|     10|                    .is_some_and(|&d| d < reload_zone)
                                                    ^8  ^8
  641|     10|            })
  642|      7|            .map(|(_, e)| ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(e.size).saturating_sub(e.byte_size))
                                        ^1                              ^1      ^1             ^1
  643|      7|            .sum();
  644|       |
  645|      7|        if needed > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (645:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  ------------------
  646|      1|            let target = self.max_bytes.saturating_sub(needed);
  647|       |            // Re-filter candidates (some may have been degraded in Phase 2).
  648|      1|            let far_candidates: Vec<(String, f32)> = candidates
  649|      1|                .iter()
  650|      2|                .filter(|(k, _)| self.entries.get(k).is_some_and(|e| !e.degraded))
                               ^1
  651|      2|                .filter(|(_, d)| *d >= reload_zone)
                               ^1
  652|      1|                .cloned()
  653|      1|                .collect();
  654|       |
  655|      2|            for (key, _) in &far_candidates {
                                          ^1
  656|      2|                if self.total_bytes <= target {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (656:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  657|      1|                    break;
  658|      1|                }
  659|      3|                let non_degraded_count = self.entries.values().filter(|e| !e.degraded).count();
                                  ^1                   ^1           ^1       ^1                      ^1
  660|      1|                if non_degraded_count <= 1 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (660:20): [True: 0, False: 1]
  ------------------
  661|      0|                    break;
  662|      1|                }
  663|      1|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  664|       |            }
  665|      6|        }
  666|      7|    }
  667|       |
  668|       |    /// Returns true if restoring a degraded entry (replacing its 2x2 texture
  669|       |    /// with full-resolution) would keep `total_bytes` within `max_bytes`.
  670|     11|    fn would_restore_fit(&self, key: &str) -> bool {
  671|     11|        let entry = match self.entries.get(key) {
                          ^10
  672|     10|            Some(e) => e,
  673|      1|            None => return false,
  674|       |        };
  675|     10|        if !entry.degraded {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (675:12): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
  676|      1|            return false;
  677|      9|        }
  678|      9|        let full_bytes = ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(entry.size);
  679|      9|        self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(entry.byte_size) + full_bytes + self.pending_restore_bytes
  680|      9|            <= self.max_bytes
  681|     11|    }
  682|       |
  683|    282|    fn clear(&mut self) {
  684|    282|        self.entries.clear();
  685|    282|        self.order.clear();
  686|    282|        self.total_bytes = 0;
  687|    282|        self.viewport_distances.clear();
  688|    282|        self.pending_restore_bytes = 0;
  689|    282|    }

  892|    259|    fn build_layout_job(

 2839|    145|    fn render_element_body(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, element: &MarkdownElement) {
 2840|    145|        match element {
 2841|     52|            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) => {
 2842|     52|                self.render_inline_spans(ui, spans);
 2843|     52|                ui.add_space(4.0);
 2844|     52|            }
 2845|      1|            MarkdownElement::Quote { depth, blocks } => {
 2846|      1|                ui.add_space(4.0);
 2847|      1|                let bar_width = 3.0;
 2848|      1|                let bar_gap = 6.0;
 2849|      1|                let left_pad = 10.0 + (*depth as f32) * (bar_width + bar_gap);
 2850|       |                // Substack-like styling: themed block with orange accent bars
 2851|      1|                let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
 2852|       |
 2853|      1|                let resp = egui::Frame::none()
 2854|      1|                    .fill(tc.blockquote_bg)
 2855|      1|                    .stroke(Stroke::new(1.0, tc.blockquote_border))
 2856|      1|                    .rounding(egui::Rounding::same(6.0))
 2857|      1|                    .inner_margin(egui::Margin {
 2858|      1|                        left: left_pad,
 2859|      1|                        right: 10.0,
 2860|      1|                        top: 8.0,
 2861|      1|                        bottom: 8.0,
 2862|      1|                    })
 2863|      1|                    .show(ui, |ui| {
 2864|      1|                        let prev_override = ui.style().visuals.override_text_color;
 2865|      1|                        ui.style_mut().visuals.override_text_color = Some(tc.blockquote_text);
 2866|      1|                        for block in blocks {
 2867|      1|                            self.render_element_body(ui, block);
 2868|      1|                        }
 2869|      1|                        ui.style_mut().visuals.override_text_color = prev_override;
 2870|      1|                    });
 2871|       |
 2872|       |                // Draw vertical orange quote bars on the left of the frame
 2873|      1|                let rect = resp.response.rect;
 2874|      1|                let top = rect.top() + 6.0;
 2875|      1|                let bottom = rect.bottom() - 6.0;
 2876|      1|                let bar_color = tc.blockquote_bar;
 2877|      1|                for d in 0..*depth {
 2878|      1|                    let x = rect.left() + 6.0 + (d as f32) * (bar_width + bar_gap);
 2879|      1|                    let bar_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 2880|      1|                        egui::pos2(x, top),
 2881|      1|                        egui::pos2(x + bar_width, bottom),
 2882|      1|                    );

 2884|      1|                }

 2886|      1|                ui.add_space(6.0);
 2887|       |            }

 3274|       |    /// Render a clickable link's content directly in the current layout.
 3275|       |    /// Returns (clicked, hovered, last_response) so the caller can handle
 3276|       |    /// link activation, cursor changes, and context menus.
 3277|       |    ///
 3278|       |    /// Each sub-widget (Label for text, Image for emoji) gets
 3279|       |    /// `Sense::click()` applied during creation - this is the egui 0.27
 3280|       |    /// approved pattern that avoids the broken `ui.interact()` overlay.
 3281|     14|    fn render_clickable_link(
 3282|     14|        &self,
 3283|     14|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
 3284|     14|        text: &str,
 3285|     14|        size: f32,

                           ^14

  |  Branch (3293:12): [True: 10, False: 4]

 3296|     10|            let expanded = Self::expand_superscripts(&expanded);

 3298|     10|            if is_strong {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (3298:16): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
 3299|      1|                rich = rich.strong();
 3300|      9|            }

 3302|     10|            let clicked = r.clicked();

 3304|     10|            return (clicked, hovered, Some(r));

                                      ^4

  |  Branch (3314:20): [True: 4, False: 43]

 4930|       |    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
 4931|     26|    fn resolve_table_rects(
 4932|     26|        &self,
 4933|     26|        header_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4934|     26|        body_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4935|     26|        layout_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4936|     26|        header_clip_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4937|     26|        body_clip: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4938|     26|        widths: &[f32],
 4939|     26|        column_spacing: f32,
 4940|     26|    ) -> (egui::Rect, egui::Rect) {
 4941|       |        // Combine header and body clip rects to ensure dividers respect scroll bounds.
 4942|     26|        let clip_rect = match (header_clip_rect, body_clip) {
 4943|     19|            (Some(h), Some(b)) => Some(h.union(b)),
 4944|      7|            (h, b) => h.or(b),
 4945|       |        };
 4946|       |
 4947|       |        // Calculate accurate table width from column widths.
 4948|       |        // This is more accurate than union of cell min_rects which may be smaller
 4949|       |        // than the allocated column space.
 4950|     26|        let calculated_width: f32 =
 4951|     26|            widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + column_spacing * widths.len().saturating_sub(1) as f32;
 4952|       |
 4953|       |        // Combine header/body bounds with layout bounds for accurate borders,
 4954|       |        // especially when the first column is centered or right-aligned.
 4955|     26|        let left = layout_rect
 4956|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.left())
                                      ^22  ^22
 4957|     26|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()))
                                      ^4          ^4         ^1   ^1
 4958|     26|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()));
                                      ^3        ^3         ^1   ^1
 4959|     26|        let right = left.map(|value| value + calculated_width);
                                                   ^24     ^24
 4960|     26|        let top = header_rect
 4961|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.top())
                                      ^22  ^22
 4962|     26|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()))
                                      ^4        ^4         ^1   ^1
 4963|     26|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()));
                                      ^3          ^3         ^1   ^1
 4964|     26|        let bottom = body_rect
 4965|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.bottom())
                                      ^21  ^21
 4966|     26|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()))
                                      ^5          ^5         ^2   ^2
 4967|     26|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()));
                                      ^3          ^3         ^1   ^1
 4968|     26|        let table_rect = match (left, right, top, bottom) {
 4969|     24|            (Some(left), Some(right), Some(top), Some(bottom)) => Some(egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 4970|     24|                egui::pos2(left, top),
 4971|     24|                egui::pos2(right, bottom),
 4972|     24|            )),
 4973|      2|            _ => None,
 4974|       |        };
 4975|       |
 4976|     26|        let zero_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 4977|     26|        let rect = table_rect.or(layout_rect).unwrap_or(zero_rect);
 4978|     26|        let clip_rect = clip_rect.unwrap_or(rect);
 4979|     26|        (rect, clip_rect)
 4980|     26|    }
