 8323|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_accepts_valid_rect() {
 8324|       |        let mut target = None;
 8325|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::vec2(3.0, 4.0));
 8326|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect);
 8327|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(rect));
 8328|       |
 8329|       |        let rect2 = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0));
 8330|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect2);
 8331|       |        let merged = target.expect("merged rect");
 8332|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect.min));
 8333|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect2.max));
 8334|       |    }

 8352|       |    #[test]
 8353|       |    fn test_markdown_renderer_creation() {
 8354|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8355|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, 14.0);
 8356|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.h1, 28.0);
 8357|       |    }

 8368|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_basic() {

 8371|       |        assert!(out.contains("5??"));

 8381|       |    #[test]
 8382|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_single_caret() {
 8383|       |        // Test that single carets (not paired) are left as-is
 8384|       |        let s = "2^32 = 4,294,967,296";
 8385|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(s);
 8386|       |        assert_eq!(out, "2^32 = 4,294,967,296");
 8387|       |
 8388|       |        // Test the problematic line from the bug report
 8389|       |        let problematic = "A 32-bit address bus would provide 2^32 = 4,294,967,296 bytes (4 GB) of addressable memory.";
 8390|       |        let fixed = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(problematic);
 8391|       |        assert_eq!(fixed, problematic);
 8392|       |
 8393|       |        // Test mixed cases
 8394|       |        let mixed = "Use 2^32 for math and 5^th^ for ordinal";
 8395|       |        let result = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(mixed);
 8396|       |        assert!(result.contains("2^32"));
 8397|       |        assert!(result.contains("5??"));
 8398|       |    }

 8401|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_rejects_invalid_sequences() {

 8404|       |        assert_eq!(out, s);
 8405|       |        let no_close = "10^abc";
 8406|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(no_close);
 8407|       |        assert_eq!(out, no_close);
 8408|       |    }

 8418|       |    #[test]
 8419|       |    fn test_zoom_functionality() {
 8420|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8421|       |        let original_body = renderer.font_sizes.body;
 8422|       |
 8423|       |        renderer.zoom_in();
 8424|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body > original_body);
 8425|       |
 8426|       |        renderer.zoom_out();
 8427|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body < original_body * 1.1);
 8428|       |
 8429|       |        renderer.reset_zoom();
 8430|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, original_body);
 8431|       |    }

 8433|       |    #[test]
 8434|       |    fn test_set_zoom_scale() {
 8435|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8436|       |
 8437|       |        renderer.set_zoom_scale(1.5);
 8438|       |        assert!((renderer.font_sizes.body - 21.0).abs() < 0.1);
 8439|       |
 8440|       |        renderer.set_zoom_scale(10.0);
 8441|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, 32.0);
 8442|       |
 8443|       |        renderer.set_zoom_scale(0.1);
 8444|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, 8.0);
 8445|       |    }
 8446|       |
 8447|       |    #[test]
 8448|       |    fn font_size_change_does_not_repersist_stale_widths() {
 8449|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8450|       |        let table_id = 7u64;
 8451|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8452|       |        let widths = vec![100.0f32];
 8453|       |
 8454|       |        // Initial persist at default font size.
 8455|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8456|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8457|       |        {
 8458|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8459|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
 8460|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(100.0));
 8461|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(renderer.font_sizes.body));
 8462|       |        }
 8463|       |
 8464|       |        // Simulate zoom: font size changes but widths captured are still the old ones.
 8465|       |        renderer.font_sizes.body = 16.0;
 8466|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8467|       |        {
 8468|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8469|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8470|       |            // Cleared and NOT re-saved in the same frame.
 8471|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), None);
 8472|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8473|       |        }
 8474|       |
 8475|       |        // Next frame with new layout widths should persist again.
 8476|       |        let new_widths = vec![80.0f32];
 8477|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &new_widths);
 8478|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8479|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8480|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(80.0));
 8481|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8482|       |    }
 8483|       |
 8484|       |    #[test]
 8485|       |    fn highlight_phrase_keeps_persisted_table_widths() {
 8486|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8487|       |        let table_id = 11u64;
 8488|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8489|       |        let widths = vec![120.0f32];
 8490|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8491|       |
 8492|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8493|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("alpha"));
 8494|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("beta"));
 8495|       |
 8496|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8497|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8498|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(120.0));
 8499|       |    }
 8500|       |
 8501|       |    #[test]
 8502|       |    fn test_tight_list_inline_code_and_styles() {
 8503|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8504|       |        let md = "Intro\n\n- Use `code` and **bold** and *italic* and ~~strike~~\n";
 8505|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8506|       |        let (ordered, items) = parsed
 8507|       |            .iter()
 8508|       |            .find_map(|el| {
 8509|       |                if let MarkdownElement::List { ordered, items } = el {
 8510|       |                    Some((*ordered, items))
 8511|       |                } else {
 8512|       |                    None
 8513|       |                }
 8514|       |            })
 8515|       |            .expect("list element");
 8516|       |        assert!(!ordered);
 8517|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
 8518|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
 8519|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
 8520|       |        blocks.extend(items[0].blocks.clone());
 8521|       |        let spans = blocks
 8522|       |            .iter()
 8523|       |            .find_map(|block| {
 8524|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = block {
 8525|       |                    Some(spans)
 8526|       |                } else {
 8527|       |                    None
 8528|       |                }
 8529|       |            })
 8530|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
 8531|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
 8532|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 8533|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
 8534|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
 8535|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 8536|       |        for span in spans {
 8537|       |            match span {
 8538|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
 8539|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 8540|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
 8541|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
 8542|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 8543|       |            }
 8544|       |        }
 8545|       |        assert!(saw_code);
 8546|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 8547|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
 8548|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
 8549|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 8550|       |    }

 8553|       |    fn test_expand_shortcodes_basic() {
 8554|       |        let rocket = MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes(":rocket:");
 8555|       |        assert_ne!(rocket, ":rocket:");
 8556|       |        assert!(crate::emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(&rocket).is_some());
 8557|       |
 8558|       |        let tada = MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes(":tada:");
 8559|       |        assert_ne!(tada, ":tada:");
 8560|       |        assert!(crate::emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(&tada).is_some());
 8561|       |
 8562|       |        assert_eq!(
 8563|       |            MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes("Hello :tada:!"),
 8564|       |            format!("Hello {}!", tada)
 8565|       |        );
 8566|       |    }

 8576|       |    fn test_fix_unicode_chars_normalizes_basic_cases() {

 8579|       |        let normalized = renderer.normalize_text_for_test(input);
 8580|       |        assert_eq!(normalized, "A B-C");

 8647|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_mixed_blocks_adds_newlines() {
 8648|       |        let elements = vec![
 8649|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("First".to_string())]),
 8650|       |            MarkdownElement::List {
 8651|       |                ordered: false,
 8652|       |                items: vec![ListItem {
 8653|       |                    blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8654|       |                        "List".to_string(),
 8655|       |                    )])],
 8656|       |                }],
 8657|       |            },
 8658|       |            MarkdownElement::Quote {
 8659|       |                depth: 1,
 8660|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8661|       |                    "Quote".to_string(),
 8662|       |                )])],
 8663|       |            },
 8664|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {
 8665|       |                headers: vec![
 8666|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H1".to_string())],
 8667|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H2".to_string())],
 8668|       |                ],
 8669|       |                rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("R1".to_string())]]],
 8670|       |                alignments: vec![Alignment::Left, Alignment::Left],
 8671|       |            },
 8672|       |            MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule,
 8673|       |        ];
 8674|       |        let plain_text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
 8675|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "First\nList\nQuote\nH1\nH2\nR1\n---\n");
 8676|       |    }

 8758|       |
 8759|       |    #[test]
 8760|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_detects_file_changes() {
 8761|       |        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().expect("temp dir");
 8762|       |        let file_path = dir.path().join("image.bin");
 8763|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [1u8, 2, 3, 4]).expect("write image");
 8764|       |        let initial = MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(&file_path);
 8765|       |
 8766|       |        // Unchanged file ? not stale
 8767|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8768|       |
 8769|       |        // Deleted file ? NOT stale (keep cached texture; avoids flashing when
 8770|       |        // antivirus or filesystem transiently locks the file)
 8771|       |        std::fs::remove_file(&file_path).expect("remove image");
 8772|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8773|       |
 8774|       |        // File without cached timestamp appears ? stale (pick it up)
 8775|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [4u8, 3, 2, 1]).expect("rewrite image");
 8776|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(None, &file_path));
 8777|       |
 8778|       |        // A timestamp far in the past ? stale (beyond tolerance)
 8779|       |        let old = Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH);
 8780|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(old, &file_path));
 8781|       |    }
 8782|       |
 8783|       |    #[test]
 8784|       |    fn test_disk_image_timestamp_missing_returns_none() {
 8785|       |        let missing = std::path::Path::new("missing_image_timestamp.bin");
 8786|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(missing).is_none());
 8787|       |    }
 8788|       |
 8789|       |    #[test]
 8790|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_with_timestamp_combinations() {
 8791|       |        let stamp = SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH;
 8792|       |        // Had cache, metadata now fails ? NOT stale (transient failure)
 8793|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8794|       |            Some(stamp),
 8795|       |            None
 8796|       |        ));
 8797|       |        // No cache, file appeared ? stale (pick it up)
 8798|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8799|       |            None,
 8800|       |            Some(stamp)
 8801|       |        ));
 8802|       |        // Both None ? not stale
 8803|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8804|       |            None, None
 8805|       |        ));
 8806|       |        // Same timestamp ? not stale
 8807|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8808|       |            Some(stamp),
 8809|       |            Some(stamp)
 8810|       |        ));
 8811|       |        // Within tolerance ? not stale
 8812|       |        let nearby = stamp + Duration::from_secs(5);
 8813|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8814|       |            Some(stamp),
 8815|       |            Some(nearby)
 8816|       |        ));
 8817|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8818|       |            Some(nearby),
 8819|       |            Some(stamp)
 8820|       |        ));
 8821|       |        // Beyond tolerance ? stale
 8822|       |        let far = stamp + IMAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE + Duration::from_secs(1);
 8823|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8824|       |            Some(stamp),
 8825|       |            Some(far)
 8826|       |        ));
 8827|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8828|       |            Some(far),
 8829|       |            Some(stamp)
 8830|       |        ));
 8831|       |    }
 8832|       |
 8833|       |    #[test]
 8834|       |    fn test_inline_code_preserves_whitespace() {
 8835|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8836|       |        let md = "Start `code` end";
 8837|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8838|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8839|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8840|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8841|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8842|       |                let code_span = spans.iter().find_map(|span| match span {
 8843|       |                    InlineSpan::Code(t) => Some(t),
 8844|       |                    _ => None,
 8845|       |                });
 8846|       |                assert_eq!(code_span, Some(&"code".to_string()));
 8847|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8848|       |            }
 8849|       |        }
 8850|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8851|       |    }

 8854|       |    fn test_footnote_markers_render_as_visible_text() {
 8855|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8856|       |        let md = "Paragraph with footnote[^1].\n\n[^1]: footnote body.";
 8857|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8858|       |        assert_eq!(parsed.len(), 2);
 8859|       |        let first_plain = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&parsed[0]);
 8860|       |        assert!(first_plain.contains("footnote[^1]"));
 8861|       |        let second_plain = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&parsed[1]);
 8862|       |        assert!(second_plain.contains("[^1]: footnote body."));
 8863|       |    }

 8866|       |    fn test_parse_headers_assign_ids_and_dedupe() {
 8867|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8868|       |        let md = "# Getting Started\n\nParagraph text.\n\n## Getting Started\n\n### API & Usage\n\n## API & Usage\n";
 8869|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8870|       |
 8871|       |        let mut ids = vec![];
 8872|       |        for el in parsed {
 8873|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = el {
 8874|       |                ids.push(id);
 8875|       |            }
 8876|       |        }
 8877|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 4);
 8878|       |        assert_eq!(ids[0], "getting-started");
 8879|       |        assert_eq!(ids[1], "getting-started-1");
 8880|       |        assert_eq!(ids[2], "api-usage");
 8881|       |        assert_eq!(ids[3], "api-usage-1");
 8882|       |    }

 8884|       |    #[test]
 8885|       |    fn test_formatting_sample_contains_expected_header_ids() {
 8886|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8887|       |        let formatting = SAMPLE_FILES
 8888|       |            .iter()
 8889|       |            .find(|f| f.name == "formatting.md")
 8890|       |            .expect("formatting sample present");
 8891|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(formatting.content).expect("parse ok");
 8892|       |        let ids: Vec<String> = parsed
 8893|       |            .into_iter()
 8894|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
 8895|       |                MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } => Some(id),
 8896|       |                _ => None,
 8897|       |            })
 8898|       |            .collect();
 8899|       |
 8900|       |        for expected in [
 8901|       |            "markdown-formatting-guide",
 8902|       |            "table-of-contents",
 8903|       |            "text-formatting",
 8904|       |            "headers",
 8905|       |            "lists",
 8906|       |            "links-and-images",
 8907|       |            "emojis",
 8908|       |            "blockquotes",
 8909|       |            "horizontal-rules",
 8910|       |            "tables",
 8911|       |        ] {
 8912|       |            assert!(ids.iter().any(|id| id == expected));
 8913|       |        }
 8914|       |    }

 8916|       |    #[test]
 8917|       |    fn test_inline_image_parsing() {
 8918|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8919|       |        let md = "Here is an image: ![Alt text](images/pic.webp \"Title\") end.";
 8920|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8921|       |        assert_eq!(parsed.len(), 1);
 8922|       |        assert!(matches!(&parsed[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
 8923|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8924|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8925|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8926|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8927|       |                let img = spans.iter().find(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. }));
 8928|       |                assert!(img.is_some());
 8929|       |                let (src, alt, title) = image_span_fields(img.unwrap()).expect("image span");
 8930|       |                assert_eq!(src, "images/pic.webp");
 8931|       |                assert_eq!(alt, "Alt text");
 8932|       |                assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
 8933|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8934|       |            }
 8935|       |        }
 8936|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8937|       |        assert!(image_span_fields(&InlineSpan::Text("nope".into())).is_none());
 8938|       |    }

 8953|       |            level: 2,
 8954|       |            spans: vec![InlineSpan::Text("Title".to_string())],
 8955|       |            id: "title".to_string(),
 8956|       |        };
 8957|       |        let code = MarkdownElement::CodeBlock {
 8958|       |            text: "fn main() {}".to_string(),
 8959|       |            language: Some("rust".to_string()),
 8960|       |            highlighted: None,
 8961|       |        };

 8965|       |        );

 8972|       |    #[test]
 8973|       |    fn test_cell_fragments_split_text_and_images() {
 8974|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8975|       |        let spans = vec![
 8976|       |            InlineSpan::Text("alpha".into()),
 8977|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("beta".into()),
 8978|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
 8979|       |                src: "img.png".into(),
 8980|       |                alt: "img".into(),
 8981|       |                title: None,
 8982|       |            },
 8983|       |            InlineSpan::Text("gamma".into()),
 8984|       |        ];
 8985|       |        let fragments = renderer.cell_fragments(&spans);
 8986|       |        assert_eq!(fragments.len(), 3);
 8987|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[0], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 8988|       |        let mut saw_first_text = false;
 8989|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[0], &fragments[1]] {
 8990|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 8991|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 2);
 8992|       |                saw_first_text = true;
 8993|       |            }
 8994|       |        }
 8995|       |        assert!(saw_first_text);
 8996|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[1], CellFragment::Image(_)));
 8997|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 8998|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[1], &fragments[0]] {
 8999|       |            if let CellFragment::Image(span) = fragment {
 9000|       |                let (src, _alt, _title) = image_span_fields(span).expect("image span");
 9001|       |                assert_eq!(src, "img.png");
 9002|       |                saw_image = true;
 9003|       |            }
 9004|       |        }
 9005|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 9006|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[2], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 9007|       |        let mut saw_last_text = false;
 9008|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[2], &fragments[1]] {
 9009|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 9010|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 1);
 9011|       |                let content = text_span_content(&slice[0]).expect("text span");
 9012|       |                assert_eq!(content, "gamma");
 9013|       |                saw_last_text = true;
 9014|       |            }
 9015|       |        }
 9016|       |        assert!(saw_last_text);
 9017|       |        assert!(text_span_content(&InlineSpan::Image {
 9018|       |            src: "img.png".into(),
 9019|       |            alt: "img".into(),
 9020|       |            title: None,
 9021|       |        })
 9022|       |        .is_none());
 9023|       |    }

 9103|       |    #[test]
 9104|       |    fn test_get_or_make_emoji_texture_generates_on_decode_error() {
 9105|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9106|       |        let fallback = [
 9107|       |            "\u{2705}",
 9108|       |            "\u{1f389}",
 9109|       |            "\u{1f680}",
 9110|       |            "\u{2764}",
 9111|       |            "\u{1f496}",
 9112|       |            "\u{2b50}",
 9113|       |            "\u{1f525}",
 9114|       |        ];
 9115|       |        let emoji = crate::emoji_catalog::shortcode_map()
 9116|       |            .values()
 9117|       |            .find(|emoji| {
 9118|       |                !fallback.contains(emoji) && emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(emoji).is_some()
 9119|       |            })
 9120|       |            .expect("emoji outside fallback");
 9121|       |        assert!(emoji_assets::make_image(emoji, 64).is_none());
 9122|       |        force_emoji_decode_error_once();
 9123|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
 9124|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, emoji);
 9125|       |        });
 9126|       |    }

 9199|       |            "A long column entry that should wrap neatly within the supplied width.".into(),
 9200|       |        )];

 9208|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_highlights_matches() {
 9209|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9210|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("wrap"));
 9211|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("wrap me, wrap me again".into())];
 9212|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9213|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 200.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9214|       |        let highlight_bg = style.visuals.selection.bg_fill;
 9215|       |        assert!(build

 9222|       |    #[test]
 9223|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_tracks_link_ranges() {
 9224|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9225|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
 9226|       |            text: "Docs".into(),
 9227|       |            url: "https://example.org/docs".into(),
 9228|       |        }];
 9229|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9230|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 220.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9231|       |        assert_eq!(build.link_ranges.len(), 1);
 9232|       |        let link = &build.link_ranges[0];
 9233|       |        assert_eq!(link.url, "https://example.org/docs");
 9234|       |        let char_len = link.char_range.end - link.char_range.start;
 9235|       |        let linked_text: String = build
 9236|       |            .job
 9237|       |            .text
 9238|       |            .chars()
 9239|       |            .skip(link.char_range.start)
 9240|       |            .take(char_len)
 9241|       |            .collect();
 9242|       |        assert_eq!(linked_text, "Docs");
 9243|       |    }

 9271|       |    #[test]
 9272|       |    fn test_append_spans_empty_and_code_light() {
 9273|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9274|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9275|       |        let mut job = LayoutJob::default();
 9276|       |        let mut plain_text = String::new();
 9277|       |
 9278|       |        assert_eq!(
 9279|       |            renderer.append_plain_span(
 9280|       |                &style,
 9281|       |                &mut job,
 9282|       |                &mut plain_text,
 9283|       |                "",
 9284|       |                InlineStyle::default(),
 9285|       |                None
 9286|       |            ),
 9287|       |            0
 9288|       |        );
 9289|       |        assert_eq!(
 9290|       |            renderer.append_code_span(&style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, ""),
 9291|       |            0
 9292|       |        );
 9293|       |
 9294|       |        let mut light_style = style.clone();
 9295|       |        light_style.visuals.dark_mode = false;
 9296|       |        assert!(renderer.append_code_span(&light_style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, "code") > 0);
 9297|       |
 9298|       |        let mut empty_job = LayoutJob::default();
 9299|       |        let mut empty_plain = String::new();
 9300|       |        assert_eq!(
 9301|       |            renderer.append_text_sections(
 9302|       |                &style,
 9303|       |                &mut empty_job,
 9304|       |                &mut empty_plain,
 9305|       |                "",
 9306|       |                12.0,
 9307|       |                InlineStyle::default(),
 9308|       |                None
 9309|       |            ),
 9310|       |            0
 9311|       |        );
 9312|       |    }

 9314|       |    #[test]
 9315|       |    fn test_is_html_line_break_and_indented_code_tabs() {
 9316|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_html_line_break("<b"));
 9317|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_html_line_break("<br >"));
 9318|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_html_line_break("<brx>"));
 9319|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_indented_code_line("\tcode"));
 9320|       |    }
 9321|       |
 9322|       |    #[test]
 9323|       |    fn test_is_html_line_break_rejects_missing_bracket() {
 9324|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_html_line_break("<br"));
 9325|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_html_line_break("<br/"));
 9326|       |    }

 9332|       |    }

 9341|       |    fn test_escape_pipes_inline_code_line_branches() {
 9342|       |        let plain = "no pipes here";
 9343|       |        assert_eq!(
 9344|       |            MarkdownRenderer::escape_pipes_in_inline_code_line(plain),
 9345|       |            plain
 9346|       |        );
 9347|       |
 9348|       |        let line = "``code`|more``";
 9349|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_pipes_in_inline_code_line(line);
 9350|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
 9351|       |    }

 9366|       |
 9367|       |    #[test]
 9368|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_keeps_short_columns_readable() {
 9369|       |        let mins = vec![90.0, 90.0, 90.0, 90.0];
 9370|       |        let desired = vec![120.0, 120.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9371|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(420.0, &mins, &desired);
 9372|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9373|       |        // Narrow columns should stay near their minimums even when a wide column exists
 9374|       |        assert!(widths[0] >= 85.0);
 9375|       |        assert!(widths[2] > widths[0]);
 9376|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9377|       |        assert!((sum - 420.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9378|       |    }
 9379|       |
 9380|       |    #[test]
 9381|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_handles_constrained_space() {
 9382|       |        let mins = vec![100.0, 100.0, 100.0, 100.0];
 9383|       |        let desired = vec![200.0, 240.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9384|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(260.0, &mins, &desired);
 9385|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9386|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 0.0));
 9387|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9388|       |        assert!((sum - 260.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9389|       |    }
 9390|       |
 9391|       |    #[test]
 9392|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_clip_rect_fallbacks() {
 9393|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9394|       |        let widths = vec![12.0];
 9395|       |        let body_clip = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(3.0, 4.0));
 9396|       |        let (rect, clip) =
 9397|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, Some(body_clip), &widths, 4.0);
 9398|       |        assert_eq!(clip, body_clip);
 9399|       |
 9400|       |        let (rect2, clip2) =
 9401|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9402|       |        assert_eq!(clip2, rect2);
 9403|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9404|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.max, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9405|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9406|       |    }
 9407|       |
 9408|       |    #[test]
 9409|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_prefers_layout_then_header_then_body() {
 9410|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9411|       |        let widths = vec![10.0, 20.0];
 9412|       |        let layout = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(5.0, 6.0), egui::pos2(25.0, 26.0));
 9413|       |        let header = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(9.0, 12.0));
 9414|       |        let body = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(2.0, 3.0), egui::pos2(14.0, 18.0));
 9415|       |
 9416|       |        let (rect, _) = renderer.resolve_table_rects(
 9417|       |            Some(header),
 9418|       |            Some(body),
 9419|       |            Some(layout),
 9420|       |            None,
 9421|       |            None,
 9422|       |            &widths,
 9423|       |            4.0,
 9424|       |        );
 9425|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, layout.min.x);
 9426|       |
 9427|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9428|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(Some(header), None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9429|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, header.min.x);
 9430|       |
 9431|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9432|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, Some(body), None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9433|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, body.min.x);
 9434|       |    }
 9435|       |
 9436|       |    #[test]
 9437|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_layout_only_fallbacks() {
 9438|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9439|       |        let widths = vec![12.0];
 9440|       |        let layout = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(4.0, 7.0), egui::pos2(18.0, 21.0));
 9441|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9442|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, Some(layout), None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9443|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.y, layout.min.y);
 9444|       |        assert_eq!(rect.max.y, layout.max.y);
 9445|       |    }
 9446|       |
 9447|       |    #[test]
 9448|       |    fn table_cells_keep_images_and_formatting() {
 9449|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9450|       |        let md = "\
 9451|       |Intro
 9452|       |
 9453|       || H1 | H2 |
 9454|       || --- | --- |
 9455|       || text **extra** ![Alt](img.png) | **bold** and [link](https://example.com) |";
 9456|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9457|       |        let table = elements
 9458|       |            .iter()
 9459|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9460|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9461|       |                    headers: _,
 9462|       |                    rows,
 9463|       |                    alignments: _,
 9464|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9465|       |                _ => None,
 9466|       |            })
 9467|       |            .expect("table present");
 9468|       |        assert_eq!(table.len(), 1);
 9469|       |        let row = &table[0];
 9470|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 9471|       |        let mut saw_text = false;
 9472|       |        for span in &row[0] {
 9473|       |            match span {
 9474|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
 9475|       |                InlineSpan::Text(_) => saw_text = true,
 9476|       |                _ => {}
 9477|       |            }
 9478|       |        }
 9479|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 9480|       |        assert!(saw_text);
 9481|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 9482|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
 9483|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 9484|       |        for span in &row[1] {
 9485|       |            match span {
 9486|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 9487|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
 9488|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 9489|       |            }
 9490|       |        }
 9491|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 9492|       |        assert!(saw_link);
 9493|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 9494|       |    }
 9495|       |
 9496|       |    #[test]
 9497|       |    fn table_inline_code_keeps_pipes() {
 9498|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9499|       |        let md = "\
 9500|       |Intro
 9501|       |
 9502|       || Col | Notes |
 9503|       || --- | --- |
 9504|       || code | see `a|b|c` |";
 9505|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9506|       |        let rows = elements
 9507|       |            .iter()
 9508|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9509|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9510|       |                    headers: _,
 9511|       |                    rows,
 9512|       |                    alignments: _,
 9513|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9514|       |                _ => None,
 9515|       |            })
 9516|       |            .expect("table present");
 9517|       |        let code_text = rows[0][1]
 9518|       |            .iter()
 9519|       |            .find_map(|span| match span {
 9520|       |                InlineSpan::Code(text) => Some(text.as_str()),
 9521|       |                _ => None,
 9522|       |            })
 9523|       |            .expect("code span");
 9524|       |        assert_eq!(code_text, "a|b|c");
 9525|       |    }

 9554|       |    #[test]
 9555|       |    fn blockquote_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9556|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9557|       |        let md = "\
 9558|       |Prelude
 9559|       |
 9560|       |> Intro line
 9561|       |> | Col | Notes |
 9562|       |> | --- | --- |
 9563|       |> | A | B |";
 9564|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9565|       |        let blocks = elements
 9566|       |            .iter()
 9567|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9568|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9569|       |                _ => None,
 9570|       |            })
 9571|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9572|       |        assert!(blocks
 9573|       |            .iter()
 9574|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9575|       |        Ok(())
 9576|       |    }
 9577|       |
 9578|       |    #[test]
 9579|       |    fn blockquote_list_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9580|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9581|       |        let md = "\
 9582|       |Prelude
 9583|       |
 9584|       |> Note before list
 9585|       |> - Item:
 9586|       |>   | Key | Val |
 9587|       |>   | --- | --- |
 9588|       |>   | A | 1 |";
 9589|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9590|       |        let blocks = elements
 9591|       |            .iter()
 9592|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9593|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9594|       |                _ => None,
 9595|       |            })
 9596|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9597|       |        let list = blocks
 9598|       |            .iter()
 9599|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
 9600|       |                MarkdownElement::List { items, .. } => Some(items),
 9601|       |                _ => None,
 9602|       |            })
 9603|       |            .expect("list block");
 9604|       |        let first = list.first().expect("list item");
 9605|       |        assert!(first
 9606|       |            .blocks
 9607|       |            .iter()
 9608|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9609|       |        Ok(())
 9610|       |    }

 9806|       |    #[test]
 9807|       |    fn list_marker_any_indent_tab_stops_leading_whitespace() {
 9808|       |        let line = " \t- item";
 9809|       |        let (_content, _indent, _content_indent, leading) =
 9810|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(line).expect("list marker");
 9811|       |        assert_eq!(leading, 4);
 9812|       |    }

 9969|       |    #[test]
 9970|       |    fn table_ids_are_unique_per_position() {
 9971|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9972|       |        let md = "\
 9973|       |Intro
 9974|       |
 9975|       || H |
 9976|       || - |
 9977|       || a |
 9978|       |
 9979|       || H |
 9980|       || - |
 9981|       || a |";
 9982|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9983|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
 9984|       |            .iter()
 9985|       |            .enumerate()
 9986|       |            .filter_map(|(idx, el)| match el {
 9987|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9988|       |                    headers,
 9989|       |                    rows,
 9990|       |                    alignments,
 9991|       |                } => Some(renderer.compute_table_id(headers, rows, alignments, idx as u64)),
 9992|       |                _ => None,
 9993|       |            })
 9994|       |            .collect();
 9995|       |        assert_eq!(tables.len(), 2);
 9996|       |        assert_ne!(tables[0], tables[1]);
 9997|       |    }

10002|       |        let md = "\
10003|       |# Coverage Demo
10004|       |
10005|       |Paragraph with :tada: emoji and **bold** text and a [link](#target).
10006|       |
10007|       |> Quote line one
10008|       |> Quote line two
10009|       |
10010|       |- item one
10011|       |- item two
10012|       |
10013|       |---
10014|       |
10015|       |```rust
10016|       |fn main() {}
10017|       |```
10018|       |
10019|       || Col A | Col B |
10020|       || --- | --- |
10021|       || A1 | B1 |
10022|       || A2 | B2 |
10023|       |

10025|       |![Sample](assets/samples/webp_sample.webp \"Sample\")
10026|       |![Missing](missing_test_image.png \"Missing\")
10027|       |![Remote](https://example.com/image.png \"Remote\")
10028|       |";
10029|       |
10030|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
10031|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("item"));
10032|       |

10035|       |        });

10038|       |        assert!(renderer.header_rect_for("coverage-demo").is_some());
10039|       |        let (rendered, total) = renderer.table_render_stats();
10040|       |        assert!(total > 0);
10041|       |        assert!(rendered > 0);
10042|       |        let (_hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
10043|       |        assert!(misses > 0);
10044|       |    }

10046|       |    #[test]
10047|       |    fn test_table_layout_cache_records_hits() {
10048|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10049|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("alpha".to_string())];
10050|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
10051|       |
10052|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10053|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10054|       |
10055|       |        let (hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
10056|       |        assert!(hits >= 1);
10057|       |        assert!(misses >= 1);
10058|       |
10059|       |        renderer.clear_table_layout_cache();
10060|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.table_layout_cache_stats(), (0, 0));
10061|       |    }

10063|       |    #[test]
10064|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_eviction() {
10065|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(2);
10066|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10067|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10068|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10069|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10070|       |        };
10071|       |        let key = |row, col| CellLayoutKey {
10072|       |            row: Some(row),
10073|       |            col,
10074|       |            width: 120,
10075|       |            align: 0,
10076|       |            strong: false,
10077|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10078|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10079|       |            content_hash: row as u64,
10080|       |        };
10081|       |
10082|       |        let key_a = key(0, 0);
10083|       |        let key_b = key(1, 0);
10084|       |        let key_c = key(2, 0);
10085|       |
10086|       |        cache.insert(key_a.clone(), build.clone());
10087|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build.clone());
10088|       |        cache.insert(key_c.clone(), build);
10089|       |
10090|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_a).is_none());
10091|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
10092|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_c).is_some());
10093|       |    }
10094|       |
10095|       |    #[test]
10096|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_handles_empty_order() {
10097|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(1);
10098|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10099|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10100|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10101|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10102|       |        };
10103|       |        let key_a = CellLayoutKey {
10104|       |            row: Some(0),
10105|       |            col: 0,
10106|       |            width: 120,
10107|       |            align: 0,
10108|       |            strong: false,
10109|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10110|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10111|       |            content_hash: 1,
10112|       |        };
10113|       |        let key_b = CellLayoutKey {
10114|       |            row: Some(1),
10115|       |            col: 0,
10116|       |            width: 120,
10117|       |            align: 0,
10118|       |            strong: false,
10119|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10120|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10121|       |            content_hash: 2,
10122|       |        };
10123|       |
10124|       |        cache.insert(key_a, build.clone());
10125|       |        cache.order.clear();
10126|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build);
10127|       |
10128|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
10129|       |    }

10132|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_reinsert_existing_key() {
10133|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(1);
10134|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10135|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10136|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10137|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10138|       |        };
10139|       |        let key = CellLayoutKey {
10140|       |            row: Some(0),
10141|       |            col: 0,
10142|       |            width: 120,
10143|       |            align: 0,
10144|       |            strong: false,
10145|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10146|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10147|       |            content_hash: 42,
10148|       |        };
10149|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build.clone());
10150|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build);
10151|       |        assert_eq!(cache.entries.len(), 1);
10152|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order.len(), 1);
10153|       |    }

10163|       |

10166|       |        let _ =
10167|       |            renderer.cached_layout_job(&dark_style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10168|       |

10209|       |    #[test]
10210|       |    fn test_table_first_column_alignment_parses_three_columns() -> Result<()> {
10211|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10212|       |        let md = fs::read_to_string("tests/regression/cases/026-table-first-column-align.md")?;
10213|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(&md)?;
10214|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
10215|       |            .iter()
10216|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
10217|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
10218|       |                    headers,
10219|       |                    rows,
10220|       |                    alignments,
10221|       |                } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
10222|       |                _ => None,
10223|       |            })
10224|       |            .collect();
10225|       |        assert!(tables.len() >= 3);
10226|       |        let (first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments) = tables[0];
10227|       |        for (headers, rows, alignments) in tables.iter() {
10228|       |            assert_eq!(headers.len(), 3);
10229|       |            assert_eq!(alignments.len(), 3);
10230|       |            assert!(rows.iter().all(|row| row.len() == 3));
10231|       |        }
10232|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments, 0);
10233|       |        let available = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10234|       |        let spacing = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10235|       |        let _estimated_before = {
10236|       |            let column_stats = renderer.column_stats_for_table(
10237|       |                table_id,
10238|       |                first_headers,
10239|       |                first_rows,
10240|       |                first_alignments,
10241|       |            );
10242|       |            let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(
10243|       |                first_headers,
10244|       |                first_rows,
10245|       |                &column_stats,
10246|       |                renderer.font_sizes.body,
10247|       |                table_id,
10248|       |            );
10249|       |            let column_specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
10250|       |            let column_spacing = 6.0f32.max(egui::Style::default().spacing.item_spacing.x);
10251|       |            renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &column_specs, column_spacing)
10252|       |        };
10253|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10254|       |            available.set(ui.available_width());
10255|       |            spacing.set(ui.spacing().item_spacing.x.max(6.0));
10256|       |            renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(
10257|       |                ui,
10258|       |                first_headers,
10259|       |                first_rows,
10260|       |                first_alignments,
10261|       |                table_id,
10262|       |            );
10263|       |        });
10264|       |        let widths = renderer
10265|       |            .table_metrics
10266|       |            .borrow()
10267|       |            .entry(table_id)
10268|       |            .map(|entry| entry.current_widths().to_vec())
10269|       |            .unwrap_or_default();
10270|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
10271|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 8.0));
10272|       |        Ok(())
10273|       |    }
10274|       |
10275|       |    #[test]
10276|       |    fn test_trigger_link_handles_fragments_and_schemes() {
10277|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10278|       |
10279|       |        renderer.trigger_link("#Section-One");
10280|       |        assert_eq!(
10281|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
10282|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
10283|       |        );
10284|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10285|       |
10286|       |        renderer.trigger_link("ftp://example.com");
10287|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10288|       |
10289|       |        renderer.trigger_link("https://example.com");
10290|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10291|       |
10292|       |        renderer.trigger_link("mailto:hello@example.com");
10293|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10294|       |    }

10296|       |    #[test]
10297|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_with_base_dir() {
10298|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10299|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10300|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10301|       |
10302|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("sample.png");
10303|       |        assert!(resolved.contains("sample.png"));
10304|       |
10305|       |        let abs = temp.path().join("abs.png");
10306|       |        let abs_str = abs.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
10307|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(&abs_str), abs_str);
10308|       |
10309|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(None);
10310|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path("relative.png"), "relative.png");
10311|       |    }

10313|       |    #[test]
10314|       |    fn test_disk_image_loads_and_caches() {
10315|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10316|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10317|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("disk.png");
10318|       |
10319|       |        let mut img = image::RgbaImage::new(2, 2);
10320|       |        for pixel in img.pixels_mut() {
10321|       |            *pixel = image::Rgba([10, 20, 30, 255]);
10322|       |        }
10323|       |        img.save(&image_path).expect("save png");
10324|       |
10325|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10326|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("disk.png");
10327|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
10328|       |            // Use wait_for_image to handle async image loading
10329|       |            let loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
10330|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some());
10331|       |        });
10332|       |
10333|       |        assert!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved));
10334|       |    }

10372|       |    fn test_estimate_table_column_widths_scales_down() {
10373|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10374|       |        let specs = vec![
10375|       |            resizable_spec(0, "A", 100.0, 100.0),
10376|       |            resizable_spec(1, "B", 100.0, 100.0),
10377|       |        ];
10378|       |
10379|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_table_column_widths(&specs, 150.0, 10.0);
10380|       |        let total = widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + 10.0;
10381|       |        assert!(total <= 150.5);
10382|       |    }

10384|       |    #[test]
10385|       |    fn test_estimate_table_row_widths_distributes_remainder() {
10386|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10387|       |        let min_floor = renderer.table_min_column_width();
10388|       |        let specs = vec![
10389|       |            fixed_spec(0, "Ord", 80.0),
10390|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "Params", ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false }, None),
10391|       |        ];
10392|       |        let resolved = vec![80.0, min_floor];
10393|       |        let adjusted = resolved.clone();
10394|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_table_row_widths(
10395|       |            &specs, &resolved, &adjusted, 320.0, min_floor, false, false,
10396|       |        );
10397|       |        assert!((widths[0] - 80.0).abs() < 0.1);
10398|       |        assert!(widths[1] > min_floor + 40.0);
10399|       |    }

10457|       |    fn test_render_inline_span_variants() {
10458|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10459|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10460|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
10461|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes()).expect("write png");
10462|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10463|       |
10464|       |        let image_span = InlineSpan::Image {
10465|       |            src: "image.png".to_string(),
10466|       |            alt: "Alt".to_string(),
10467|       |            title: Some("Title".to_string()),
10468|       |        };
10469|       |        let missing_span = InlineSpan::Image {
10470|       |            src: "missing.png".to_string(),
10471|       |            alt: "".to_string(),
10472|       |            title: None,
10473|       |        };
10474|       |        let remote_span = InlineSpan::Image {
10475|       |            src: "https://example.com/image.png".to_string(),
10476|       |            alt: "Remote".to_string(),
10477|       |            title: None,
10478|       |        };
10479|       |        let remote_http_span = InlineSpan::Image {
10480|       |            src: "http://example.com/image.png".to_string(),
10481|       |            alt: "RemoteHttp".to_string(),
10482|       |            title: None,
10483|       |        };
10484|       |
10485|       |        let _guard = ForcedRenderActions::new(&["link_hover", "link_click", "image_hover"]);
10486|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
10487|       |            ui.visuals_mut().dark_mode = false;
10488|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &InlineSpan::Code("code".to_string()), None, None);
10489|       |
10490|       |            ui.visuals_mut().dark_mode = true;
10491|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &InlineSpan::Text("text".to_string()), None, None);
10492|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &InlineSpan::Strong("bold".to_string()), None, None);
10493|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(
10494|       |                ui,
10495|       |                &InlineSpan::Emphasis("italic".to_string()),
10496|       |                None,
10497|       |                None,
10498|       |            );
10499|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(
10500|       |                ui,
10501|       |                &InlineSpan::Strikethrough("strike".to_string()),
10502|       |                None,
10503|       |                None,
10504|       |            );
10505|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(
10506|       |                ui,
10507|       |                &InlineSpan::Link {
10508|       |                    text: "ext".to_string(),
10509|       |                    url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
10510|       |                },
10511|       |                None,
10512|       |                None,
10513|       |            );
10514|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(
10515|       |                ui,
10516|       |                &InlineSpan::Link {
10517|       |                    text: "local".to_string(),
10518|       |                    url: "#anchor".to_string(),
10519|       |                },
10520|       |                None,
10521|       |                None,
10522|       |            );
10523|       |            let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("image.png");
10524|       |            let _loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
10525|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &image_span, None, None);
10526|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &missing_span, None, None);
10527|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &remote_span, None, None);
10528|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &remote_http_span, None, None);
10529|       |        });
10530|       |    }

10544|       |    fn test_has_pending_renders_with_mermaid_pending() {
10545|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10546|       |        // Give worker threads time to start up under heavy parallel test execution.
10547|       |        // Workers need to initialize QuickJS runtime which can be slow under load.
10548|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(200));
10549|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10550|       |            renderer.mermaid.begin_frame();
10551|       |            renderer
10552|       |                .mermaid
10553|       |                .render_block(ui, "graph TD; A-->B;", 1.0, 14.0, MermaidTheme::Default);
10554|       |        });
10555|       |        assert!(renderer.has_pending_renders());
10556|       |    }

10558|       |    #[test]
10559|       |    fn test_parse_lists_and_blockquotes() {
10560|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10561|       |        let md = "\
10562|       |- Item 1
10563|       |- Item 2
10564|       |  - Nested
10565|       |
10566|       |1. First
10567|       |2. Second
10568|       |
10569|       |> Quote line
10570|       |> > Nested quote
10571|       |
10572|       |---";
10573|       |
10574|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
10575|       |        assert!(elements
10576|       |            .iter()
10577|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: false, .. })));
10578|       |        assert!(elements
10579|       |            .iter()
10580|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10581|       |        assert!(elements
10582|       |            .iter()
10583|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::Quote { .. })));
10584|       |        assert!(elements
10585|       |            .iter()
10586|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule)));
10587|       |    }

10590|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_variants() {
10591|       |        let elements = vec![
10592|       |            MarkdownElement::Header {
10593|       |                level: 1,
10594|       |                spans: vec![InlineSpan::Text("Title".to_string())],
10595|       |                id: "title".to_string(),
10596|       |            },
10597|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![
10598|       |                InlineSpan::Text("Hello".to_string()),
10599|       |                InlineSpan::Code("code".to_string()),
10600|       |            ]),
10601|       |            MarkdownElement::CodeBlock {
10602|       |                language: Some("rust".to_string()),
10603|       |                text: "fn main() {}".to_string(),
10604|       |                highlighted: None,
10605|       |            },
10606|       |            MarkdownElement::List {
10607|       |                ordered: false,
10608|       |                items: vec![ListItem {
10609|       |                    blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10610|       |                        "Item".to_string(),
10611|       |                    )])],
10612|       |                }],
10613|       |            },
10614|       |            MarkdownElement::Quote {
10615|       |                depth: 1,
10616|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10617|       |                    "Quote".to_string(),
10618|       |                )])],
10619|       |            },
10620|       |            MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule,
10621|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {
10622|       |                headers: vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("H".to_string())]],
10623|       |                rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Image {
10624|       |                    src: "img.png".to_string(),
10625|       |                    alt: "Alt".to_string(),
10626|       |                    title: None,
10627|       |                }]]],
10628|       |                alignments: Vec::new(),
10629|       |            },
10630|       |        ];
10631|       |        let text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
10632|       |        assert!(text.contains("Title"));

10699|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_respects_line_breaks() {
10700|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10701|       |        let single_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10702|       |            "Short This is a much longer line".to_string(),
10703|       |        )];
10704|       |        let multi_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10705|       |            "Short\nThis is a much longer line".to_string(),
10706|       |        )];
10707|       |
10708|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10709|       |            let single = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &single_line);
10710|       |            let multi = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &multi_line);
10711|       |            assert!(multi < single);
10712|       |        });
10713|       |    }

10716|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_normalizes_unicode() {
10717|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10718|       |        let unicode = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A\u{2192}B".to_string())];
10719|       |        let ascii = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A->B".to_string())];
10720|       |
10721|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10722|       |            let unicode_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &unicode);
10723|       |            let ascii_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &ascii);
10724|       |            assert!((unicode_width - ascii_width).abs() < 0.5);
10725|       |        });

10729|       |    fn test_to_superscript_full_mapping() {
10730|       |        let input = "0123456789+-=()abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ#";
10731|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::to_superscript(input);
10732|       |        assert_eq!(out.chars().count(), input.chars().count());
10733|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{2070}'));
10734|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{00b9}'));
10735|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{1d43}'));
10736|       |        assert!(out.contains('#'));
10737|       |    }

10740|       |    fn test_element_plain_text_variants() {
10741|       |        let list = MarkdownElement::List {
10742|       |            ordered: false,
10743|       |            items: vec![ListItem {
10744|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10745|       |                    "Item".to_string(),
10746|       |                )])],
10747|       |            }],
10748|       |        };
10749|       |        let quote = MarkdownElement::Quote {
10750|       |            depth: 1,
10751|       |            blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10752|       |                "Quote".to_string(),
10753|       |            )])],
10754|       |        };
10755|       |        let table = MarkdownElement::Table {
10756|       |            headers: vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Header".to_string())]],
10757|       |            rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Cell".to_string())]]],
10758|       |            alignments: Vec::new(),
10759|       |        };
10760|       |
10761|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&list).contains("Item"));
10762|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&quote).contains("Quote"));
10763|       |        assert_eq!(
10764|       |            MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule),
10765|       |            "---"
10766|       |        );
10767|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&table).contains("Header"));
10768|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&table).contains("Cell"));
10769|       |    }

10771|       |    #[test]
10772|       |    fn test_parse_element_image_outside_paragraph() {
10773|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10774|       |        let events = vec![
10775|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10776|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10777|       |                "img.png".into(),
10778|       |                "Title".into(),
10779|       |            )),
10780|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
10781|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10782|       |        ];
10783|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
10784|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10785|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&events, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
10786|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10787|       |        assert!(matches!(
10788|       |            elements.first(),
10789|       |            Some(MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_))
10790|       |        ));
10791|       |        let spans = paragraph_spans(elements.first().expect("element")).expect("paragraph spans");
10792|       |        let (_src, _alt, title) = image_span_fields(&spans[0]).expect("image span");
10793|       |        assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
10794|       |        assert!(paragraph_spans(&MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule).is_none());
10795|       |    }
10796|       |
10797|       |    #[test]
10798|       |    fn test_parse_element_empty_paragraph_skips() {
10799|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10800|       |        let events = vec![Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph), Event::End(Tag::Paragraph)];
10801|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
10802|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
10803|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&events, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
10804|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10805|       |        assert!(elements.is_empty());
10806|       |    }

10824|       |    #[test]
10825|       |    fn test_parse_list_event_variants() -> Result<()> {
10826|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10827|       |        let events = vec![
10828|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
10829|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10830|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10831|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10832|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10833|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10834|       |            Event::Text("strong".into()),
10835|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10836|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10837|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10838|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10839|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10840|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10841|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10842|       |                "".into(),
10843|       |            )),
10844|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10845|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10846|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10847|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10848|       |                "img.png".into(),
10849|       |                "Title".into(),
10850|       |            )),
10851|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10852|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10853|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10854|       |            Event::Text("text".into()),
10855|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10856|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10857|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10858|       |            Event::Text("nested".into()),
10859|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10860|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10861|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10862|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
10863|       |        ];
10864|       |
10865|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10866|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
10867|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10868|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
10869|       |        let item = &items[0];
10870|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
10871|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
10872|       |        blocks.extend(item.blocks.clone());
10873|       |        let spans = blocks
10874|       |            .iter()
10875|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
10876|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) => Some(spans),
10877|       |                _ => None,
10878|       |            })
10879|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
10880|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
10881|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strong(_))));
10882|       |        assert!(spans
10883|       |            .iter()
10884|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
10885|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
10886|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
10887|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Code(_))));
10888|       |        assert!(item
10889|       |            .blocks
10890|       |            .iter()
10891|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10892|       |        Ok(())
10893|       |    }
