10895|       |    #[test]
10896|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_variants() {
10897|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10898|       |        let events = vec![
10899|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10900|       |            Event::Text("Hello".into()),
10901|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10902|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10903|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10904|       |            Event::Text("bold".into()),
10905|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10906|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10907|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10908|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10909|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10910|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10911|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10912|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10913|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10914|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10915|       |                "".into(),
10916|       |            )),
10917|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10918|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10919|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10920|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10921|       |                "img.png".into(),
10922|       |                "Title".into(),
10923|       |            )),
10924|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10925|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10926|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10927|       |        ];
10928|       |
10929|       |        let (spans, next) =
10930|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10931|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10932|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
10933|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
10934|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
10935|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
10936|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
10937|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
10938|       |        let mut saw_break_text = false;
10939|       |        for span in &spans {
10940|       |            match span {
10941|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
10942|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
10943|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
10944|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
10945|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
10946|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
10947|       |                InlineSpan::Text(t) if t.contains('\n') => saw_break_text = true,
10948|       |                _ => {}
10949|       |            }
10950|       |        }
10951|       |        assert!(saw_code);
10952|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
10953|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
10954|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
10955|       |        assert!(saw_link);
10956|       |        assert!(saw_image);
10957|       |        assert!(saw_break_text);
10958|       |    }
10959|       |
10960|       |    #[test]
10961|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_html_breaks() {
10962|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10963|       |        let events = vec![
10964|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10965|       |            Event::Text("one".into()),
10966|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
10967|       |            Event::Text("two".into()),
10968|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10969|       |        ];
10970|       |
10971|       |        let (spans, next) =
10972|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10973|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10974|       |        assert!(spans
10975|       |            .iter()
10976|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10977|       |
10978|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
10979|       |        let text = MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans_no_break);
10980|       |        assert!(text.contains("one two"));
10981|       |
10982|       |        let attr_events = vec![
10983|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10984|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
10985|       |            Event::Html("<br class=\"tight\">".into()),
10986|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
10987|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10988|       |        ];
10989|       |        let (spans, next) =
10990|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&attr_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10991|       |        assert_eq!(next, attr_events.len());
10992|       |        assert!(spans
10993|       |            .iter()
10994|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10995|       |
10996|       |        let strong_events = vec![
10997|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10998|       |            Event::Text("lead ".into()),
10999|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
11000|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
11001|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
11002|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
11003|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
11004|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11005|       |        ];
11006|       |        let (_spans, next) =
11007|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
11008|       |        assert_eq!(next, strong_events.len());
11009|       |
11010|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
11011|       |        let mut saw_non_strong = false;
11012|       |        let mut strong_text = None;
11013|       |        for span in &spans_no_break {
11014|       |            if let InlineSpan::Strong(text) = span {
11015|       |                strong_text = Some(text.as_str());
11016|       |            } else {
11017|       |                saw_non_strong = true;
11018|       |            }
11019|       |        }
11020|       |        assert!(saw_non_strong);
11021|       |        let mut saw_match = false;
11022|       |        let mut saw_miss = false;
11023|       |        let mut saw_none = false;
11024|       |        for candidate in [strong_text, Some("nope"), None] {
11025|       |            if let Some(text) = candidate {
11026|       |                if text.contains("alpha beta") {
11027|       |                    saw_match = true;
11028|       |                } else {
11029|       |                    saw_miss = true;
11030|       |                }
11031|       |            } else {
11032|       |                saw_none = true;
11033|       |            }
11034|       |        }
11035|       |        assert!(saw_match);
11036|       |        assert!(saw_miss);
11037|       |        assert!(saw_none);
11038|       |    }
11039|       |

11060|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),

11069|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11070|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11071|       |        ];
11072|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();

11238|       |    #[test]
11239|       |    fn test_parse_element_variants() {
11240|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11241|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
11242|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
11243|       |
11244|       |        let paragraph = vec![
11245|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11246|       |            Event::Text("para".into()),
11247|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11248|       |        ];
11249|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&paragraph, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11250|       |        assert_eq!(next, paragraph.len());
11251|       |
11252|       |        let heading = vec![
11253|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11254|       |            Event::Text("Heading".into()),
11255|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11256|       |        ];
11257|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&heading, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11258|       |        assert_eq!(next, heading.len());
11259|       |
11260|       |        let code_block = vec![
11261|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11262|       |                "rust".into(),
11263|       |            ))),
11264|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}\n".into()),
11265|       |            Event::End(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11266|       |                "rust".into(),
11267|       |            ))),
11268|       |        ];
11269|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&code_block, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11270|       |        assert_eq!(next, code_block.len());
11271|       |
11272|       |        let list = vec![
11273|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
11274|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
11275|       |            Event::Text("item".into()),
11276|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
11277|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
11278|       |        ];
11279|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&list, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11280|       |        assert_eq!(next, list.len());
11281|       |
11282|       |        let quote = vec![
11283|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
11284|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11285|       |            Event::Text("quote".into()),
11286|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11287|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11288|       |        ];
11289|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&quote, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11290|       |        assert_eq!(next, quote.len());
11291|       |
11292|       |        let table = vec![
11293|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
11294|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
11295|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
11296|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
11297|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
11298|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
11299|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
11300|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
11301|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
11302|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
11303|       |            Event::Text("R".into()),
11304|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
11305|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
11306|       |            Event::End(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
11307|       |        ];
11308|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&table, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11309|       |        assert_eq!(next, table.len());
11310|       |
11311|       |        let rule = vec![Event::Rule];
11312|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&rule, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11313|       |        assert_eq!(next, rule.len());
11314|       |    }
11315|       |

11317|       |    fn test_parse_code_block_with_language() -> Result<()> {
11318|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11319|       |        let elements = renderer.parse("Intro\n\n```rust\nfn main() {}\n```")?;
11320|       |        let mut rust_lang = None;
11321|       |        for element in &elements {
11322|       |            if let MarkdownElement::CodeBlock { language, .. } = element {
11323|       |                rust_lang = language.as_deref();
11324|       |            }
11325|       |        }
11326|       |        assert_eq!(rust_lang, Some("rust"));
11327|       |        Ok(())
11328|       |    }

11334|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11335|       |                "".into(),
11336|       |            ))),
11337|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}".into()),
11338|       |            Event::End(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11339|       |                "".into(),
11340|       |            ))),
11341|       |        ];

11343|       |        assert_eq!(language, None);

11375|       |        let spans = vec![
11376|       |            InlineSpan::Text("t".to_string()),
11377|       |            InlineSpan::Code("c".to_string()),
11378|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("s".to_string()),
11379|       |            InlineSpan::Emphasis("e".to_string()),
11380|       |            InlineSpan::Strikethrough("x".to_string()),
11381|       |            InlineSpan::Link {
11382|       |                text: "link".to_string(),
11383|       |                url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
11384|       |            },
11385|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
11386|       |                src: "img.png".to_string(),
11387|       |                alt: "alt".to_string(),
11388|       |                title: Some("title".to_string()),
11389|       |            },
11390|       |        ];
11391|       |        let hash = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&spans);
11392|       |        let hash2 = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&[InlineSpan::Text("other".to_string())]);
11393|       |        assert_ne!(hash, hash2);
11394|       |    }

11397|       |    fn test_highlight_segments_edge_cases() {
11398|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11399|       |        assert!(renderer.highlight_segments("", Some("a")).is_empty());
11400|       |        assert_eq!(
11401|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", None),
11402|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11403|       |        );
11404|       |        assert_eq!(
11405|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", Some("")),
11406|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11407|       |        );
11408|       |        let segments = renderer.highlight_segments("Hello", Some("ell"));
11409|       |        assert!(segments.iter().any(|(_, highlighted)| *highlighted));
11410|       |    }

11413|       |    fn test_resolve_table_widths_branches() {
11414|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(50.0, &[], &[]).is_empty());
11415|       |        assert_eq!(
11416|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(100.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]),
11417|       |            vec![20.0, 30.0]
11418|       |        );
11419|       |
11420|       |        assert_eq!(
11421|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[0.0, 0.0], &[0.0, 0.0]),
11422|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11423|       |        );
11424|       |
11425|       |        assert_eq!(
11426|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 20.0]),
11427|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11428|       |        );
11429|       |
11430|       |        assert_eq!(
11431|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[10.0, 10.0]),
11432|       |            vec![15.0, 15.0]
11433|       |        );
11434|       |
11435|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]);
11436|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
11437|       |        assert!((widths[0] + widths[1] - 30.0).abs() < 0.1);
11438|       |    }

11474|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11475|       |        let md = fs::read_to_string("tests/regression/cases/026-table-first-column-align.md")?;

11477|       |        let mut tables = elements.iter().filter_map(|el| match el {
11478|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {
11479|       |                headers,
11480|       |                rows,
11481|       |                alignments,
11482|       |            } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
11483|       |            _ => None,
11484|       |        });

11628|       |        renderer.set_allow_remote_images(true);

11630|       |            spawn_http_response_server("HTTP/1.1 200 OK", "image/png", tiny_png_bytes());
11631|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {

11634|       |        });

11738|       |    #[test]
11739|       |    fn test_paint_table_text_job_link_interaction() {
11740|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11741|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
11742|       |            text: "Click here for more text".to_string(),
11743|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
11744|       |        }];
11745|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
11746|       |        let click_pos = egui::pos2(5.0, 5.0);
11747|       |
11748|       |        let input = input_with_click(click_pos, egui::PointerButton::Secondary);
11749|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
11750|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
11751|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
11752|       |                |ui| {
11753|       |                    let build =
11754|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 30.0, false, Align::LEFT);
11755|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
11756|       |                },
11757|       |            );
11758|       |        });
11759|       |
11760|       |        let input = input_with_click(click_pos, egui::PointerButton::Primary);
11761|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
11762|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
11763|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
11764|       |                |ui| {
11765|       |                    let build =
11766|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 30.0, false, Align::LEFT);
11767|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
11768|       |                },
11769|       |            );
11770|       |        });
11771|       |    }

11773|       |    #[test]
11774|       |    fn test_aligned_text_origin_anchors_to_rect() {
11775|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(10.0, 20.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 20.0));
11776|       |        let galley = Arc::new(Galley {
11777|       |            job: Arc::new(LayoutJob::default()),
11778|       |            rows: Vec::new(),
11779|       |            elided: false,
11780|       |            rect: egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(20.0, 10.0)),
11781|       |            mesh_bounds: egui::Rect::NOTHING,
11782|       |            num_vertices: 0,
11783|       |            num_indices: 0,
11784|       |            pixels_per_point: 1.0,
11785|       |        });
11786|       |        let left_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::LEFT);
11787|       |        let center_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::Center);
11788|       |        let right_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::RIGHT);
11789|       |
11790|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.x, rect.left());
11791|       |        assert_eq!(center_origin.x, rect.center().x - galley.rect.center().x);
11792|       |        assert_eq!(right_origin.x, rect.right() - galley.rect.right());
11793|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.y, rect.top());
11794|       |    }
11795|       |

11797|       |    fn test_layout_job_center_rect_is_origin() {
11798|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11799|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("Item".to_string())];
11800|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
11801|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 200.0, true, Align::Center);
11802|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
11803|       |            assert!(galley.rect.center().x.abs() <= 0.01);
11804|       |        });
11805|       |    }

11856|       |    fn test_find_syntax_for_language_direct_name_match() -> Result<()> {
11857|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11858|       |        let syntax = SyntaxDefinition::load_from_str(
11859|       |            r#"
11860|       |name: testlang
11861|       |scope: source.testlang
11862|       |file_extensions: [tst]
11863|       |contexts:
11864|       |  main:
11865|       |    - match: '.'
11866|       |      scope: source.testlang
11867|       |"#,
11868|       |            true,
11869|       |            None,
11870|       |        )
11871|       |        .expect("syntax");
11872|       |        let mut builder = renderer.syntax_set.clone().into_builder();
11873|       |        builder.add(syntax);
11874|       |        renderer.syntax_set = builder.build();
11875|       |        assert!(renderer.find_syntax_for_language("testlang").is_some());
11876|       |        Ok(())
11877|       |    }

12075|       |>

12088|       |    fn test_find_syntax_for_language_variants() {
12089|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12090|       |        assert!(renderer.find_syntax_for_language("rs").is_some());
12091|       |        let cs_ext = renderer.syntax_set.find_syntax_by_extension("cs");
12092|       |        let cs_lang = renderer.find_syntax_for_language("csharp");
12093|       |        assert_eq!(cs_lang.is_some(), cs_ext.is_some());
12094|       |        let ps_ext = renderer.syntax_set.find_syntax_by_extension("ps1");
12095|       |        let ps_lang = renderer.find_syntax_for_language("powershell");
12096|       |        assert_eq!(ps_lang.is_some(), ps_ext.is_some());
12097|       |        assert!(renderer.find_syntax_for_language("madeuplang").is_none());
12098|       |    }

12131|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_code_and_image() {
12132|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12133|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12134|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
12135|       |                "test-image",
12136|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
12137|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
12138|       |            );
12139|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
12140|       |                "image.png".to_string(),
12141|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
12142|       |                    texture: tex,
12143|       |                    size: [64, 32],
12144|       |                    modified: None,
12145|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([64, 32]),
12146|       |                    degraded: false,
12147|       |                },
12148|       |            );
12149|       |            let spans = vec![
12150|       |                InlineSpan::Text("Hello".to_string()),
12151|       |                InlineSpan::Code("line1\nline2".to_string()),
12152|       |                InlineSpan::Image {
12153|       |                    src: "image.png".to_string(),
12154|       |                    alt: "Alt".to_string(),
12155|       |                    title: None,
12156|       |                },
12157|       |            ];

12159|       |            assert!(width > 0.0);

12163|       |    #[test]
12164|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_cache_and_remote() -> Result<()> {
12165|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12166|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12167|       |            assert!(renderer
12168|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/logo.png")
12169|       |                .is_none());
12170|       |        });
12171|       |
12172|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12173|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12174|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12175|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12176|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12177|       |            let first = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
12178|       |            assert!(first.is_some());
12179|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12180|       |            assert!(second.is_some());
12181|       |        });
12182|       |        Ok(())
12183|       |    }
12184|       |
12185|       |    #[test]
12186|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_stale_cache_evicted() -> Result<()> {
12187|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12188|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12189|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12190|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12191|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12192|       |
12193|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12194|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
12195|       |                "stale-image",
12196|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
12197|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
12198|       |            );
12199|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
12200|       |                resolved.clone(),
12201|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
12202|       |                    texture: tex,
12203|       |                    size: [2, 2],
12204|       |                    modified: Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH),
12205|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([2, 2]),
12206|       |                    degraded: false,
12207|       |                },
12208|       |            );
12209|       |
12210|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12211|       |            assert!(result.is_none());
12212|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved));
12213|       |            assert!(renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains(&resolved));
12214|       |        });
12215|       |
12216|       |        Ok(())
12217|       |    }
12218|       |
12219|       |    #[test]
12220|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_enqueue_failure() -> Result<()> {
12221|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12222|       |        let (image_job_tx, image_job_rx) = bounded(0);
12223|       |        drop(image_job_rx);
12224|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = image_job_tx;
12225|       |
12226|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12227|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12228|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12229|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12230|       |
12231|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12232|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12233|       |            assert!(result.is_none());
12234|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains(&resolved));
12235|       |        });
12236|       |
12237|       |        Ok(())
12238|       |    }

12240|       |    #[test]
12241|       |    fn test_enqueue_degraded_reload_remote_enqueues_request() {
12242|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12243|       |        let (tx, rx) = bounded(1);
12244|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = tx;
12245|       |
12246|       |        assert!(renderer.enqueue_degraded_reload(
12247|       |            "http://127.0.0.1/test.png",
12248|       |            "remote-cache",
12249|       |            None,
12250|       |            true,
12251|       |            Path::new("ignored"),
12252|       |        ));
12253|       |
12254|       |        let request = rx.try_recv().expect("request");
12255|       |        assert_eq!(request.key, "remote-cache");
12256|       |        assert!(matches!(
12257|       |            request.source,
12258|       |            ImageLoadSource::Remote(ref url) if url == "http://127.0.0.1/test.png"
12259|       |        ));
12260|       |        assert!(renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains("remote-cache"));
12261|       |    }
12262|       |

12267|       |            "missing.png",

12319|       |    fn test_poll_image_results_records_failure() {
12320|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12321|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
12322|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
12323|       |        renderer
12324|       |            .image_pending
12325|       |            .borrow_mut()
12326|       |            .insert("failed.png".to_string());
12327|       |
12328|       |        result_tx
12329|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
12330|       |                key: "failed.png".to_string(),
12331|       |                generation: 0,
12332|       |            })
12333|       |            .expect("send");
12334|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
12335|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
12336|       |        assert!(renderer
12337|       |            .image_failures
12338|       |            .borrow()
12339|       |            .contains_key(&"failed.png".to_string()));
12340|       |        assert!(!renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains("failed.png"));
12341|       |    }

12343|       |    #[test]
12344|       |    fn test_poll_image_results_loaded_degraded_entry_clears_pending_restore_bytes() {
12345|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12346|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
12347|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
12348|       |
12349|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
12350|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
12351|       |                "reload-entry",
12352|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
12353|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
12354|       |            );
12355|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
12356|       |                "reload.png".to_string(),
12357|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
12358|       |                    texture: tex,
12359|       |                    size: [10, 10],
12360|       |                    modified: None,
12361|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([2, 2]),
12362|       |                    degraded: true,
12363|       |                },
12364|       |            );
12365|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().pending_restore_bytes =
12366|       |                ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]);
12367|       |            renderer
12368|       |                .image_pending
12369|       |                .borrow_mut()
12370|       |                .insert("reload.png".to_string());
12371|       |
12372|       |            result_tx
12373|       |                .send(ImageLoadResult::Loaded {
12374|       |                    key: "reload.png".to_string(),
12375|       |                    image: egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
12376|       |                    size: [10, 10],
12377|       |                    modified: None,
12378|       |                    generation: 0,
12379|       |                })
12380|       |                .expect("send");
12381|       |
12382|       |            assert!(renderer.poll_image_results(ctx));
12383|       |        });
12384|       |
12385|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().pending_restore_bytes, 0);
12386|       |        assert!(!renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains("reload.png"));
12387|       |        assert!(renderer
12388|       |            .image_textures
12389|       |            .borrow()
12390|       |            .entries
12391|       |            .get("reload.png")
12392|       |            .is_some_and(|entry| !entry.degraded));
12393|       |    }

12408|       |    fn test_enqueue_image_job_full_returns_err() {
12409|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12410|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
12411|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = job_tx;
12412|       |        let request = ImageLoadRequest {
12413|       |            key: "full".to_string(),
12414|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12415|       |            generation: 0,
12416|       |        };
12417|       |        renderer.image_job_tx.send(request).expect("fill queue");
12418|       |        let request = ImageLoadRequest {
12419|       |            key: "full-2".to_string(),
12420|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12421|       |            generation: 0,
12422|       |        };
12423|       |        assert!(renderer.enqueue_image_job(request).is_err());
12424|       |        drop(job_rx);
12425|       |    }
12426|       |
12427|       |    /// Integration test: solid-color PNGs that are tiny on disk but large when
12428|       |    /// decoded.  Verifies that (a) insert() does not evict by bytes, and
12429|       |    /// (b) enforce_budget() degrades far entries to bring memory under budget.
12430|       |    #[test]
12431|       |    fn test_cache_pressure_with_large_decoded_images() {
12432|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12433|       |
12434|       |        // Each 200x200 image decodes to 200*200*4 = 160,000 bytes.
12435|       |        // Set cache limit to hold exactly 2 images worth (320,000 bytes).
12436|       |        renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().max_bytes = 160_000 * 2;
12437|       |
12438|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
12439|       |        let mut keys = Vec::new();
12440|       |        for i in 0..4 {
12441|       |            let name = format!("img_{i}.png");
12442|       |            let path = temp.path().join(&name);
12443|       |            std::fs::write(&path, solid_png_bytes(200, 200)).expect("write png");
12444|       |            keys.push(path.to_string_lossy().to_string());
12445|       |        }
12446|       |
12447|       |        // Verify the PNGs are tiny on disk
12448|       |        let disk_size = std::fs::metadata(temp.path().join("img_0.png"))
12449|       |            .unwrap()
12450|       |            .len();
12451|       |        assert!(
12452|       |            disk_size < 2000,
12453|       |            "solid PNG should be tiny on disk, got {disk_size} bytes"
12454|       |        );
12455|       |
12456|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
12457|       |            // Simulate the background loader completing all 4 images.
12458|       |            // insert() no longer evicts by bytes — all 4 should be present.
12459|       |            for key in keys.iter() {
12460|       |                let image = egui::ColorImage::new([200, 200], Color32::WHITE);
12461|       |                let tex =
12462|       |                    ctx.load_texture(format!("img:{key}"), image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR);
12463|       |                renderer.store_image_texture(key, tex, [200, 200], None);
12464|       |            }
12465|       |
12466|       |            {
12467|       |                let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12468|       |                assert_eq!(
12469|       |                    cache.entries.len(),
12470|       |                    4,
12471|       |                    "all 4 images should be present (no byte eviction on insert)"
12472|       |                );
12473|       |                assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 160_000 * 4);
12474|       |            }
12475|       |
12476|       |            // Set viewport distances: keys[0] near, keys[3] far
12477|       |            {
12478|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12479|       |                let mut distances = HashMap::new();
12480|       |                for (i, key) in keys.iter().enumerate() {
12481|       |                    distances.insert(key.clone(), (i as f32 + 1.0) * 1000.0);
12482|       |                }
12483|       |                cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
12484|       |            }
12485|       |
12486|       |            // enforce_budget should degrade the furthest entries
12487|       |            {
12488|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12489|       |                cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
12490|       |            }
12491|       |
12492|       |            let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12493|       |            // All entries should still exist (degraded, not evicted)
12494|       |            for key in &keys {
12495|       |                assert!(cache.contains_key(key), "entry should still exist: {key}");
12496|       |            }
12497|       |            // Memory should be at or under budget
12498|       |            assert!(
12499|       |                cache.current_bytes() <= 160_000 * 2,
12500|       |                "total_bytes should be under budget, got {}",
12501|       |                cache.current_bytes()
12502|       |            );
12503|       |            // Near entry should not be degraded
12504|       |            assert!(
12505|       |                !cache.entries.get(&keys[0]).unwrap().degraded,
12506|       |                "nearest image should not be degraded"
12507|       |            );
12508|       |        });
12509|       |    }
12510|       |
12511|       |    /// Verify that solid PNGs produce the expected compression ratio:
12512|       |    /// small on disk, large decoded.
12513|       |    #[test]
12514|       |    fn test_solid_png_compression_ratio() {
12515|       |        let bytes = solid_png_bytes(500, 500);
12516|       |        // 500x500 decodes to 1,000,000 bytes (1 MB), but the PNG should be
12517|       |        // well under 10 KB on disk due to solid-color compression.
12518|       |        assert!(
12519|       |            bytes.len() < 10_000,
12520|       |            "solid 500x500 PNG should compress well, got {} bytes",
12521|       |            bytes.len()
12522|       |        );
12523|       |        // Verify it decodes to the expected dimensions
12524|       |        let (img, w, h) =
12525|       |            image_decode::bytes_to_color_image_guess(&bytes, None).expect("should decode");
12526|       |        assert_eq!(w, 500);
12527|       |        assert_eq!(h, 500);
12528|       |        assert_eq!(img.pixels.len(), 500 * 500);
12529|       |    }
12530|       |
12531|       |    #[test]
12532|       |    fn test_wait_for_image_with_limit_returns_none() {
12533|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();

12536|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
12537|       |            let missing = wait_for_image_with_limit(&renderer, &ctx, ui, "missing", 0);
12538|       |            assert!(missing.is_none());
12539|       |        });
12540|       |    }

12731|       |    #[test]
12732|       |    fn test_render_table_estimates_wrapped_row_height() {
12733|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12734|       |        let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12735|       |            width: 80.0,
12736|       |            clip: false,
12737|       |        }]);
12738|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Params".to_string())]];
12739|       |        let rows = vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
12740|       |            "word word word word word word word word".to_string(),
12741|       |        )]]];
12742|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 18);
12743|       |
12744|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12745|       |            let layout = *ui.layout();
12746|       |            ui.allocate_ui_with_layout(Vec2::new(120.0, 0.0), layout, |ui| {
12747|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 6.0;
12748|       |                ui.set_width(120.0);
12749|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
12750|       |            });
12751|       |        });
12752|       |
12753|       |        // Check stored row height in metrics
12754|       |        let height = renderer
12755|       |            .table_metrics
12756|       |            .borrow()
12757|       |            .entry(table_id)
12758|       |            .and_then(|e| e.row(0))
12759|       |            .map(|r| r.max_height)
12760|       |            .unwrap_or(0.0);
12761|       |        assert!(height > renderer.row_height_fallback() + 0.5);
12762|       |    }

12872|       |    #[test]
12873|       |    fn test_list_marker_info_any_indent_variants() {
12874|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("").is_none());
12875|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("   ").is_none());
12876|       |
12877|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12878|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(" 1. item").expect("marker");
12879|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12880|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 1);
12881|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12882|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 1);
12883|       |
12884|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12885|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("2)\titem").expect("marker");
12886|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12887|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 2);
12888|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12889|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 0);
12890|       |    }

12905|       |    #[test]
12906|       |    fn test_collect_until_tag_end_breaks_and_code() {
12907|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12908|       |        let events = vec![
12909|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
12910|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
12911|       |            Event::Code("beta".into()),
12912|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
12913|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
12914|       |            Event::HardBreak,
12915|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
12916|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
12917|       |        ];
12918|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12919|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12920|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta\n\n\n");
12921|       |
12922|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, false);
12923|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12924|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta   ");
12925|       |
12926|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into()), Event::SoftBreak];
12927|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12928|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12929|       |        assert_eq!(text, "tail\n");
12930|       |    }

12932|       |    #[test]
12933|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_link_image_and_unclosed() {
12934|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12935|       |        let events = vec![
12936|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
12937|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12938|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12939|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12940|       |                "".into(),
12941|       |            )),
12942|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12943|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12944|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12945|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12946|       |                "".into(),
12947|       |            )),
12948|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
12949|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12950|       |                "img.png".into(),
12951|       |                "Title".into(),
12952|       |            )),
12953|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
12954|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(
12955|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12956|       |                "img.png".into(),
12957|       |                "Title".into(),
12958|       |            )),
12959|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12960|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
12961|       |        ];
12962|       |        let (spans, next) =
12963|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12964|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12965|       |        assert!(spans
12966|       |            .iter()
12967|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
12968|       |        assert!(spans
12969|       |            .iter()
12970|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
12971|       |
12972|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into())];
12973|       |        let (spans, next) =
12974|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12975|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12976|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans), "tail");
12977|       |    }

13093|       |    fn test_collect_blockquotes_unclosed_variants() -> Result<()> {
13094|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13095|       |        let events = vec![
13096|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
13097|       |            Event::Text("plain".into()),
13098|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
13099|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
13100|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
13101|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
13102|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
13103|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
13104|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
13105|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
13106|       |                LinkType::Inline,
13107|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
13108|       |                "".into(),
13109|       |            )),
13110|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
13111|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
13112|       |                LinkType::Inline,
13113|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
13114|       |                "".into(),
13115|       |            )),
13116|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13117|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
13118|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13119|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
13120|       |        ];
13121|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
13122|       |        let (quotes, next) = renderer.collect_blockquotes(&events, 1, 1, &mut slugs);
13123|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13124|       |        let mut ids = Vec::new();
13125|       |        for (_, blocks) in &quotes {
13126|       |            for block in blocks {
13127|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = block {
13128|       |                    ids.push(id.clone());
13129|       |                }
13130|       |            }
13131|       |        }
13132|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2);
13133|       |        assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1]);
13134|       |        Ok(())
13135|       |    }

13166|       |    #[test]
13167|       |    fn test_render_inline_span_image_scale_and_title() {
13168|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13169|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13170|       |        let input = screen_input(40.0, 240.0);
13171|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13172|       |            let color_image = egui::ColorImage::new([200, 120], Color32::WHITE);
13173|       |            let tex =
13174|       |                ui.ctx()
13175|       |                    .load_texture("img:test.png", color_image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR);
13176|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
13177|       |                "test.png".to_string(),
13178|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
13179|       |                    texture: tex,
13180|       |                    size: [200, 120],
13181|       |                    modified: None,
13182|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([200, 120]),
13183|       |                    degraded: false,
13184|       |                },
13185|       |            );
13186|       |            let span = InlineSpan::Image {
13187|       |                src: "test.png".to_string(),
13188|       |                alt: "Alt".to_string(),
13189|       |                title: Some("Title".to_string()),
13190|       |            };
13191|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
13192|       |        });
13193|       |    }

13196|       |    fn test_paint_table_text_job_triggers_anchor_on_click() {
13197|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13198|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
13199|       |            text: "Jump".to_string(),
13200|       |            url: "#Section-One".to_string(),
13201|       |        }];
13202|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13203|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
13204|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
13205|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
13206|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13207|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13208|       |                |ui| {
13209|       |                    let build =
13210|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13211|       |                    let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
13212|       |                    let (rect, _response) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
13213|       |                        egui::vec2(120.0, galley.size().y),
13214|       |                        egui::Sense::click(),
13215|       |                    );
13216|       |                    let text_origin =
13217|       |                        MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
13218|       |                    click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
13219|       |                },
13220|       |            );
13221|       |        });
13222|       |
13223|       |        let input = input_with_click(click_pos.get(), egui::PointerButton::Primary);
13224|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13225|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13226|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13227|       |                |ui| {
13228|       |                    let build =
13229|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13230|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
13231|       |                },
13232|       |            );
13233|       |        });
13234|       |
13235|       |        assert_eq!(
13236|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
13237|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
13238|       |        );
13239|       |    }

13373|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();

13375|       |        assert_eq!(empty, vec![120.0]);
13376|       |

13379|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);

13392|       |    // -- Stage 1: content-driven natural width tests ----------------------
13393|       |
13394|       |    #[test]
13395|       |    fn test_natural_widths_two_short_columns() {
13396|       |        // Two columns with content wider than policy ? content drives widths
13397|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13398|       |        let headers = vec![
13399|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".to_string())],
13400|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Value".to_string())],
13401|       |        ];
13402|       |        let stats = vec![
13403|       |            ColumnStat {
13404|       |                max_graphemes: 20,
13405|       |                ..Default::default()
13406|       |            },
13407|       |            ColumnStat {
13408|       |                max_graphemes: 18,
13409|       |                ..Default::default()
13410|       |            },
13411|       |        ];
13412|       |        // Small resolved widths so content should dominate
13413|       |        let resolved = vec![60.0, 60.0];
13414|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 2);
13415|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
13416|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=14*0.62=8.68, cell_padding=14*1.8=25.2
13417|       |        // col0: max(4,20)=20 ? 20*8.68+25.2 = 198.8 ? clamp(198.8, 56, 490) = 198.8
13418|       |        // col1: max(5,18)=18 ? 18*8.68+25.2 = 181.44 ? clamp(181.44, 56, 490) = 181.44
13419|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 150.0, "col 0 = {} should be > 150", widths[0]);
13420|       |        assert!(widths[1] > 150.0, "col 1 = {} should be > 150", widths[1]);
13421|       |    }
13422|       |
13423|       |    #[test]
13424|       |    fn test_natural_widths_uses_max_of_content_and_policy() {
13425|       |        // Short content (3 graphemes), policy width 98 should win
13426|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13427|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("ID".to_string())]];
13428|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13429|       |            max_graphemes: 3,
13430|       |            ..Default::default()
13431|       |        }];
13432|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=8.68, cell_padding=25.2
13433|       |        // header "ID" ? width 2; max(2,3)=3 ? 3*8.68+25.2 = 51.24
13434|       |        // policy = 98.0 ? max(51.24, 98.0) = 98.0
13435|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0];
13436|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 1);
13437|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 1);
13438|       |        assert!(
13439|       |            (widths[0] - 98.0).abs() < 0.1,
13440|       |            "policy should win: got {}",
13441|       |            widths[0]
13442|       |        );
13443|       |    }
13444|       |
13445|       |    #[test]
13446|       |    fn test_natural_widths_clamped_to_bounds() {
13447|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13448|       |        // body=14.0 ? min_col_width=56.0, max_col_width=490.0
13449|       |        let min_col = 14.0 * 4.0; // 56
13450|       |        let max_col = 14.0 * 35.0; // 490
13451|       |
13452|       |        // Tiny content: 1 grapheme ? 1*8.68+25.2 = 33.88, policy=10 ? clamp to min
13453|       |        let headers_tiny = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("X".to_string())]];
13454|       |        let stats_tiny = vec![ColumnStat {
13455|       |            max_graphemes: 1,
13456|       |            ..Default::default()
13457|       |        }];
13458|       |        let resolved_tiny = vec![10.0];
13459|       |        let widths_tiny =
13460|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_tiny, &headers_tiny, &resolved_tiny, 1);
13461|       |        assert!(
13462|       |            (widths_tiny[0] - min_col).abs() < 0.1,
13463|       |            "tiny should clamp to min {}: got {}",
13464|       |            min_col,
13465|       |            widths_tiny[0]
13466|       |        );
13467|       |
13468|       |        // Huge content: 200 graphemes ? 200*8.68+25.2 = 1761.2 ? clamp to max
13469|       |        let headers_huge = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Description".to_string())]];
13470|       |        let stats_huge = vec![ColumnStat {
13471|       |            max_graphemes: 200,
13472|       |            ..Default::default()
13473|       |        }];
13474|       |        let resolved_huge = vec![10.0];
13475|       |        let widths_huge =
13476|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_huge, &headers_huge, &resolved_huge, 1);
13477|       |        assert!(
13478|       |            (widths_huge[0] - max_col).abs() < 0.1,
13479|       |            "huge should clamp to max {}: got {}",
13480|       |            max_col,
13481|       |            widths_huge[0]
13482|       |        );
13483|       |    }

13485|       |    #[test]
13486|       |    fn test_natural_table_width_sums_correctly() {
13487|       |        // Empty input
13488|       |        let empty = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[], 10.0);
13489|       |        assert!((empty - 0.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13490|       |
13491|       |        // Single column ? no spacing
13492|       |        let single = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[120.0], 10.0);
13493|       |        assert!((single - 120.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13494|       |
13495|       |        // Three columns: 100+200+150 = 450, spacing = 10*2 = 20, total = 470
13496|       |        let three = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[100.0, 200.0, 150.0], 10.0);
13497|       |        assert!((three - 470.0).abs() < 0.1, "expected 470, got {}", three);
13498|       |    }

13551|       |    // Test: column_count exceeds headers/stats/resolved_widths length
13552|       |    #[test]
13553|       |    fn test_natural_widths_handles_mismatched_lengths() {
13554|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13555|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // only 1 header
13556|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13557|       |            max_graphemes: 10,
13558|       |            longest_word: 5,
13559|       |            ..Default::default()
13560|       |        }]; // only 1 stat
13561|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0]; // only 1 width
13562|       |                                   // column_count = 3, exceeds all inputs
13563|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 3);
13564|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
13565|       |        // First column should be content/policy-driven
13566|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 50.0);
13567|       |        // Remaining columns should be clamped to MIN (body * 4.0 = 56.0)
13568|       |        let min_col = renderer.font_sizes.body * 4.0;
13569|       |        assert!(
13570|       |            (widths[1] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13571|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13572|       |            widths[1]
13573|       |        );
13574|       |        assert!(
13575|       |            (widths[2] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13576|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13577|       |            widths[2]
13578|       |        );
13579|       |    }

13581|       |    // Test: CJK characters in header get double-width estimate
13582|       |    #[test]
13583|       |    fn test_natural_widths_cjk_header_gets_double_width() {
13584|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13585|       |        let headers_ascii = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // 4 display width
13586|       |        let headers_cjk = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
13587|       |            "\u{540D}\u{524D}\u{5217}\u{5E45}".into(),
13588|       |        )]]; // 4 CJK chars = 8 display width
13589|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat::default()]; // no content
13590|       |        let resolved = vec![0.0]; // no policy
13591|       |        let widths_ascii =
13592|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_ascii, &resolved, 1);
13593|       |        let widths_cjk =
13594|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_cjk, &resolved, 1);
13595|       |        // CJK header should produce a wider natural width than ASCII header
13596|       |        assert!(
13597|       |            widths_cjk[0] > widths_ascii[0],
13598|       |            "CJK width {} should exceed ASCII width {}",
13599|       |            widths_cjk[0],
13600|       |            widths_ascii[0]
13601|       |        );
13602|       |    }

13604|       |    #[test]
13605|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code_nested_lists() {
13606|       |        let md_parent = "\
13607|       | - Parent
13608|       |   | Col | Notes |
13609|       |  | --- | --- |
13610|       |  | code | `a|b` |
13611|       |";
13612|       |        let escaped_parent = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_parent);
13613|       |        assert!(escaped_parent.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13614|       |
13615|       |        let md_nested = "\
13616|       |- Parent
13617|       |    - | Col | Notes |
13618|       |      | --- | --- |
13619|       |      | code | `a|b` |
13620|       |";
13621|       |        let escaped_nested = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_nested);
13622|       |        assert!(escaped_nested.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13623|       |    }

13625|       |    #[test]
13626|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_list_marker_header() {
13627|       |        let md = "\
13628|       |- Parent
13629|       |    - | H | I |
13630|       |      | --- | --- |
13631|       |      | 1 | 2 |";
13632|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13633|       |        assert_eq!(escaped, md);
13634|       |    }
13635|       |
13636|       |    #[test]
13637|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_list_marker_any_indent() {
13638|       |        let md = "\
13639|       |- Parent
13640|       |    - | H | I |
13641|       |      | --- | --- |
13642|       |      | `a|b` | c |
13643|       |";
13644|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13645|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13646|       |    }

13649|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_marker_within_range() {
13650|       |        let md = "\
13651|       |- Parent
13652|       |    - | H | I |
13653|       |      | --- | --- |
13654|       |      | `a|b` | c |
13655|       |";
13656|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13657|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13658|       |        let _parent_indent =
13659|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13660|       |        let _leading_spaces =
13661|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(rest).map(|(_, _, _, spaces)| spaces);
13662|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13663|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13664|       |    }

13669|       |- Parent
13670|       |  continuation
13671|       |  | H | I |
13672|       |  | --- | --- |
13673|       |  | `a|b` | c |
13674|       |";
13675|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13676|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13677|       |        let parent_indent =
13678|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13679|       |        let strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent);
13680|       |        let code_strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent + 4);
13681|       |        assert!(strip.is_some());
13682|       |        assert!(code_strip.is_none());
13683|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13684|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13685|       |    }

13805|       |    #[test]
13806|       |    fn test_parse_table_unclosed_and_noise() -> Result<()> {
13807|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13808|       |        let events = vec![
13809|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
13810|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
13811|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13812|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13813|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
13814|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13815|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13816|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
13817|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13818|       |            Event::Text("noise".into()),
13819|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13820|       |            Event::Text("R".into()),
13821|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13822|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13823|       |            Event::Text("after".into()),
13824|       |        ];
13825|       |        let (headers, rows, next) = renderer.parse_table(&events, 1);
13826|       |        assert_eq!(headers.len(), 1);
13827|       |        assert_eq!(rows.len(), 1);
13828|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13829|       |        Ok(())
13830|       |    }

13908|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
13909|       |                "rust".into(),
13910|       |            ))),
13911|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}".into()),
13912|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
13913|       |        ];

13915|       |        assert_eq!(language.as_deref(), Some("rust"));
13916|       |        assert_eq!(code, "fn main() {}");
13917|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13918|       |        Ok(())
13919|       |    }

13951|       |    #[test]
13952|       |    fn test_render_table_tablebuilder_scaled_down_adjustments() {
13953|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13954|       |        let headers = vec![
13955|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
13956|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
13957|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("C".to_string())],
13958|       |        ];
13959|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
13960|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row1".to_string())],
13961|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row2".to_string())],
13962|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row3".to_string())],
13963|       |        ]];
13964|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13965|       |        let widths = [220.0, 240.0];
13966|       |        for (idx, width) in widths.iter().enumerate() {
13967|       |            let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![
13968|       |                ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
13969|       |                    min: 40.0,
13970|       |                    preferred: 200.0,
13971|       |                    clip: false,
13972|       |                },
13973|       |                ColumnPolicy::Auto,
13974|       |                ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false },
13975|       |            ]);
13976|       |            let input = screen_input(*width, 120.0);
13977|       |            run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13978|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 2.0;
13979|       |                let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 90 + idx as u64);
13980|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
13981|       |            });
13982|       |        }
13983|       |    }

14158|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("H".to_string())]];
14159|       |        let rows: Vec<Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>>> = (0..(COLUMN_STATS_SAMPLE_ROWS + 1))
14160|       |            .map(|idx| vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(format!("R{idx}"))]])
14161|       |            .collect();
14162|       |        let hash = renderer.compute_table_content_hash(&headers, &rows, &[]);

14166|       |    #[test]
14167|       |    fn test_table_width_helpers_empty_inputs() {
14168|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14169|       |        let table_id = 100u64;
14170|       |        let mut specs: Vec<ColumnSpec> = Vec::new();
14171|       |        renderer.apply_persisted_widths(table_id, &mut specs);
14172|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &[], &[]);
14173|       |        let change = renderer.record_resolved_widths(table_id, 0, &[]);
14174|       |        assert!(matches!(change, WidthChange::None));
14175|       |        assert!(renderer
14176|       |            .resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &[], 10.0)
14177|       |            .is_empty());
14178|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &[], 6.0), 0.0);
14179|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14180|       |            let style = ui.style().clone();
14181|       |            let row = vec![vec![]];
14182|       |            let height =
14183|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
14184|       |            assert_eq!(height, 12.0);
14185|       |            let row = vec![
14186|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("a".to_string())],
14187|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("b".to_string())],
14188|       |            ];
14189|       |            let height =
14190|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
14191|       |            assert!(height >= 12.0);
14192|       |            let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0));
14193|       |            renderer.paint_table_dividers(
14194|       |                ui.painter(),
14195|       |                ui.visuals(),
14196|       |                rect,
14197|       |                rect,
14198|       |                &[],
14199|       |                0.0,
14200|       |                0.0,
14201|       |                0.0,
14202|       |                true,
14203|       |            );
14204|       |        });
14205|       |    }

14208|       |    fn test_resolve_table_column_widths_ignores_invalid_stored_widths() {
14209|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14210|       |        let table_id = 101u64;
14211|       |        {
14212|       |            let mut metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow_mut();
14213|       |            let entry = metrics.entry_mut(table_id);
14214|       |            entry.resolved_widths = vec![f32::NAN, -5.0];
14215|       |        }
14216|       |        let specs = vec![
14217|       |            resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 40.0),
14218|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "B", ColumnPolicy::Auto, None),
14219|       |        ];
14220|       |        let widths = renderer.resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &specs, 30.0);
14221|       |        assert_eq!(widths, vec![40.0, 30.0]);

14225|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_stores_new_width() {
14226|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14227|       |        let table_id = 200u64;
14228|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
14229|       |        let widths = vec![140.0f32];
14230|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
14231|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
14232|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
14233|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(specs[0].policy_hash), Some(140.0));
14234|       |    }

14237|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_skips_small_delta() {
14238|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14239|       |        let table_id = 201u64;
14240|       |        let spec = resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0);
14241|       |        let hash = spec.policy_hash;
14242|       |        let specs = vec![spec];
14243|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.0]);
14244|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.2]);
14245|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
14246|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
14247|       |        let stored = entry.persisted_width(hash).unwrap_or_default();
14248|       |        assert!((stored - 120.0).abs() < 0.01);
14249|       |    }

14413|       |    #[test]
14414|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_outside_text_rect() {
14415|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14416|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14417|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14418|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
14419|       |        }];
14420|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14421|       |        let outside_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14422|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14423|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14424|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14425|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14426|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14427|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14428|       |            let text_origin =
14429|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14430|       |            let text_rect = galley.rect.translate(text_origin.to_vec2());
14431|       |            let pos = egui::pos2(rect.right() - 2.0, text_rect.center().y);
14432|       |            assert!(pos.x > text_rect.right());
14433|       |            outside_pos.set(pos);
14434|       |        });
14435|       |
14436|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14437|       |        input
14438|       |            .events
14439|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(outside_pos.get()));
14440|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14441|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14442|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14443|       |            let (rect, response) =
14444|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14445|       |            let text_origin =
14446|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14447|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14448|       |            assert!(link.is_none());
14449|       |        });
14450|       |    }
14451|       |
14452|       |    #[test]
14453|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_inside_text_rect_returns_link() {
14454|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14455|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14456|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14457|       |            url: "#anchor".to_string(),
14458|       |        }];
14459|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14460|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14461|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14462|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14463|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14464|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14465|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14466|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14467|       |            let text_origin =
14468|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14469|       |            click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
14470|       |        });
14471|       |
14472|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14473|       |        input
14474|       |            .events
14475|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(click_pos.get()));
14476|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14477|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14478|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14479|       |            let (rect, response) =
14480|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14481|       |            let text_origin =
14482|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14483|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14484|       |            assert!(link.is_some());
14485|       |        });
14486|       |    }
