14489|       |    fn test_element_plain_text_list_quote_table_spacing() {
14490|       |        let list = MarkdownElement::List {
14491|       |            ordered: false,
14492|       |            items: vec![ListItem {
14493|       |                blocks: vec![
14494|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("One".to_string())]),
14495|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Two".to_string())]),
14496|       |                ],
14497|       |            }],
14498|       |        };
14499|       |        let list_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&list);
14500|       |        assert!(list_text.contains('\n'));
14501|       |
14502|       |        let quote = MarkdownElement::Quote {
14503|       |            depth: 1,
14504|       |            blocks: vec![
14505|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Alpha".to_string())]),
14506|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Beta".to_string())]),
14507|       |            ],
14508|       |        };
14509|       |        let quote_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&quote);
14510|       |        assert!(quote_text.contains('\n'));
14511|       |
14512|       |        let table = MarkdownElement::Table {
14513|       |            headers: vec![
14514|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("H1".to_string())],
14515|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("H2".to_string())],
14516|       |            ],
14517|       |            rows: vec![vec![
14518|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
14519|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
14520|       |            ]],
14521|       |            alignments: vec![],
14522|       |        };
14523|       |        let table_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&table);
14524|       |        assert_eq!(table_text, "H1 H2 A B");
14525|       |    }
14526|       |
14527|       |    #[test]
14528|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_data_uri_decodes_and_remote_rejects() {
14529|       |        use base64::Engine;
14530|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14531|       |
14532|       |        // resolve_image_path still passes data URIs through unchanged
14533|       |        let data_uri_raw = "data:image/png;base64,AAAA";
14534|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(data_uri_raw), data_uri_raw);
14535|       |
14536|       |        // Build a valid 1x1 PNG data URI for the texture-loading test
14537|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14538|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14539|       |        let valid_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14540|       |
14541|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14542|       |            // A valid data URI should now decode and return a texture
14543|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &valid_data_uri);
14544|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
14545|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
14546|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
14547|       |
14548|       |            // Remote images without allow_remote should still be rejected
14549|       |            assert!(renderer
14550|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/image.png")
14551|       |                .is_none());
14552|       |            assert!(renderer
14553|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "http://example.com/image.png")
14554|       |                .is_none());
14555|       |        });
14556|       |    }

14565|       |    fn test_table_line_info_variants_cover_edges() {
14566|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info("text");
14567|       |        assert_eq!(level, 0);
14568|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "text");
14569|       |
14570|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info("   text");
14571|       |        assert_eq!(level, 0);
14572|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "   text");
14573|       |
14574|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(">abc");
14575|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14576|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "abc");
14577|       |
14578|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(" >\tabc");
14579|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14580|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "abc");
14581|       |
14582|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(">");
14583|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14584|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "");
14585|       |    }

14628|       |    fn test_append_text_sections_preserves_color_when_highlighted() {
14629|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14630|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
14631|       |        let mut job = LayoutJob::default();
14632|       |        let mut plain_text = String::new();
14633|       |        let inline_style = InlineStyle {
14634|       |            strong: false,
14635|       |            italics: false,
14636|       |            strike: false,
14637|       |            color: Some(Color32::from_rgb(10, 20, 30)),
14638|       |        };
14639|       |        let count = renderer.append_text_sections(
14640|       |            &style,
14641|       |            &mut job,
14642|       |            &mut plain_text,
14643|       |            "hit",
14644|       |            renderer.font_sizes.body,
14645|       |            inline_style,
14646|       |            Some("hit"),
14647|       |        );
14648|       |        assert_eq!(count, 3);
14649|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "hit");
14650|       |    }

14677|       |                std::env::var("MDMDVIEW_TEST_ENV").ok().as_deref(),
14678|       |                Some("after")

14699|       |    #[test]
14700|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_link_and_image() {
14701|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14702|       |        let events = vec![
14703|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
14704|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
14705|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14706|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
14707|       |                "".into(),
14708|       |            )),
14709|       |            Event::Text("Link".into()),
14710|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14711|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
14712|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14713|       |                "img.png".into(),
14714|       |                "Title".into(),
14715|       |            )),
14716|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
14717|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14718|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
14719|       |        ];
14720|       |        let (spans, next) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
14721|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14722|       |        assert!(spans
14723|       |            .iter()
14724|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
14725|       |        assert!(spans
14726|       |            .iter()
14727|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
14728|       |    }

14763|       |    fn test_parse_list_item_heading_builds_header() -> Result<()> {
14764|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14765|       |        let events = vec![
14766|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
14767|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
14768|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
14769|       |            Event::Text("Title".into()),
14770|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
14771|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
14772|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
14773|       |        ];
14774|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
14775|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
14776|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14777|       |        assert!(matches!(items[0].blocks[0], MarkdownElement::Header { .. }));
14778|       |        Ok(())
14779|       |    }

14781|       |    #[test]
14782|       |    fn test_parse_list_inline_formats_without_paragraph() -> Result<()> {
14783|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14784|       |        let events = vec![
14785|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
14786|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
14787|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
14788|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
14789|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
14790|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
14791|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
14792|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
14793|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
14794|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14795|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
14796|       |                "".into(),
14797|       |            )),
14798|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
14799|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14800|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
14801|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14802|       |                "img.png".into(),
14803|       |                "Title".into(),
14804|       |            )),
14805|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
14806|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14807|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
14808|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
14809|       |        ];
14810|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
14811|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
14812|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14813|       |        assert!(matches!(items[0].blocks[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
14814|       |        let dummy = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
14815|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
14816|       |        for element in [&items[0].blocks[0], &dummy] {
14817|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
14818|       |                assert!(spans
14819|       |                    .iter()
14820|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
14821|       |                assert!(spans
14822|       |                    .iter()
14823|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
14824|       |                assert!(spans
14825|       |                    .iter()
14826|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
14827|       |                assert!(spans
14828|       |                    .iter()
14829|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
14830|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
14831|       |            }
14832|       |        }
14833|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
14834|       |        Ok(())
14835|       |    }

14837|       |    #[test]
14838|       |    fn test_parse_table_parses_header_and_rows() -> Result<()> {
14839|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14840|       |        let alignments = vec![Alignment::Left];
14841|       |        let events = vec![
14842|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(alignments.clone())),
14843|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
14844|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
14845|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
14846|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
14847|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
14848|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
14849|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
14850|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
14851|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
14852|       |            Event::Text("A".into()),
14853|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
14854|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
14855|       |            Event::End(Tag::Table(alignments)),
14856|       |        ];
14857|       |        let (headers, rows, next) = renderer.parse_table(&events, 1);
14858|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14859|       |        assert_eq!(headers.len(), 1);
14860|       |        assert_eq!(rows.len(), 1);
14861|       |        Ok(())
14862|       |    }

14875|       |    fn test_clear_image_failure_cache_and_texture_cache_stats() {
14876|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14877|       |        renderer.note_image_failure("failed.png");
14878|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
14879|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, "\u{1f600}");
14880|       |            let image = ctx.load_texture(
14881|       |                "image-cache-stats",
14882|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
14883|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14884|       |            );
14885|       |            renderer.store_image_texture("stats.png", image, [2, 2], None);
14886|       |        });
14887|       |
14888|       |        let (emoji_entries, image_entries) = renderer.texture_cache_stats();
14889|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_entries, 1);
14890|       |        assert_eq!(image_entries, 1);
14891|       |
14892|       |        renderer.clear_image_failure_cache();
14893|       |        assert!(renderer.image_failures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14894|       |    }

14916|       |    #[test]
14917|       |    fn test_render_image_info_popup_shows_kb_size_and_degraded_cache() -> Result<()> {
14918|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14919|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
14920|       |        let path = temp.path().join("popup.png");
14921|       |        std::fs::write(&path, vec![0_u8; 2048])?;
14922|       |        let resolved = path.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
14923|       |
14924|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
14925|       |            let texture = ctx.load_texture(
14926|       |                "popup-kb",
14927|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
14928|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14929|       |            );
14930|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
14931|       |                resolved.clone(),
14932|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
14933|       |                    texture,
14934|       |                    size: [64, 32],
14935|       |                    modified: None,
14936|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([64, 32]),
14937|       |                    degraded: true,
14938|       |                },
14939|       |            );
14940|       |            renderer.render_image_info_popup(ui, "popup.png", &resolved, 64, 32);
14941|       |        });
14942|       |
14943|       |        Ok(())
14944|       |    }
14945|       |

15026|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15027|       |        let md = "Text with ?? emoji and **bold** and *italic* and `code` inline.\n";
15028|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
15029|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15030|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
15031|       |        });
15032|       |    }
15033|       |
15034|       |    /// Verify that U+FE0F (Variation Selector 16) is stripped from non-catalog
15035|       |    /// emoji before they reach the text buffer. U+1F6E0 (HAMMER AND WRENCH) +
15036|       |    /// U+FE0F is NOT in the 80-emoji Twemoji catalog, so it falls through to
15037|       |    /// text rendering. Without the fix, FE0F would appear as tofu in egui.
15038|       |    #[test]
15039|       |    fn test_render_text_with_emojis_strips_vs16() {
15040|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15041|       |        // U+1F6E0 U+FE0F = ???  — not in catalog, should strip FE0F
15042|       |        let input = "before \u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F} after";
15043|       |
15044|       |        // Confirm the emoji is NOT in our catalog (prerequisite for the fix)
15045|       |        assert!(
15046|       |            renderer
15047|       |                .emoji_key_for_grapheme("\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}")
15048|       |                .is_none(),
15049|       |            "test assumes U+1F6E0+FE0F is not in emoji catalog"
15050|       |        );
15051|       |
15052|       |        // The stripping logic is inline in render_text_with_emojis, so we
15053|       |        // verify it directly: for a non-catalog grapheme containing FE0F,
15054|       |        // the chars().filter() path should remove it.
15055|       |        let g = "\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}";
15056|       |        assert!(g.contains('\u{FE0F}'));
15057|       |        let clean: String = g.chars().filter(|&c| c != '\u{FE0F}').collect();
15058|       |        assert!(!clean.contains('\u{FE0F}'), "FE0F should be stripped");
15059|       |        assert!(clean.contains('\u{1F6E0}'), "base emoji should remain");
15060|       |
15061|       |        // Smoke test: render_text_with_emojis should not panic with VS16 input
15062|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15063|       |            renderer.render_text_with_emojis(ui, input, 14.0, InlineStyle::default());
15064|       |        });
15065|       |    }
15066|       |
15067|       |    #[test]

15087|       |    #[test]
15088|       |    fn test_highlight_code_light_theme() {
15089|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15090|       |
15091|       |        // Parse with dark theme
15092|       |        let code = "let x = 42;";
15093|       |        let dark_tokens = renderer
15094|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
15095|       |            .expect("dark highlight");
15096|       |
15097|       |        // Switch to light theme and re-highlight
15098|       |        renderer.set_dark_mode(false);
15099|       |        let light_tokens = renderer
15100|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
15101|       |            .expect("light highlight");
15102|       |
15103|       |        // The two themes should produce different token colors
15104|       |        assert!(!dark_tokens.is_empty());
15105|       |        assert!(!light_tokens.is_empty());
15106|       |        // At least one token color should differ between themes
15107|       |        let dark_first_color = dark_tokens[0][0].color;
15108|       |        let light_first_color = light_tokens[0][0].color;
15109|       |        assert_ne!(dark_first_color, light_first_color);
15110|       |    }

15112|       |    #[test]
15113|       |    fn test_release_gpu_textures_clears_all_caches() {
15114|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15115|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
15116|       |            // Populate emoji_textures cache
15117|       |            let emoji_tex = ctx.load_texture(
15118|       |                "test-emoji",
15119|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::RED),
15120|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
15121|       |            );
15122|       |            renderer
15123|       |                .emoji_textures
15124|       |                .borrow_mut()
15125|       |                .insert("rocket".to_string(), emoji_tex);
15126|       |
15127|       |            // Populate image_textures cache
15128|       |            let img_tex = ctx.load_texture(
15129|       |                "test-image",
15130|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::BLUE),
15131|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
15132|       |            );
15133|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
15134|       |                "test.png".to_string(),
15135|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
15136|       |                    texture: img_tex,
15137|       |                    size: [64, 32],
15138|       |                    modified: None,
15139|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([64, 32]),
15140|       |                    degraded: false,
15141|       |                },
15142|       |            );
15143|       |
15144|       |            // Verify caches are populated
15145|       |            assert!(!renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
15146|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
15147|       |
15148|       |            // Call release_gpu_textures
15149|       |            renderer.release_gpu_textures();
15150|       |
15151|       |            // Assert all texture caches are empty
15152|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().len(), 0);
15153|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.len(), 0);
15154|       |        });
15155|       |    }

15157|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
15158|       |    // decode_data_uri() tests
15159|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
15160|       |
15161|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal 1x1 PNG in memory and return the raw bytes.
15162|       |    fn make_1x1_png() -> Vec<u8> {
15163|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
15164|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut buf);
15165|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGBA
15166|       |        encoder
15167|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgba8)
15168|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 PNG");
15169|       |        buf
15170|       |    }
15171|       |
15172|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal JPEG in memory using the `image` crate.
15173|       |    fn make_1x1_jpeg() -> Vec<u8> {
15174|       |        use image::codecs::jpeg::JpegEncoder;
15175|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
15176|       |        let encoder = JpegEncoder::new(&mut buf);
15177|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGB (JPEG doesn't support alpha)
15178|       |        encoder
15179|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgb8)
15180|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 JPEG");
15181|       |        buf
15182|       |    }
15183|       |
15184|       |    #[test]
15185|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_valid_png() {
15186|       |        use base64::Engine;
15187|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15188|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15189|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15190|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15191|       |        assert!(decoded.is_some(), "valid PNG data URI should decode");
15192|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15193|       |    }
15194|       |

15274|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_charset_parameter_present() {

15277|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15278|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;charset=utf-8;base64,{b64}");
15279|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15280|       |        assert!(
15281|       |            decoded.is_some(),

15287|       |    #[test]
15288|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_whitespace_in_payload() {
15289|       |        use base64::Engine;
15290|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15291|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15292|       |        // Inject newlines into the base64 payload
15293|       |        let b64_with_newlines: String = b64
15294|       |            .chars()
15295|       |            .enumerate()
15296|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
15297|       |                if i > 0 && i % 10 == 0 {
15298|       |                    vec!['\n', c]
15299|       |                } else {
15300|       |                    vec![c]
15301|       |                }
15302|       |            })
15303|       |            .collect();
15304|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_newlines}");
15305|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15306|       |        assert!(
15307|       |            decoded.is_some(),
15308|       |            "whitespace in payload should be stripped before decoding"
15309|       |        );
15310|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15311|       |    }
15312|       |
15313|       |    #[test]
15314|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_tabs_in_payload() {
15315|       |        use base64::Engine;
15316|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15317|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15318|       |        // Inject tabs and spaces into the base64 payload
15319|       |        let b64_with_tabs: String = b64
15320|       |            .chars()
15321|       |            .enumerate()
15322|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
15323|       |                if i > 0 && i % 8 == 0 {
15324|       |                    vec!['\t', ' ', c]
15325|       |                } else {
15326|       |                    vec![c]
15327|       |                }
15328|       |            })
15329|       |            .collect();
15330|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_tabs}");
15331|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15332|       |        assert!(
15333|       |            decoded.is_some(),
15334|       |            "tabs and spaces in payload should be stripped before decoding"
15335|       |        );
15336|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15337|       |    }

15344|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_produces_texture() {
15345|       |        use base64::Engine;
15346|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15347|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15348|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15349|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15350|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15351|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15352|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
15353|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
15354|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
15355|       |        });
15356|       |    }

15359|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_invalid_returns_none() {
15360|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15361|       |        // "dGVzdA==" decodes to "test" — valid base64 but not a valid image
15362|       |        let data_uri = "data:image/png;base64,dGVzdA==";
15363|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(data_uri);
15364|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15365|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
15366|       |            assert!(loaded.is_none(), "garbage image bytes should return None");
15367|       |            // Failure should be cached under the hash key
15368|       |            assert!(
15369|       |                renderer.image_failures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
15370|       |                "failure should be cached under the hash key"
15371|       |            );
15372|       |        });
15373|       |    }

15375|       |    #[test]
15376|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_cached_on_second_call() {
15377|       |        use base64::Engine;
15378|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15379|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15380|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15381|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15382|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(&data_uri);
15383|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15384|       |            // First call: decodes and caches
15385|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15386|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "first call should produce a texture");
15387|       |
15388|       |            // Verify the hash key exists in image_textures
15389|       |            assert!(
15390|       |                renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
15391|       |                "hash key should exist in image_textures after first call"
15392|       |            );
15393|       |
15394|       |            // Second call: should hit cache and still return Some
15395|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15396|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "second call should return cached texture");
15397|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15398|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15399|       |            assert_eq!((w1, h1), (w2, h2), "dimensions should match between calls");
15400|       |        });
15401|       |    }
15402|       |

15414|       |    #[test]
15415|       |    fn test_data_uri_not_stale_after_cache() {
15416|       |        use base64::Engine;
15417|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15418|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15419|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15420|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15421|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15422|       |            // Load into cache
15423|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15424|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "initial load should succeed");
15425|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15426|       |
15427|       |            // Second call: served from cache (not re-decoded), same dimensions
15428|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15429|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "cached lookup should succeed");
15430|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15431|       |            assert_eq!(
15432|       |                (w1, h1),
15433|       |                (w2, h2),
15434|       |                "cached image should have same dimensions"
15435|       |            );
15436|       |        });
15437|       |    }

15439|       |    #[test]
15440|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_data_uri_truncated() {
15441|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15442|       |        // Use a data URI with invalid image bytes so the placeholder path is taken
15443|       |        let long_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1000));
15444|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15445|       |            src: long_data_uri.clone(),
15446|       |            alt: "".to_string(),
15447|       |            title: None,
15448|       |        };
15449|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15450|       |            // Ensure the data URI fails to load (garbage bytes)
15451|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &long_data_uri);
15452|       |            assert!(
15453|       |                loaded.is_none(),
15454|       |                "garbage data URI should not produce a texture"
15455|       |            );
15456|       |            // Render the span — this exercises the placeholder path
15457|       |            // The placeholder should show "Embedded image (data URI)" instead of the raw base64
15458|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15459|       |            // If we got here without hanging or crashing, the truncation is working.
15460|       |            // The placeholder text is rendered internally via ui.label() — we verify
15461|       |            // the code path is exercised rather than inspecting the rendered text.
15462|       |        });
15463|       |    }

15466|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_data_uri_uses_alt_text() {
15467|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15468|       |        let data_uri = "data:image/png;base64,dGVzdA==";
15469|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15470|       |            src: data_uri.to_string(),
15471|       |            alt: "My alt text".to_string(),
15472|       |            title: None,
15473|       |        };
15474|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {

15476|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
15477|       |            // Render — should use alt text, not "Embedded image (data URI)"
15478|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15479|       |        });

15482|       |    #[test]
15483|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_file_path_unchanged() {
15484|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15485|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15486|       |            src: "missing_file.png".to_string(),
15487|       |            alt: "".to_string(),
15488|       |            title: None,
15489|       |        };
15490|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15491|       |            // missing_file.png won't exist — triggers placeholder
15492|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15493|       |            // The placeholder for file paths should show the file path itself,
15494|       |            // not "Embedded image (data URI)". This test verifies the code path
15495|       |            // doesn't incorrectly truncate non-data-URI sources.
15496|       |        });

15504|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_deterministic() {
15505|       |        let uri = "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=";
15506|       |        let key1 = data_uri_cache_key(uri);
15507|       |        let key2 = data_uri_cache_key(uri);
15508|       |        assert_eq!(key1, key2, "same URI must produce same cache key");
15509|       |    }
15510|       |
15511|       |    #[test]
15512|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_different_uris_differ() {
15513|       |        let key1 = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,AAAA");
15514|       |        let key2 = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,BBBB");
15515|       |        assert_ne!(
15516|       |            key1, key2,
15517|       |            "different URIs must produce different cache keys"
15518|       |        );
15519|       |    }

15523|       |        let key = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,test");
15524|       |        assert!(
15525|       |            key.starts_with("data-uri:"),
15526|       |            "cache key must start with 'data-uri:' prefix, got: {key}"
15527|       |        );
15528|       |    }
15529|       |
15530|       |    #[test]
15531|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_fixed_length() {
15532|       |        let short_uri = "data:image/png;base64,AA==";
15533|       |        let long_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1_000_000));
15534|       |        let short_key = data_uri_cache_key(short_uri);
15535|       |        let long_key = data_uri_cache_key(&long_uri);
15536|       |        assert_eq!(
15537|       |            short_key.len(),
15538|       |            long_key.len(),
15539|       |            "cache key length must be fixed regardless of input size (short={}, long={})",
15540|       |            short_key.len(),
15541|       |            long_key.len()
15542|       |        );
15543|       |        // "data-uri:" (9 chars) + 16 hex digits = 25 chars
15544|       |        assert_eq!(
15545|       |            short_key.len(),
15546|       |            25,
15547|       |            "expected 25-char cache key, got {}",
15548|       |            short_key.len()
15549|       |        );
15550|       |    }

  554|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  555|       |#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
  556|       |enum MermaidEnqueueError {
  557|       |    QueueFull,
  558|       |    Disconnected,
  559|       |}
  560|       |
  561|       |/// Per-diagram debounce state for resize-aware rendering.
  562|       |/// Tracks the last-enqueued width bucket and the time when the width bucket last changed.
  563|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  564|       |struct WidthDebounce {
  565|       |    /// The width bucket for which a render was last successfully enqueued.
  566|       |    last_enqueued_bucket: u32,
  567|       |    /// The width bucket observed on the most recent frame.
  568|       |    /// Used to detect continuing resize (trailing-edge timer reset).
  569|       |    last_seen_bucket: u32,
  570|       |    /// When the width bucket last changed (i.e., last_seen_bucket was updated).
  571|       |    /// None if the current bucket matches last_enqueued_bucket (no resize in progress).
  572|       |    bucket_changed_at: Option<Instant>,
  573|       |}
  574|       |
  575|       |/// How long to wait after the last width bucket change before
  576|       |/// enqueueing a re-render. Prevents flooding workers during resize.
  577|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  578|       |const MERMAID_RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
  579|       |
  580|       |pub(crate) struct MermaidRenderer {
  581|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  582|       |    mermaid_textures: RefCell<TextureCache>,
  583|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  584|       |    mermaid_pending: RefCell<HashSet<String>>,
  585|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  586|       |    mermaid_frame_pending: Cell<bool>,
  587|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  588|       |    mermaid_svg_cache: RefCell<SvgCache>,
  589|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  590|       |    mermaid_errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  591|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  592|       |    mermaid_texture_errors: RefCell<LruCache<String, String>>,
  593|       |    /// Job sender wrapped in Option so Drop can take and drop it first,
  594|       |    /// closing the channel before joining worker threads.
  595|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  596|       |    mermaid_job_tx: Option<MermaidJobSender>,
  597|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  598|       |    mermaid_result_rx: MermaidResultReceiver,
  599|       |    /// Worker thread handles for cleanup on drop.
  600|       |    /// When MermaidRenderer is dropped, we join all worker threads to ensure
  601|       |    /// they exit cleanly and release memory (font database, QuickJS runtime).
  602|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  603|       |    worker_handles: RefCell<Vec<JoinHandle<()>>>,
  604|       |    /// Deferred worker spawn: holds channel ends until the first diagram is requested.
  605|       |    /// Avoids loading QuickJS + mermaid.min.js when no Mermaid diagrams are used.
  606|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  607|       |    deferred_spawn: RefCell<Option<(MermaidJobReceiver, MermaidResultSender)>>,
  608|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize-aware rendering.
  609|       |    /// Key: svg_key (u64). Tracks the last-enqueued width bucket
  610|       |    /// and the time when the width bucket last changed.
  611|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  612|       |    mermaid_width_debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, WidthDebounce>>,
  613|       |    /// Maps svg_key ? the texture_key of the most recently stored texture.
  614|       |    /// Used during resize debounce to find a stale-but-displayable texture.
  615|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  616|       |    mermaid_latest_texture: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  617|       |    /// The width bucket that was most recently *requested* for each svg_key.

  656|    477|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
  657|       |        #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  658|    477|        let (mermaid_job_tx, mermaid_job_rx): (MermaidJobSender, MermaidJobReceiver) =
  659|    477|            bounded(Self::MAX_MERMAID_JOBS * 4);

  666|    477|            #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  667|    477|            mermaid_textures: RefCell::new(TextureCache::new(
  668|    477|                Self::MERMAID_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY,
  669|    477|                Self::MERMAID_TEXTURE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES,
  670|    477|            )),

  679|    477|            )),
  680|    477|            #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]

  682|    477|            #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  683|    477|            mermaid_texture_errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(

  703|       |    /// Returns (texture_entries, texture_mb, texture_max_mb, svg_entries, svg_mb, svg_max_mb).

  705|     62|    pub(crate) fn cache_stats(&self) -> (usize, f32, f32, usize, f32, f32) {
  706|     62|        let tex = self.mermaid_textures.borrow();

  713|     62|            svg.current_bytes as f32 / (1024.0 * 1024.0),
  714|     62|            svg.max_bytes as f32 / (1024.0 * 1024.0),
  715|     62|        )
  716|     62|    }

  718|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage.
  719|       |    /// SVG cache is retained so diagrams can be quickly re-rasterized on restore.

  721|      4|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  722|      4|        self.mermaid_textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  723|       |        // mermaid_svg_cache intentionally retained for fast rebuild
  724|       |        // mermaid_errors intentionally retained to prevent retry storms
  725|       |        // mermaid_texture_errors intentionally retained
  726|      4|        self.mermaid_width_debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  727|      4|        self.mermaid_latest_texture.borrow_mut().clear();
  728|      4|        self.mermaid_wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  729|      4|    }
  730|       |

 1274|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 1275|      8|    fn mermaid_worker_count() -> usize {
 1276|       |        // One worker is sufficient: Mermaid diagrams render in the background and
 1277|       |        // typical documents have few diagrams. Each worker loads a QuickJS runtime
 1278|       |        // with mermaid.min.js (~30-50 MB), so multiple workers waste memory.
 1279|      8|        1
 1280|      8|    }

 1287|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 1288|      7|    fn spawn_mermaid_worker_thread(
 1289|      7|        worker_idx: usize,
 1290|      7|        worker_rx: MermaidJobReceiver,
 1291|      7|        worker_tx: MermaidResultSender,
 1292|      7|        worker_fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
 1293|      7|    ) -> std::io::Result<JoinHandle<()>> {
 1294|       |        #[cfg(test)]
 1295|      7|        if FORCE_MERMAID_THREAD_SPAWN_ERROR.swap(false, Ordering::Relaxed) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1295:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  ------------------
 1296|      1|            return Err(std::io::Error::other("forced mermaid spawn error"));
 1297|      6|        }
 1298|      6|        std::thread::Builder::new()
 1299|      6|            .name(format!("mdmdview-mermaid-{worker_idx}"))
 1300|      6|            .spawn(move || {
 1301|      6|                let mut worker = MermaidWorker::new(worker_idx, worker_fontdb).map_err(|e| {
                                                                                                         ^1
 1302|      1|                    eprintln!("Mermaid worker init failed: {}", e);
 1303|      1|                    e
 1304|      1|                });
 1305|      6|                for job in worker_rx.iter() {
 1306|      6|                    let payload = match &mut worker {
 1307|      5|                        Ok(state) => state.process_job(job),
 1308|      1|                        Err(err) => {
 1309|       |                            let MermaidRequest {
 1310|      1|                                svg_key,
 1311|      1|                                texture_key,
 1312|      1|                                svg,
 1313|       |                                ..
 1314|      1|                            } = job;
 1315|      1|                            MermaidResult {
 1316|      1|                                svg_key,
 1317|      1|                                texture_key,
 1318|      1|                                svg,
 1319|      1|                                rgba: None,
 1320|      1|                                size: None,
 1321|      1|                                error: Some(err.clone()),
 1322|      1|                            }
 1323|       |                        }
 1324|       |                    };
 1325|       |                    // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
 1326|       |                    // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
 1327|       |                    // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
 1328|       |                    // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable
 1329|       |                    // since we're shutting down anyway.
 1330|      6|                    let _ = worker_tx.try_send(payload);
 1331|       |                }
 1332|      6|            })
 1333|      7|    }
 1334|       |
 1335|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 1336|      7|    fn spawn_mermaid_workers(
 1337|      7|        job_rx: MermaidJobReceiver,
 1338|      7|        result_tx: MermaidResultSender,
 1339|      7|    ) -> Vec<JoinHandle<()>> {
 1340|      7|        let worker_count = Self::mermaid_worker_count();
 1341|      7|        let mut fontdb = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
 1342|      7|        fontdb.load_system_fonts();
 1343|      7|        let fontdb = Arc::new(fontdb);
 1344|      7|        let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(worker_count);
 1345|      7|        for worker_idx in 0..worker_count {
 1346|      7|            let worker_rx = job_rx.clone();
 1347|      7|            let worker_tx = result_tx.clone();
 1348|      7|            let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
 1349|      7|            match Self::spawn_mermaid_worker_thread(worker_idx, worker_rx, worker_tx, worker_fontdb)
 1350|       |            {
 1351|      6|                Ok(handle) => handles.push(handle),
 1352|      1|                Err(err) => eprintln!("Failed to start Mermaid worker thread: {}", err),
 1353|       |            }
 1354|       |        }
 1355|      7|        drop(job_rx);
 1356|      7|        handles
 1357|      7|    }

 1387|      9|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, svg_key: u64) -> bool {
 1388|      9|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
 1389|      9|        let wanted_bucket = self.mermaid_wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&svg_key).copied();

 1396|       |    #[cfg(any(test, feature = "mermaid-quickjs"))]
 1397|     18|    fn should_allow_html_labels(_code: &str) -> bool {
 1398|       |        // Always return false because usvg cannot render foreignObject elements.
 1399|       |        // When htmlLabels is true, mermaid renders labels inside <foreignObject>
 1400|       |        // which usvg ignores, resulting in invisible text.
 1401|       |        // By forcing htmlLabels=false, mermaid uses SVG <text> elements instead.
 1402|     18|        false
 1403|     18|    }

 1478|    504|    fn drop(&mut self) {

 1481|    504|        drop(self.mermaid_job_tx.take());

 2897|       |function __mdmdview_measure_text(text, fontSize, fontWeight) {
 2898|       |  var size = fontSize || 16;
 2899|       |  var raw = text ? String(text) : '';
 2900|       |  if (!raw.length) {
 2901|       |    return { width: 0, height: 0 };
 2902|       |  }
 2903|       |  var weight = (typeof fontWeight === 'number' && !isNaN(fontWeight)) ? fontWeight : null;
 2904|       |  var key = size + '|' + (weight || 0) + '|' + raw;
 2905|       |  var hit = __mdmdview_text_cache[key];
 2906|       |  if (hit) { return hit; }
 2907|       |  if (typeof __mdmdview_measure_text_native === 'function') {
 2908|       |    try {
 2909|       |      var native = __mdmdview_measure_text_native(raw, size, weight);
 2910|       |      if (native && typeof native.width === 'number' && typeof native.height === 'number') {
 2911|       |        __mdmdview_text_cache[key] = native;
 2912|       |        return native;
 2913|       |      }
 2914|       |      if (native && typeof native.length === 'number' && native.length >= 2) {
 2915|       |        var w = Number(native[0]);
 2916|       |        var h = Number(native[1]);
 2917|       |        if (isFinite(w) && isFinite(h)) {
 2918|       |          var converted = { width: w, height: h };
 2919|       |          __mdmdview_text_cache[key] = converted;
 2920|       |          return converted;
 2921|       |        }
 2922|       |      }
 2923|       |    } catch (e) {}

 2926|       |  var maxLen = 0;

 2928|       |    if (lines[i].length > maxLen) { maxLen = lines[i].length; }
 2929|       |  }

 2931|       |  var height = lines.length * size * 1.2;
 2932|       |  var res = { width: width, height: height };
 2933|       |  __mdmdview_text_cache[key] = res;
 2934|       |  return res;
 2935|       |}
 2936|       |function __mdmdview_parse_font_size(value, baseSize) {
 2937|       |  if (value === null || value === undefined) { return baseSize; }
 2938|       |  var raw = String(value).trim();
 2939|       |  if (!raw) { return baseSize; }
 2940|       |  var num = parseFloat(raw);
 2941|       |  if (isNaN(num)) { return baseSize; }
 2942|       |  if (raw.indexOf('em') >= 0) {
 2943|       |    var base = baseSize || 16;
 2944|       |    return num * base;
 2945|       |  }
 2946|       |  if (raw.indexOf('ex') >= 0) {
 2947|       |    var baseEx = baseSize || 16;
 2948|       |    return num * baseEx * 0.5;
 2949|       |  }
 2950|       |  return num;
 2951|       |}
 2952|       |function __mdmdview_parse_font_weight_value(value) {
 2953|       |  if (value === null || value === undefined) { return null; }
 2954|       |  var raw = String(value).trim().toLowerCase();
 2955|       |  if (!raw) { return null; }
 2956|       |  if (raw === 'normal') { return 400; }
 2957|       |  if (raw === 'bold' || raw === 'bolder') { return 700; }
 2958|       |  if (raw === 'lighter') { return 300; }
 2959|       |  var num = parseFloat(raw);
 2960|       |  if (isNaN(num)) { return null; }
 2961|       |  return num;
 2962|       |}
 2963|       |function __mdmdview_parse_css_font_sizes(cssText, cache) {
 2964|       |  if (!cssText || !cache) { return; }

 3244|       |function __mdmdview_parse_length(value, fontSize) {

 3251|       |    return num * (fontSize || 16);
 3252|       |  }
 3253|       |  if (raw.indexOf('ex') >= 0) {
 3254|       |    return num * (fontSize || 16) * 0.5;
 3255|       |  }

 3262|       |function __mdmdview_path_points(el) {
 3263|       |  var d = el && el.getAttribute ? el.getAttribute('d') : null;
 3264|       |  if (!d) { return []; }
 3265|       |  var nums = String(d).match(/-?\d*\.?\d+(?:e[-+]?\d+)?/ig);
 3266|       |  if (!nums || nums.length < 2) { return []; }
 3267|       |  var points = [];
 3268|       |  for (var i = 0; i + 1 < nums.length; i += 2) {
 3269|       |    var x = __mdmdview_parse_num(nums[i]);
 3270|       |    var y = __mdmdview_parse_num(nums[i + 1]);
 3271|       |    points.push(__mdmdview_transform_point(el, x, y));
 3272|       |  }
 3273|       |  return points;

 3275|       |function __mdmdview_path_total_length(el) {
 3276|       |  var points = __mdmdview_path_points(el);
 3277|       |  if (points.length < 2) { return 0; }
 3278|       |  var total = 0;
 3279|       |  for (var i = 1; i < points.length; i++) {
 3280|       |    var dx = points[i].x - points[i - 1].x;
 3281|       |    var dy = points[i].y - points[i - 1].y;
 3282|       |    total += Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
 3283|       |  }
 3284|       |  return total;
 3285|       |}
 3286|       |function __mdmdview_path_point_at_length(el, length) {
 3287|       |  var points = __mdmdview_path_points(el);
 3288|       |  if (!points.length) { return { x: 0, y: 0 }; }
 3289|       |  if (points.length === 1) { return points[0]; }
 3290|       |  var remaining = Math.max(0, __mdmdview_parse_num(length));
 3291|       |  for (var i = 1; i < points.length; i++) {
 3292|       |    var p0 = points[i - 1];
 3293|       |    var p1 = points[i];
 3294|       |    var dx = p1.x - p0.x;
 3295|       |    var dy = p1.y - p0.y;
 3296|       |    var seg = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
 3297|       |    if (seg <= 0) { continue; }
 3298|       |    if (remaining <= seg) {
 3299|       |      var t = remaining / seg;
 3300|       |      return { x: p0.x + dx * t, y: p0.y + dy * t };
 3301|       |    }
 3302|       |    remaining -= seg;
 3303|       |  }
 3304|       |  return points[points.length - 1];
 3305|       |}
 3306|       |function __mdmdview_text_metrics(el) {
 3307|       |  var size = __mdmdview_get_font_size(el);
 3308|       |  var weight = __mdmdview_get_font_weight(el);
 3309|       |  if (!el || !el.children || !el.children.length) {
 3310|       |    var raw = el ? __mdmdview_collect_text(el) : '';
 3311|       |    return __mdmdview_measure_text(raw, size, weight);
 3312|       |  }
 3313|       |  var max_w = 0;
 3314|       |  var max_h = 0;
 3315|       |  var lines = 0;
 3316|       |  for (var i = 0; i < el.children.length; i++) {
 3317|       |    var child = el.children[i];
 3318|       |    var tag = (child.tagName || '').toLowerCase();
 3319|       |    if (tag !== 'tspan') { continue; }
 3320|       |    var text = __mdmdview_collect_text(child);
 3321|       |    if (!text || !String(text).trim()) { continue; }
 3322|       |    var child_weight = __mdmdview_get_font_weight(child);
 3323|       |    var m = __mdmdview_measure_text(text, size, child_weight);
 3324|       |    if (m.width > max_w) { max_w = m.width; }
 3325|       |    if (m.height > max_h) { max_h = m.height; }
 3326|       |    lines += 1;
 3327|       |  }
 3328|       |  if (!lines) {
 3329|       |    var fallback = __mdmdview_collect_text(el);
 3330|       |    return __mdmdview_measure_text(fallback, size, weight);
 3331|       |  }
 3332|       |  var line_height = max_h > 0 ? max_h : size * 1.2;
 3333|       |  return { width: max_w, height: lines * line_height };
 3334|       |}
 3335|       |function __mdmdview_apply_matrix(bbox, a, b, c, d, e, f) {
 3336|       |  if (!bbox) { return bbox; }
 3337|       |  var x = bbox.x;
 3338|       |  var y = bbox.y;
 3339|       |  var w = bbox.width;
 3340|       |  var h = bbox.height;
 3341|       |  var pts = [
 3342|       |    [x, y],
 3343|       |    [x + w, y],
 3344|       |    [x, y + h],
 3345|       |    [x + w, y + h]
 3346|       |  ];
 3347|       |  var min_x = Infinity;
 3348|       |  var min_y = Infinity;
 3349|       |  var max_x = -Infinity;
 3350|       |  var max_y = -Infinity;
 3351|       |  for (var i = 0; i < pts.length; i++) {
 3352|       |    var px = pts[i][0];
 3353|       |    var py = pts[i][1];
 3354|       |    var nx = a * px + c * py + e;
 3355|       |    var ny = b * px + d * py + f;
 3356|       |    if (nx < min_x) { min_x = nx; }
 3357|       |    if (ny < min_y) { min_y = ny; }
 3358|       |    if (nx > max_x) { max_x = nx; }
 3359|       |    if (ny > max_y) { max_y = ny; }
 3360|       |  }
 3361|       |  if (!isFinite(min_x) || !isFinite(min_y) || !isFinite(max_x) || !isFinite(max_y)) {
 3362|       |    return bbox;
 3363|       |  }
 3364|       |  return { x: min_x, y: min_y, width: max_x - min_x, height: max_y - min_y };
 3365|       |}
 3366|       |function __mdmdview_apply_transform(el, bbox) {
 3367|       |  if (!el || !bbox) { return bbox; }
 3368|       |  var transform = el.getAttribute ? el.getAttribute('transform') : null;
 3369|       |  if (!transform) { return bbox; }
 3370|       |  var current = bbox;
 3371|       |  var re = /([a-zA-Z]+)\(([^)]+)\)/g;
 3372|       |  var match = null;
 3373|       |  while ((match = re.exec(String(transform))) !== null) {
 3374|       |    var name = match[1].toLowerCase();
 3375|       |    var params = match[2].split(/[, ]+/).filter(function(p) { return p.length; });
 3376|       |    var nums = [];
 3377|       |    for (var i = 0; i < params.length; i++) {
 3378|       |      nums.push(__mdmdview_parse_num(params[i]));
 3379|       |    }
 3380|       |    if (name === 'translate') {
 3381|       |      var tx = nums.length ? nums[0] : 0;
 3382|       |      var ty = nums.length > 1 ? nums[1] : 0;
 3383|       |      current = __mdmdview_apply_matrix(current, 1, 0, 0, 1, tx, ty);
 3384|       |    } else if (name === 'scale') {
 3385|       |      var sx = nums.length ? nums[0] : 1;
 3386|       |      var sy = nums.length > 1 ? nums[1] : sx;
 3387|       |      current = __mdmdview_apply_matrix(current, sx, 0, 0, sy, 0, 0);
 3388|       |    } else if (name === 'matrix' && nums.length >= 6) {
 3389|       |      current = __mdmdview_apply_matrix(
 3390|       |        current,
 3391|       |        nums[0],
 3392|       |        nums[1],
 3393|       |        nums[2],
 3394|       |        nums[3],
 3395|       |        nums[4],
 3396|       |        nums[5]
 3397|       |      );

 3421|       |function __mdmdview_children_bounds(el) {

 3768|       |function __mdmdview_order_children(node) {

 3790|       |function __mdmdview_serialize_attrs(node) {

 3806|       |  }
 3807|       |  return out;
 3808|       |}
 3809|       |function __mdmdview_serialize(node) {
 3810|       |  if (!node) { return ''; }
 3811|       |  if (node.tagName === '#text') {
 3812|       |    return __mdmdview_escape_text(node.textContent || '');
 3813|       |  }
 3814|       |  var tag = node.tagName || '';
 3815|       |  var attrs = __mdmdview_serialize_attrs(node);
 3816|       |  var content = '';
 3817|       |  var lower = String(tag).toLowerCase();
 3818|       |  // Check if this is an xhtml element (e.g., div inside foreignObject)
 3819|       |  var isXhtml = node.namespaceURI && node.namespaceURI.indexOf('xhtml') >= 0;
 3820|       |  if (node.children && node.children.length) {
 3821|       |    if (lower === 'style' || lower === 'script') {
 3822|       |      content = __mdmdview_collect_text(node);
 3823|       |    } else {
 3824|       |      var ordered = __mdmdview_order_children(node);
 3825|       |      for (var i = 0; i < ordered.length; i++) {
 3826|       |        content += __mdmdview_serialize(ordered[i]);
 3827|       |      }
 3828|       |    }
 3829|       |  } else if (node.__rawInnerHTML && isXhtml) {
 3830|       |    // For xhtml elements with raw innerHTML, escape & but preserve HTML tags
 3831|       |    // Only escape & that aren't already part of entity references like &amp; &lt; etc.
 3832|       |    content = node.__rawInnerHTML.replace(/&(?!(amp|lt|gt|quot|apos|#\d+|#x[0-9a-fA-F]+);)/g, '&amp;');
 3833|       |  } else if (node.textContent) {
 3834|       |    if (lower === 'style' || lower === 'script') {
 3835|       |      content = node.textContent;
 3836|       |    } else {
 3837|       |      content = __mdmdview_escape_text(node.textContent);
 3838|       |    }
 3839|       |  }
 3840|       |  return '<' + tag + attrs + '>' + content + '</' + tag + '>';
 3841|       |}
 3842|       |function __mdmdview_serialize_children(node) {
 3843|       |  if (!node || !node.children) { return ''; }
 3844|       |  var content = '';
 3845|       |  var ordered = __mdmdview_order_children(node);
 3846|       |  for (var i = 0; i < ordered.length; i++) {
 3847|       |    content += __mdmdview_serialize(ordered[i]);
 3848|       |  }

 3851|       |function __mdmdview_make_style() {

 3854|       |  style.removeProperty = function(key) { delete style[key]; };

 3856|       |    return Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(style, key) ? style[key] : '';
 3857|       |  };

 3860|       |function __mdmdview_detach(child) {
 3861|       |  if (!child || typeof child !== 'object') { return; }
 3862|       |  var parent = child.parentNode;
 3863|       |  if (!parent || !parent.children) { return; }
 3864|       |  var idx = parent.children.indexOf(child);
 3865|       |  if (idx >= 0) {
 3866|       |    parent.children.splice(idx, 1);
 3867|       |    parent.childNodes = parent.children;
 3868|       |    parent.firstChild = parent.children[0] || null;
 3869|       |  }
 3870|       |  child.parentNode = null;

 3872|       |function __mdmdview_parse_font_px(font) {

 3875|       |  if (match && match[1]) {
 3876|       |    var value = parseFloat(match[1]);
 3877|       |    if (!isNaN(value)) { return value; }
 3878|       |  }
 3879|       |  return 16;

 3929|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_get_size(node) {
 3930|       |  var data = node && typeof node.data === 'function' ? node.data() : null;
 3931|       |  var w = data && data.width !== undefined ? Number(data.width) : 80;
 3932|       |  var h = data && data.height !== undefined ? Number(data.height) : 40;
 3933|       |  if (!isFinite(w) || w <= 0) { w = 80; }
 3934|       |  if (!isFinite(h) || h <= 0) { h = 40; }
 3935|       |  return { w: w, h: h };
 3936|       |}
 3937|       |function __mdmdview_rect_intersection(hw, hh, ux, uy) {
 3938|       |  var ax = Math.abs(ux);
 3939|       |  var ay = Math.abs(uy);
 3940|       |  var dx = ax > 1e-6 ? hw / ax : Infinity;
 3941|       |  var dy = ay > 1e-6 ? hh / ay : Infinity;
 3942|       |  var dist = Math.min(dx, dy);
 3943|       |  if (!isFinite(dist) || dist <= 0) { dist = Math.max(hw, hh); }
 3944|       |  return dist;
 3945|       |}
 3946|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_collect(nodes, edges) {
 3947|       |  var node_map = {};
 3948|       |  var parents = {};
 3949|       |  var children = {};
 3950|       |  var root = null;
 3951|       |  var root_id = null;
 3952|       |  for (var i = 0; i < nodes.length; i++) {
 3953|       |    var node = nodes[i];
 3954|       |    if (!node || typeof node.data !== 'function') { continue; }
 3955|       |    var data = node.data();
 3956|       |    if (!data) { continue; }
 3957|       |    var id = data.id !== undefined ? String(data.id) : null;
 3958|       |    if (id === null) { continue; }
 3959|       |    node_map[id] = node;
 3960|       |  }
 3961|       |  for (var j = 0; j < edges.length; j++) {
 3962|       |    var edge = edges[j];
 3963|       |    if (!edge || typeof edge.data !== 'function') { continue; }
 3964|       |    var ed = edge.data();
 3965|       |    if (!ed) { continue; }
 3966|       |    var source = ed.source !== undefined ? String(ed.source) : null;
 3967|       |    var target = ed.target !== undefined ? String(ed.target) : null;
 3968|       |    if (!source || !target) { continue; }
 3969|       |    if (!children[source]) { children[source] = []; }
 3970|       |    children[source].push(target);
 3971|       |    parents[target] = source;
 3972|       |  }
 3973|       |  for (var key in node_map) {
 3974|       |    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(node_map, key)) { continue; }
 3975|       |    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parents, key)) {
 3976|       |      root = node_map[key];
 3977|       |      root_id = key;
 3978|       |      break;
 3979|       |    }
 3980|       |  }
 3981|       |  if (!root && nodes.length) {
 3982|       |    root = nodes[0];
 3983|       |    if (root && typeof root.data === 'function') {
 3984|       |      var rd = root.data();
 3985|       |      if (rd && rd.id !== undefined) { root_id = String(rd.id); }
 3986|       |    }
 3987|       |  }
 3988|       |  return { node_map: node_map, children: children, parents: parents, root: root, root_id: root_id };
 3989|       |}
 3990|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(node_id, tree) {
 3991|       |  var kids = tree.children[node_id] || [];
 3992|       |  return kids.slice();
 3993|       |}
 3994|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_assign_sides(tree) {
 3995|       |  var sides = {};
 3996|       |  var root_id = tree.root_id;
 3997|       |  if (!root_id) { return sides; }
 3998|       |  sides[root_id] = 0;
 3999|       |  function assign(node_id) {
 4000|       |    var kids = __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(node_id, tree);
 4001|       |    for (var i = 0; i < kids.length; i++) {
 4002|       |      var child_id = kids[i];
 4003|       |      var child_node = tree.node_map[child_id];
 4004|       |      var side = sides[node_id];
 4005|       |      if (node_id === root_id) {
 4006|       |        var data = child_node && typeof child_node.data === 'function' ? child_node.data() : null;
 4007|       |        var section = data && data.section !== undefined ? Number(data.section) : i;
 4008|       |        if (!isFinite(section)) { section = i; }
 4009|       |        side = (section % 2 === 0) ? -1 : 1;
 4010|       |      }
 4011|       |      sides[child_id] = side;
 4012|       |      assign(child_id);
 4013|       |    }
 4014|       |  }
 4015|       |  assign(root_id);
 4016|       |  return sides;
 4017|       |}
 4018|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_subtree_height(node_id, tree, sizes, gap, heights) {
 4019|       |  var kids = __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(node_id, tree);
 4020|       |  if (!kids.length) {
 4021|       |    heights[node_id] = sizes[node_id].h;
 4022|       |    return heights[node_id];
 4023|       |  }
 4024|       |  var total = 0;
 4025|       |  for (var i = 0; i < kids.length; i++) {
 4026|       |    total += __mdmdview_mindmap_subtree_height(kids[i], tree, sizes, gap, heights);
 4027|       |  }
 4028|       |  total += gap * Math.max(0, kids.length - 1);
 4029|       |  var base = sizes[node_id].h;
 4030|       |  heights[node_id] = total > base ? total : base;
 4031|       |  return heights[node_id];
 4032|       |}
 4033|       |function __mdmdview_mindmap_layout(nodes, edges) {
 4034|       |  var tree = __mdmdview_mindmap_collect(nodes, edges);
 4035|       |  if (!tree.root || !tree.root_id) { return; }
 4036|       |  var sizes = {};
 4037|       |  for (var key in tree.node_map) {
 4038|       |    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(tree.node_map, key)) { continue; }
 4039|       |    sizes[key] = __mdmdview_mindmap_get_size(tree.node_map[key]);
 4040|       |  }
 4041|       |  var sides = __mdmdview_mindmap_assign_sides(tree);
 4042|       |  var pad = 10;
 4043|       |  var root_data = tree.root && typeof tree.root.data === 'function' ? tree.root.data() : null;
 4044|       |  if (root_data && root_data.padding !== undefined) {
 4045|       |    var p = Number(root_data.padding);
 4046|       |    if (isFinite(p) && p > 0) { pad = p; }
 4047|       |  }
 4048|       |  var gap_x = Math.max(24, pad * 2.2);
 4049|       |  var positions = {};
 4050|       |  positions[tree.root_id] = { x: 0, y: 0 };
 4051|       |  var angles = {};
 4052|       |  angles[tree.root_id] = 0;
 4053|       |  var deg = Math.PI / 180;
 4054|       |  var left_start = 245 * deg;
 4055|       |  var left_end = 122 * deg;
 4056|       |  var right_start = 345 * deg;
 4057|       |  var right_end = 353 * deg;
 4058|       |  function assign_even(children, start, end) {
 4059|       |    if (!children.length) { return; }
 4060|       |    if (children.length === 1) {
 4061|       |      angles[children[0]] = (start + end) / 2;
 4062|       |      return;
 4063|       |    }
 4064|       |    var span = end - start;
 4065|       |    var step = span / (children.length - 1);
 4066|       |    for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
 4067|       |      angles[children[i]] = start + step * i;
 4068|       |    }
 4069|       |  }
 4070|       |  var root_kids = __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(tree.root_id, tree);
 4071|       |  var left = [];
 4072|       |  var right = [];
 4073|       |  for (var rk = 0; rk < root_kids.length; rk++) {
 4074|       |    var kid = root_kids[rk];
 4075|       |    var side = sides[kid] || 1;
 4076|       |    if (side < 0) { left.push(kid); } else { right.push(kid); }
 4077|       |  }
 4078|       |  assign_even(left, left_start, left_end);
 4079|       |  assign_even(right, right_start, right_end);
 4080|       |  function spread_for_depth(depth, parent_angle) {
 4081|       |    var base = 38 * deg;
 4082|       |    var cos = Math.cos(parent_angle || 0);
 4083|       |    var bias = cos < 0 ? 1.4 : 1.0;
 4084|       |    var depth_factor = 1 + Math.max(0, depth - 1) * 0.2;
 4085|       |    return (base * bias) / depth_factor;
 4086|       |  }
 4087|       |  function assign_child_angles(node_id, depth) {
 4088|       |    var kids = __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(node_id, tree);
 4089|       |    if (!kids.length) { return; }
 4090|       |    var parent_angle = angles[node_id] || 0;
 4091|       |    if (kids.length === 1) {
 4092|       |      var sin = Math.sin(parent_angle);
 4093|       |      var cos = Math.cos(parent_angle);
 4094|       |      var offset = 0;
 4095|       |      if (cos < -0.1 && sin < -0.1) { offset = 20 * deg; }
 4096|       |      angles[kids[0]] = parent_angle + offset;
 4097|       |      assign_child_angles(kids[0], depth + 1);
 4098|       |      return;
 4099|       |    }
 4100|       |    var spread = spread_for_depth(depth, parent_angle);
 4101|       |    var upper = 1.3;
 4102|       |    var lower = 0.9;
 4103|       |    var start = parent_angle - spread * upper;
 4104|       |    var end = parent_angle + spread * lower;
 4105|       |    assign_even(kids, start, end);
 4106|       |    for (var i = 0; i < kids.length; i++) {
 4107|       |      assign_child_angles(kids[i], depth + 1);
 4108|       |    }
 4109|       |  }
 4110|       |  for (var a = 0; a < root_kids.length; a++) {
 4111|       |    assign_child_angles(root_kids[a], 1);
 4112|       |  }
 4113|       |  function place_node(node_id, depth) {
 4114|       |    var kids = __mdmdview_mindmap_sorted_children(node_id, tree);
 4115|       |    if (!kids.length) { return; }
 4116|       |    var parent_pos = positions[node_id];
 4117|       |    var parent_size = sizes[node_id];
 4118|       |    var parent_radius = Math.max(parent_size.w, parent_size.h) / 2;
 4119|       |    var parent_angle = angles[node_id] || 0;
 4120|       |    for (var i = 0; i < kids.length; i++) {
 4121|       |      var child = kids[i];
 4122|       |      var angle = angles[child];
 4123|       |      if (angle === undefined) { angle = angles[node_id] || 0; }
 4124|       |      var child_size = sizes[child];
 4125|       |      var child_radius = Math.max(child_size.w, child_size.h) / 2;
 4126|       |      var dist = parent_radius + child_radius + gap_x;
 4127|       |      if (depth > 0) {
 4128|       |        dist *= 1.15;
 4129|       |      }
 4130|       |      var factor = 1;
 4131|       |      if (depth === 0) {
 4132|       |        var cos = Math.cos(angle);
 4133|       |        var sin = Math.sin(angle);
 4134|       |        factor *= 1.1 * (1 + 0.2 * cos + 0.1 * sin);
 4135|       |      } else {
 4136|       |        var diff = angle - parent_angle;
 4137|       |        while (diff > Math.PI) { diff -= Math.PI * 2; }
 4138|       |        while (diff < -Math.PI) { diff += Math.PI * 2; }
 4139|       |        var norm = diff / (Math.PI / 6);
 4140|       |        if (norm > 1) { norm = 1; }
 4141|       |        if (norm < -1) { norm = -1; }
 4142|       |        factor *= 1 + 0.12 * norm;
 4143|       |        factor *= 0.98 + depth * 0.04;
 4144|       |      }
 4145|       |      if (depth > 0) {
 4146|       |        var sin = Math.sin(angle);
 4147|       |        var cos = Math.cos(angle);
 4148|       |        if (sin >= 0) {
 4149|       |          factor *= 1 + 0.15 * sin;
 4150|       |        } else if (cos > 0) {
 4151|       |          factor *= 1 + 0.05 * sin;
 4152|       |        } else {
 4153|       |          factor *= 1 + 0.25 * sin;
 4154|       |        }
 4155|       |        if (sin < -0.2 && cos < 0) {
 4156|       |          factor *= 0.9;
 4157|       |        }
 4158|       |      }
 4159|       |      if (kids.length === 1) {
 4160|       |        factor *= 0.95;
 4161|       |      }
 4162|       |      dist = dist * factor;
 4163|       |      var cx = parent_pos.x + Math.cos(angle) * dist;
 4164|       |      var cy = parent_pos.y + Math.sin(angle) * dist;
 4165|       |      positions[child] = { x: cx, y: cy };
 4166|       |      place_node(child, depth + 1);
 4167|       |    }
 4168|       |  }
 4169|       |  place_node(tree.root_id, 0);
 4170|       |  var min_x = Infinity;
 4171|       |  var min_y = Infinity;
 4172|       |  for (var id in positions) {
 4173|       |    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(positions, id)) { continue; }
 4174|       |    var pos = positions[id];
 4175|       |    var size = sizes[id];
 4176|       |    var lx = pos.x - size.w / 2;
 4177|       |    var ly = pos.y - size.h / 2;
 4178|       |    if (lx < min_x) { min_x = lx; }
 4179|       |    if (ly < min_y) { min_y = ly; }
 4180|       |  }
 4181|       |  if (!isFinite(min_x)) { min_x = 0; }
 4182|       |  if (!isFinite(min_y)) { min_y = 0; }
 4183|       |  var dx = pad - min_x;
 4184|       |  var dy = pad - min_y;
 4185|       |  for (var nid in positions) {
 4186|       |    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(positions, nid)) { continue; }
 4187|       |    positions[nid].x += dx;
 4188|       |    positions[nid].y += dy;
 4189|       |  }
 4190|       |  for (var n = 0; n < nodes.length; n++) {
 4191|       |    var node = nodes[n];
 4192|       |    if (!node || typeof node.data !== 'function') { continue; }
 4193|       |    var data = node.data();
 4194|       |    var id = data && data.id !== undefined ? String(data.id) : null;
 4195|       |    if (!id || !positions[id]) { continue; }
 4196|       |    node._position = { x: positions[id].x, y: positions[id].y };
 4197|       |    node.position = function() { return this._position; };
 4198|       |    data.x = positions[id].x;
 4199|       |    data.y = positions[id].y;
 4200|       |  }
 4201|       |  for (var e = 0; e < edges.length; e++) {
 4202|       |    var edge = edges[e];
 4203|       |    if (!edge || typeof edge.data !== 'function') { continue; }
 4204|       |    var ed = edge.data();
 4205|       |    var source = ed && ed.source !== undefined ? String(ed.source) : null;
 4206|       |    var target = ed && ed.target !== undefined ? String(ed.target) : null;
 4207|       |    if (!source || !target) { continue; }
 4208|       |    var s_pos = positions[source];
 4209|       |    var t_pos = positions[target];
 4210|       |    if (!s_pos || !t_pos) { continue; }
 4211|       |    var s_size = sizes[source];
 4212|       |    var t_size = sizes[target];
 4213|       |    var dx = t_pos.x - s_pos.x;
 4214|       |    var dy = t_pos.y - s_pos.y;
 4215|       |    var len = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
 4216|       |    if (!isFinite(len) || len <= 0.001) { len = 1; }
 4217|       |    var ux = dx / len;
 4218|       |    var uy = dy / len;
 4219|       |    var s_radius = __mdmdview_rect_intersection(s_size.w / 2, s_size.h / 2, ux, uy);
 4220|       |    var t_radius = __mdmdview_rect_intersection(t_size.w / 2, t_size.h / 2, ux, uy);
 4221|       |    var overlap = -2;
 4222|       |    s_radius += overlap;
 4223|       |    t_radius += overlap;
 4224|       |    if (s_radius < 0) { s_radius = 0; }
 4225|       |    if (t_radius < 0) { t_radius = 0; }
 4226|       |    var max_total = len * 0.95;
 4227|       |    if (s_radius + t_radius > max_total) {
 4228|       |      var scale = max_total / (s_radius + t_radius);
 4229|       |      s_radius *= scale;
 4230|       |      t_radius *= scale;
 4231|       |    }
 4232|       |    var sx = s_pos.x + ux * s_radius;
 4233|       |    var sy = s_pos.y + uy * s_radius;
 4234|       |    var ex = t_pos.x - ux * t_radius;
 4235|       |    var ey = t_pos.y - uy * t_radius;
 4236|       |    var mx = (sx + ex) / 2;
 4237|       |    var my = (sy + ey) / 2;
 4238|       |    edge[0] = edge[0] || {};
 4239|       |    edge[0]._private = {
 4240|       |      bodyBounds: true,
 4241|       |      rscratch: {
 4242|       |        startX: sx,
 4243|       |        startY: sy,
 4244|       |        midX: mx,
 4245|       |        midY: my,
 4246|       |        endX: ex,
 4247|       |        endY: ey
 4248|       |      }
 4249|       |    };
 4250|       |  }

 5176|       |      if (p && p.tagName) { return p; }
 5177|       |      return null;
 5178|       |    },
 5179|       |    configurable: true
 5180|       |  });
 5181|       |}
 5182|       |if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty('offsetWidth')) {
 5183|       |  Object.defineProperty(Object.prototype, 'offsetWidth', {
 5184|       |    get: function() {
 5185|       |      if (this && typeof this.getBBox === 'function') {
 5186|       |        var width = this.getBBox().width || 0;
 5187|       |        if (width) { return width; }
 5188|       |      }
 5189|       |      return window.innerWidth || 0;
 5190|       |    },
 5191|       |    configurable: true
 5192|       |  });
