  158|       |/// Per-diagram resize debounce state.
  159|       |struct DiagramDebounce {
  160|       |    /// Width bucket at which we last rasterized (and cached a texture).
  161|       |    last_rasterized_bucket: u32,
  162|       |    /// Width bucket seen on the most recent frame (for trailing-edge reset).
  163|       |    last_seen_bucket: u32,
  164|       |    /// When `last_seen_bucket` last changed. `None` when no resize is active.
  165|       |    bucket_changed_at: Option<Instant>,
  166|       |}
  167|       |
  168|       |/// Pikchr diagram renderer.
  169|       |///
  170|       |/// Renders Pikchr markup to SVG using the embedded pikchr C library,
  171|       |/// then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
  172|       |/// runs on a dedicated worker thread; the main thread only uploads the
  173|       |/// resulting RGBA data to the GPU (~0.1-1ms).
  174|       |pub(crate) struct PikchrRenderer {
  175|       |    /// LRU cache: texture_key (String) -> (TextureHandle, [width, height])
  176|       |    textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, PikchrTextureEntry>>,
  177|       |    /// SVG cache: svg_cache_key (u128: code_hash << 1 | dark_mode) -> SVG string
  178|       |    svg_cache: RefCell<LruCache<u128, String>>,
  179|       |    /// Error cache: code_hash (u64) -> error message.
  180|       |    /// Errors don't depend on dark_mode, only on source syntax.
  181|       |    errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  182|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize suppression.
  183|       |    debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, DiagramDebounce>>,
  184|       |    /// Maps code_hash -> texture_key of the most recently rasterized texture.
  185|       |    /// Used to display a stale texture scaled to fit during debounce cooldown.
  186|       |    latest_texture_key: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  187|       |    /// Job sender. Wrapped in Option so Drop can close the channel
  188|       |    /// before joining the worker thread (same pattern as Mermaid).
  189|       |    job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  190|       |    /// Result receiver. Polled each frame in poll_results().
  191|       |    result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  192|       |    /// Worker thread handle. Joined on drop for clean shutdown.
  193|       |    worker_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
  194|       |    /// Set of code_hashes with in-flight jobs. Keyed by code_hash (not
  195|       |    /// texture_key) to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes
  196|       |    /// while a job is already pending for the same diagram.
  197|       |    pending: RefCell<HashSet<u64>>,
  198|       |    /// Tracks the currently desired width bucket per diagram (for stale discard).
  199|       |    wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,
  200|       |}
  201|       |
  202|       |impl PikchrRenderer {
  203|       |    /// Create a new PikchrRenderer, loading system fonts once at startup.
  204|       |    /// Without system fonts, usvg's default empty fontdb silently drops all
  205|       |    /// text labels in rasterized SVGs. Takes ~50-200ms (one-time cost).
  206|       |    /// Spawns a dedicated worker thread for background rasterization.
  207|       |    /// Same pattern as mermaid_renderer.rs and image_decode.rs.
  208|    480|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
  209|    480|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  210|    480|        db.load_system_fonts();
  211|    480|        let fontdb = Arc::new(db);
  212|       |
  213|    480|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  214|    480|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  215|       |
  216|       |        // Clone fontdb for the worker thread
  217|    480|        let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
  218|    480|        let worker_handle = std::thread::Builder::new()
  219|    480|            .name("pikchr-raster".into())
  220|    480|            .spawn(move || {
  221|    480|                Self::worker_loop(worker_fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
  222|    480|            })
  223|    480|            .expect("failed to spawn pikchr raster worker");
  224|       |
  225|    480|        Self {
  226|    480|            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  227|    480|            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  228|    480|            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  229|    480|            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  230|    480|            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  231|    480|            job_tx: Some(job_tx),
  232|    480|            result_rx,
  233|    480|            worker_handle: Some(worker_handle),
  234|    480|            pending: RefCell::new(HashSet::new()),
  235|    480|            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  236|    480|        }
  237|    480|    }
  238|       |
  239|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  240|       |    // Worker thread
  241|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  242|       |
  243|       |    /// Main loop for the worker thread. Receives jobs, processes them, and
  244|       |    /// sends results back. Uses `try_send` to avoid blocking if the result
  245|       |    /// channel is full (prevents shutdown deadlock). Same pattern as Mermaid
  246|       |    /// (mermaid_renderer.rs line 1264-1269).
  247|    484|    fn worker_loop(
  248|    484|        fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  249|    484|        job_rx: JobReceiver,
  250|    484|        result_tx: ResultSender,
  251|    484|    ) {
  252|    490|        while let Ok(job) = job_rx.recv() {
                                   ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (252:19): [True: 6, False: 484]
  ------------------
  253|      6|            let result = Self::process_job(&fontdb, job);
  254|      6|            // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
  255|      6|            // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
  256|      6|            // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
  257|      6|            // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable --
  258|      6|            // the main thread still has the code_hash in the pending set, so
  259|      6|            // render_block will show the stale texture or placeholder and the
  260|      6|            // pending entry will be leaked (harmless, cleared on next
  261|      6|            // release_gpu_textures or app restart).
  262|      6|            let _ = result_tx.try_send(result);
  263|      6|        }
  264|       |        // job_rx disconnected (main thread dropped job_tx) -- exit
  265|    484|    }
  266|       |
  267|       |    /// Process a single rasterization job: source->SVG (if needed), then
  268|       |    /// SVG->RGBA. Returns a result with either RGBA data or an error.
  269|      8|    fn process_job(fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>, job: RasterJob) -> RasterResult {
  270|       |        // Step 1: Get SVG (from cache or render fresh)
  271|      8|        let svg = match job.svg {
  272|      2|            Some(cached_svg) => Ok(cached_svg),
  273|       |            None => {
  274|      6|                let code = job.code.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
  275|      6|                Self::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, job.dark_mode)
  276|       |            }
  277|       |        };
  278|       |
  279|      8|        let svg = match svg {
                          ^7
  280|      7|            Ok(svg) => svg,
  281|      1|            Err(err) => {
  282|      1|                return RasterResult {
  283|      1|                    code_hash: job.code_hash,
  284|      1|                    texture_key: job.texture_key,
  285|      1|                    dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  286|      1|                    svg: None,
  287|      1|                    rgba: None,
  288|      1|                    raster_size: None,
  289|      1|                    display_size: None,
  290|      1|                    error: Some(err),
  291|      1|                };
  292|       |            }
  293|       |        };
  294|       |
  295|       |        // Step 2: Rasterize SVG to RGBA
  296|      7|        match Self::rasterize_svg(fontdb, &svg, job.width_bucket, job.scale_bucket) {
  297|      6|            Ok(output) => RasterResult {
  298|      6|                code_hash: job.code_hash,
  299|      6|                texture_key: job.texture_key,
  300|      6|                dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  301|      6|                svg: Some(svg),
  302|      6|                rgba: Some(output.rgba),
  303|      6|                raster_size: Some(output.raster_size),
  304|      6|                display_size: Some(output.display_size),
  305|      6|                error: None,
  306|      6|            },
  307|      1|            Err(err) => RasterResult {
  308|      1|                code_hash: job.code_hash,
  309|      1|                texture_key: job.texture_key,
  310|      1|                dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  311|      1|                svg: Some(svg), // SVG succeeded -- cache it even if rasterize failed
  312|      1|                rgba: None,
  313|      1|                raster_size: None,
  314|      1|                display_size: None,
  315|      1|                error: Some(err),
  316|      1|            },
  317|       |        }
  318|      8|    }
  319|       |
  320|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  321|       |    // Rasterization (runs on worker thread)
  322|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  323|       |
  324|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  325|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  326|       |    ///
  327|       |    /// Injects font-family into Pikchr SVGs, parses via usvg (using the shared
  328|       |    /// fontdb with system fonts), and rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia.
  329|       |    /// Rasterizes at `PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  330|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  331|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  332|     10|    fn rasterize_svg(
  333|     10|        fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  334|     10|        svg: &str,
  335|     10|        width_bucket: u32,
  336|     10|        scale_bucket: u32,
  337|     10|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  338|       |        // Inject a font-family into the SVG so usvg picks a known good font.
  339|       |        // Pikchr outputs <svg style='font-size:initial;'> with no font-family
  340|       |        // on <text> elements, so usvg falls back to whatever thin default it finds.
  341|     10|        let svg = Self::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  344|     10|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  345|     10|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  346|     10|            ..Default::default()
  347|     10|        };
  348|     10|        let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
                          ^8
  349|     10|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^2                                ^2
  350|       |
  351|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  352|      8|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();
  353|      8|        let (w, h) = (size.width().max(1), size.height().max(1));
  354|       |
  355|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  356|      8|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  357|      8|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (357:30): [True: 6, False: 2]
  ------------------
  358|      6|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  359|       |        } else {
  360|      2|            base_scale
  361|       |        };
  362|      8|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  363|       |
  364|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  365|      8|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  366|      8|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  367|       |
  368|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  369|      8|        let raster_scale = scale * PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE;
  370|      8|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  371|      8|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  372|       |
  373|       |        // Clamp to reasonable maximum
  374|      8|        if raster_w > MAX_RASTER_SIDE || raster_h > MAX_RASTER_SIDE {
                                                       ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (374:12): [True: 2, False: 6]
  |  Branch (374:42): [True: 0, False: 6]
  ------------------
  375|      2|            let clamp_scale = (MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_w as f32)
  376|      2|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  377|      2|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  378|      2|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  379|      6|        }
  380|       |
  381|       |        // Rasterize: Pikchr SVGs have no background fill, so the pixmap starts
  382|       |        // transparent and the diagram draws on top.
  383|      8|        let mut pixmap = tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(raster_w, raster_h)
  384|      8|            .ok_or_else(|| "Pixmap allocation failed".to_string())?;
                                         ^0                         ^0          ^0
  385|      8|        let transform = tiny_skia::Transform::from_scale(raster_scale, raster_scale);
  386|       |        // Bind PixmapMut to a local -- can't take &mut of a temporary rvalue.
  387|      8|        let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  388|      8|        resvg::render(&tree, transform, &mut pmut);
  389|       |
  390|      8|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  391|      8|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  392|      8|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  393|      8|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  394|      8|        })
  395|     10|    }
  396|       |
  397|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  398|       |    // Main-thread polling
  399|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  400|       |
  401|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  402|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  403|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  404|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  405|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  406|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  407|     45|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {
  408|     45|        let mut any_processed = false;
  409|       |
  410|       |        loop {
  411|     50|            match self.result_rx.try_recv() {
  412|      5|                Ok(result) => {
  413|      5|                    any_processed = true;
  414|       |
  415|       |                    // Remove from pending set
  416|      5|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&result.code_hash);
  417|       |
  418|       |                    // Always cache SVG (content-addressed, width-independent)
  419|      5|                    if let Some(ref svg) = result.svg {
                                              ^4
  ------------------
  |  Branch (419:28): [True: 4, False: 1]
  ------------------
  420|      4|                        let sk = svg_cache_key(result.code_hash, result.dark_mode);
  421|      4|                        self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(sk, svg.clone());
  422|      4|                    }
                                  ^1
  423|       |
  424|       |                    // Always cache errors (prevents infinite retry loops)
  425|      5|                    if let Some(ref err) = result.error {
                                              ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (425:28): [True: 1, False: 4]
  ------------------
  426|      1|                        self.errors
  427|      1|                            .borrow_mut()
  428|      1|                            .insert(result.code_hash, err.clone());
  429|      1|                        continue; // No RGBA to upload
  430|      4|                    }
  431|       |
  432|       |                    // Stale result check: skip GPU upload if width bucket changed since enqueue
  433|      4|                    if self.is_stale_result(&result.texture_key, result.code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (433:24): [True: 2, False: 2]
  ------------------
  434|      2|                        continue;
  435|      2|                    }
  436|       |
  437|       |                    // Upload RGBA to GPU texture
  438|      2|                    if let (Some(rgba), Some(raster_size), Some(display_size)) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (438:28): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  439|      2|                        (result.rgba, result.raster_size, result.display_size)
  440|       |                    {
  441|      2|                        let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(raster_size, &rgba);
  442|      2|                        let texture = ctx.load_texture(
  443|      2|                            result.texture_key.clone(),
  444|      2|                            image,
  445|       |                            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  446|       |                        );
  447|      2|                        let entry = PikchrTextureEntry {
  448|      2|                            texture,
  449|      2|                            display_size,
  450|      2|                        };
  451|      2|                        self.textures
  452|      2|                            .borrow_mut()
  453|      2|                            .insert(result.texture_key.clone(), entry);
  454|      2|                        self.latest_texture_key
  455|      2|                            .borrow_mut()
  456|      2|                            .insert(result.code_hash, result.texture_key.clone());
  457|       |                        // Update debounce state
  458|      1|                        if let Some(width_bucket) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (458:32): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  459|      2|                            Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(&result.texture_key)
  460|      1|                        {
  461|      1|                            self.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
  462|      1|                                result.code_hash,
  463|      1|                                DiagramDebounce {
  464|      1|                                    last_rasterized_bucket: width_bucket,
  465|      1|                                    last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
  466|      1|                                    bucket_changed_at: None,
  467|      1|                                },
  468|      1|                            );
  469|      1|                        }
  470|      0|                    }
  471|       |                }
  472|     43|                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
  473|       |                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
  474|       |                    // Worker thread has died (panic or unexpected exit).
  475|       |                    // Clear all pending entries so render_block can detect the dead
  476|       |                    // worker via try_send(Disconnected) and show an error instead of
  477|       |                    // showing "Rendering..." forever.
  478|      2|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  479|      2|                    break;
  480|       |                }
  481|       |            }
  482|       |        }
  483|       |
  484|     45|        if any_processed {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (484:12): [True: 5, False: 40]
  ------------------
  485|      5|            ctx.request_repaint();
  486|     40|        }
  487|     45|        any_processed
  488|     45|    }
  489|       |
  490|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  491|       |    // Public API
  492|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  493|       |
  494|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  495|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  496|     45|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  497|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  498|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  499|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  500|     45|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  501|       |
  502|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  503|     45|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (503:12): [True: 1, False: 44]
  ------------------
  504|      1|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  505|     44|        }
  506|     45|    }
  507|       |
  508|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  509|     62|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  510|     62|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  511|     62|    }
  512|       |
  513|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  514|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  515|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  516|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  517|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  518|      3|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  519|      3|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  520|      3|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  521|      3|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  522|      3|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  523|      3|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  524|      3|    }
  525|       |
  526|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a Pikchr code block.
  527|       |    ///
  528|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  529|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  530|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  531|       |    ///
  532|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  533|     14|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  534|     14|        &self,
  535|     14|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  536|     14|        code: &str,
  537|     14|        ui_scale: f32,
  538|     14|        code_font_size: f32,
  539|     14|    ) -> bool {
  540|     14|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  541|     14|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  542|     14|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  545|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  546|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  547|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  548|     14|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  549|     14|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (549:16): [True: 1, False: 13]
  ------------------
  550|      1|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  551|      1|            return true;
  552|     13|        }
  553|       |
  554|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  555|     13|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  556|     13|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  557|     13|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  558|     13|        let texture_key = format!(
  559|       |            "pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  560|     13|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  561|       |        );
  562|       |
  563|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  564|     13|        self.wanted_bucket
  565|     13|            .borrow_mut()
  566|     13|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  567|       |
  568|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  569|       |        {
  570|     13|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  571|     13|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (571:20): [True: 1, False: 12]
  ------------------
  572|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  573|      1|                return true;
  574|     12|            }
  575|       |        }
  576|       |
  577|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  578|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  579|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  580|     12|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
                                                                                    ^6
  581|      6|            (
  582|      6|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  583|      6|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  584|      6|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  585|      6|            )
  586|      6|        });
  587|     12|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
                                   ^6               ^6         ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (587:16): [True: 6, False: 6]
  ------------------
  588|      6|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (588:16): [True: 5, False: 1]
  ------------------
  589|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  590|      5|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (590:20): [True: 3, False: 2]
  ------------------
  591|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  592|      3|                    let changed_at = Instant::now();
  593|      3|                    self.debounce.borrow_mut().entry(code_hash).and_modify(|d| {
  594|      3|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  595|      3|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(changed_at);
  596|      3|                    });
  597|      2|                }
  598|      5|                let changed_at = self
  599|      5|                    .debounce
  600|      5|                    .borrow()
  601|      5|                    .get(&code_hash)
  602|      5|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  603|      5|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  604|      5|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  605|      5|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  606|      5|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (606:20): [True: 4, False: 1]
  ------------------
  607|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  608|      2|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (608:28): [True: 2, False: 2]
  ------------------
  609|      4|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  610|       |                    {
  611|      2|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (611:32): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  612|      1|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  613|      1|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  614|      1|                            return true;
  615|      1|                        }
  616|      2|                    }
  617|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  618|      1|                }
  619|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  620|      1|            }
  621|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  622|      6|        }
  623|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  624|       |
  625|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  626|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  627|     11|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (627:12): [True: 1, False: 10]
  ------------------
  628|      1|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  629|     10|        }
  630|       |
  631|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  632|     10|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  633|     10|        let job = RasterJob {
  634|     10|            code_hash,
  635|     10|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  636|     10|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (636:22): [True: 9, False: 1]
  ------------------
  637|      9|                Some(code.to_string())
  638|       |            } else {
  639|      1|                None
  640|       |            },
  641|     10|            svg: cached_svg,
  642|     10|            dark_mode,
  643|     10|            width_bucket,
  644|     10|            scale_bucket,
  645|       |        };
  646|       |
  647|     10|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  648|      8|            Ok(()) => {
  649|      8|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  650|      8|            }
  651|      1|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  652|      1|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  653|      1|            }
  654|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  655|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  656|      1|                self.errors
  657|      1|                    .borrow_mut()
  658|      1|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  659|      1|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  660|      1|                return true;
  661|       |            }
  662|       |        }
  663|       |
  664|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  665|      9|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  666|     14|    }
  667|       |
  668|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  669|       |    // Helpers
  670|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  671|       |
  672|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  673|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  674|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  675|     12|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  676|     12|        &self,
  677|     12|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  678|     12|        code_hash: u64,
  679|     12|        available_width: f32,
  680|     12|    ) -> bool {
  681|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  682|     12|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
                                  ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (682:16): [True: 3, False: 9]
  ------------------
  683|      3|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (683:20): [True: 1, False: 2]
  ------------------
  684|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  685|      1|                return true;
  686|      2|            }
  687|      9|        }
  688|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  689|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  690|     11|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  691|     11|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  692|     11|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  693|     11|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  694|     11|        );
  695|     11|        ui.painter().text(
  696|     11|            rect.center(),
  697|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  698|       |            "Rendering...",
  699|     11|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  700|     11|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  701|       |        );
  702|     11|        true
  703|     12|    }
  704|       |
  705|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  706|       |    /// Key format: `pikchr:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  707|     14|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  708|     14|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^10                     ^4
  709|     10|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  710|     10|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  711|     14|    }
  712|       |
  713|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  714|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  715|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  716|      8|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  717|      8|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  718|      8|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  719|      8|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  720|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  721|      2|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  722|       |            // release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  723|      2|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  724|       |        }
  725|      8|    }
  726|       |
  727|       |    /// Render Pikchr source code to an SVG string.
  728|       |    ///
  729|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  730|       |    /// Uses the pikchr crate's builder-pattern API for flags.
  731|     47|    fn render_pikchr_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  732|       |        use pikchr::{Pikchr, PikchrFlags};
  733|       |
  734|     47|        let mut flags = PikchrFlags::default();
  735|     47|        flags.generate_plain_errors();
  736|     47|        if dark_mode {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (736:12): [True: 6, False: 41]
  ------------------
  737|      6|            flags.use_dark_mode();
  738|     41|        }
  739|       |
  740|     47|        Pikchr::render(code, None, flags).map(|svg| svg.to_string())
                                                                  ^40 ^40
  741|       |        // Pikchr::render already returns Err(String); no map_err needed.
  742|     47|    }
  743|       |
  744|       |    /// Inject a `font-family` declaration into a Pikchr SVG string.
  745|       |    ///
  746|       |    /// Pikchr outputs `<text>` elements with no `font-family` attribute,
  747|       |    /// causing usvg to fall back to a potentially thin default font.
  748|       |    /// We prepend a `font-family` to the existing `<svg style='...'>`
  749|       |    /// so all text inherits a known good sans-serif font.
  750|     12|    fn inject_svg_font_family(svg: &str) -> String {
  751|       |        // Pikchr outputs: <svg ... style='font-size:initial;' ...>
  752|       |        // Inject font-family before the existing font-size declaration.
  753|     12|        if let Some(pos) = svg.find("style='font-size:") {
                                  ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (753:16): [True: 6, False: 6]
  ------------------
  754|      6|            let insert_at = pos + "style='".len();
  755|      6|            let mut result = String::with_capacity(svg.len() + 60);
  756|      6|            result.push_str(&svg[..insert_at]);
  757|      6|            result.push_str("font-family:\"Segoe UI\",\"Helvetica Neue\",Arial,sans-serif;");
  758|      6|            result.push_str(&svg[insert_at..]);
  759|      6|            result
  760|       |        } else {
  761|      6|            svg.to_string()
  762|       |        }
  763|     12|    }
  764|       |
  765|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  766|      3|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &PikchrTextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  767|      3|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  768|      3|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (768:24): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------
  769|      2|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  770|       |        } else {
  771|      1|            1.0
  772|       |        };
  773|      3|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  774|      3|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  775|      3|    }
  776|       |
  777|       |    /// Display a Pikchr error block with the error message and original source code.
  778|      2|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  779|      2|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  780|      2|        egui::Frame::none()
  781|      2|            .fill(tc.box_bg)
  782|      2|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  783|      2|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  784|      2|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  785|      2|                ui.label(
  786|      2|                    egui::RichText::new("Pikchr rendering error:")
  787|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  788|      2|                        .size(code_font_size),
  789|       |                );
  790|      2|                ui.label(
  791|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(error)
  792|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::LIGHT_GRAY)
  793|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  794|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  795|       |                );
  796|      2|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  797|      2|                ui.label(
  798|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  799|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  800|      2|                        .color(tc.code_fallback_text)
  801|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  802|       |                );
  803|      2|            });
  804|      2|    }
  805|       |}
  806|       |
  807|       |impl Drop for PikchrRenderer {
  808|    499|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  809|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  810|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  811|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  812|    499|        self.job_tx.take();
  813|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  814|    499|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
                                  ^480
  ------------------
  |  Branch (814:16): [True: 480, False: 19]
  ------------------
  815|    480|            let _ = handle.join();
  816|    480|        }
                      ^19
  817|    499|    }
  818|       |}

  831|       |            screen_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
  832|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
  833|       |                egui::vec2(width, height),
  834|       |            )),
  835|       |            ..Default::default()
  836|       |        }
  837|       |    }

  839|       |    fn test_renderer_with_channels(
  840|       |        job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  841|       |        result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  842|       |    ) -> PikchrRenderer {
  843|       |        PikchrRenderer {
  844|       |            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  845|       |            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  846|       |            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  847|       |            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  848|       |            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  849|       |            job_tx,
  850|       |            result_rx,
  851|       |            worker_handle: None,

  853|       |            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),

  856|       |
  857|       |    fn insert_test_texture(
  858|       |        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  859|       |        renderer: &PikchrRenderer,
  860|       |        key: &str,
  861|       |        display_size: [u32; 2],
  862|       |    ) {
  863|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], egui::Color32::WHITE);
  864|       |        let texture =
  865|       |            ui.ctx()
  866|       |                .load_texture(key.to_string(), image, egui::TextureOptions::default());
  867|       |        renderer.textures.borrow_mut().insert(
  868|       |            key.to_string(),
  869|       |            PikchrTextureEntry {
  870|       |                texture,
  871|       |                display_size,
  872|       |            },
  873|       |        );
  874|       |    }

  947|       |    #[test]
  948|       |    fn test_width_bucketing() {
  949|       |        // Same bucket for nearby widths (round() gives +/-16px half-buckets)
  950|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(641.0));
  951|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(655.0)); // 655/32=20.47 -> rounds to 20
  952|       |                                                              // Different buckets once we cross the half-bucket boundary
  953|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(656.0)); // 656/32=20.5 -> rounds to 21
  954|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(672.0)); // clearly different bucket
  955|       |                                                              // Verify actual bucket values
  956|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), 640); // 640/32=20.0 -> 20*32=640
  957|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(672.0), 672); // 672/32=21.0 -> 21*32=672
  958|       |    }
  959|       |
  960|       |    #[test]
  961|       |    fn test_renderer_new_creates_empty_caches() {
  962|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
  963|       |        // No panics, caches start empty
  964|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
  965|       |    }

  996|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false);

 1004|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1005|       |    // bucket_scale coverage
 1006|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1007|       |
 1008|       |    #[test]
 1009|       |    fn test_scale_bucketing() {
 1010|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.0), 100);
 1011|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.25), 125);
 1012|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.5), 150);
 1013|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(2.0), 200);
 1014|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(0.5), 50);
 1015|       |        // Rounding: 1.124 * 100 = 112.4 -> rounds to 112
 1016|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.124), 112);
 1017|       |        // Rounding: 1.126 * 100 = 112.6 -> rounds to 113
 1018|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.126), 113);
 1019|       |        // Values within rounding distance bucket together
 1020|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.001), bucket_scale(1.004)); // both round to 100
 1021|       |    }

 1032|       |            svg.contains("<polygon") || svg.contains("<path") || svg.contains("<rect"),

 1110|       |    #[test]
 1111|       |    fn test_spline_produces_path() {
 1112|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("spline right then down", false).unwrap();
 1113|       |        assert!(
 1114|       |            svg.contains("<path"),
 1115|       |            "spline should produce <path> element, got:\n{}",
 1116|       |            svg
 1117|       |        );
 1118|       |    }
 1119|       |
 1120|       |    #[test]
 1121|       |    fn test_file_shape_produces_svg() {
 1122|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("file \"f.txt\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1123|       |        assert!(svg.contains("<svg"), "file shape should produce valid SVG");
 1124|       |        assert!(svg.contains("f.txt"), "file label missing from SVG");
 1125|       |    }

 1128|       |    // SVG structure and dimension validation
 1129|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1130|       |
 1131|       |    #[test]
 1132|       |    fn test_svg_has_proper_structure() {
 1133|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1134|       |        assert!(svg.contains("<svg"), "SVG must have opening <svg tag");
 1135|       |        assert!(svg.contains("</svg>"), "SVG must have closing </svg> tag");
 1136|       |        assert!(
 1137|       |            svg.contains("viewBox") || (svg.contains("width") && svg.contains("height")),
 1138|       |            "SVG must have viewBox or width/height attributes"
 1139|       |        );
 1140|       |    }
 1141|       |
 1142|       |    #[test]
 1143|       |    fn test_svg_dimensions_are_reasonable() {
 1144|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"Test\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1145|       |        // Pikchr uses viewBox="x y width height" for dimensions
 1146|       |        let (w, h) =
 1147|       |            extract_viewbox_size(&svg).expect("SVG should have a parseable viewBox attribute");
 1148|       |        assert!(w > 0.0, "viewBox width must be > 0, got {}", w);
 1149|       |        assert!(h > 0.0, "viewBox height must be > 0, got {}", h);
 1150|       |        assert!(w < 10000.0, "viewBox width must be < 10000, got {}", w);
 1151|       |        assert!(h < 10000.0, "viewBox height must be < 10000, got {}", h);
 1152|       |    }
 1153|       |
 1154|       |    #[test]
 1155|       |    fn test_different_diagrams_produce_different_dimensions() {
 1156|       |        let small = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box", false).unwrap();
 1157|       |        let large = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(
 1158|       |            "box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box",
 1159|       |            false,
 1160|       |        )
 1161|       |        .unwrap();
 1162|       |        let (w_small, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&small).expect("small SVG should have viewBox");
 1163|       |        let (w_large, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&large).expect("large SVG should have viewBox");
 1164|       |        assert!(
 1165|       |            w_large > w_small,
 1166|       |            "Chain of boxes should be wider than single box: {} vs {}",
 1167|       |            w_large,
 1168|       |            w_small
 1169|       |        );
 1170|       |    }
 1171|       |
 1172|       |    /// Helper: extract width and height from an SVG `viewBox="x y w h"` attribute.
 1173|       |    fn extract_viewbox_size(svg: &str) -> Option<(f64, f64)> {
 1174|       |        let marker = "viewBox=\"";
 1175|       |        let start = svg.find(marker)? + marker.len();
 1176|       |        let rest = &svg[start..];
 1177|       |        let end = rest.find('"')?;
 1178|       |        let parts: Vec<f64> = rest[..end]
 1179|       |            .split_whitespace()
 1180|       |            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
 1181|       |            .collect();
 1182|       |        if parts.len() >= 4 {
 1183|       |            Some((parts[2], parts[3]))
 1184|       |        } else {
 1185|       |            None
 1186|       |        }
 1187|       |    }

 1193|       |    #[test]
 1194|       |    fn test_labels_preserved_in_svg() {

 1196|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false).unwrap();
 1197|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Alpha"), "Label 'Alpha' missing from SVG");
 1198|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Beta"), "Label 'Beta' missing from SVG");
 1199|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Gamma"), "Label 'Gamma' missing from SVG");
 1200|       |    }

 1211|       |    #[test]
 1212|       |    fn test_special_characters_in_labels() {
 1213|       |        // Test that angle brackets are XML-escaped in SVG output
 1214|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"x < y\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1215|       |        // Pikchr XML-escapes < to &lt; in text content
 1216|       |        assert!(
 1217|       |            svg.contains("&lt;"),
 1218|       |            "SVG should contain XML-escaped '<':\n{}",
 1219|       |            svg
 1220|       |        );
 1221|       |
 1222|       |        // Test ampersand: Pikchr XML-escapes & to &amp;
 1223|       |        let svg2 = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"A & B\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1224|       |        assert!(
 1225|       |            svg2.contains("&amp;"),
 1226|       |            "SVG should contain XML-escaped '&':\n{}",
 1227|       |            svg2
 1228|       |        );
 1229|       |    }

 1235|       |    #[test]
 1236|       |    fn test_error_message_has_content() {
 1237|       |        let err = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("this is not valid pikchr }{}{", false)
 1238|       |            .unwrap_err();
 1239|       |        assert!(
 1240|       |            !err.is_empty(),
 1241|       |            "Error message should not be empty for invalid syntax"
 1242|       |        );
 1243|       |        // Error should contain some useful diagnostic info
 1244|       |        assert!(
 1245|       |            err.len() > 5,
 1246|       |            "Error message should be descriptive, got: '{}'",
 1247|       |            err
 1248|       |        );
 1249|       |    }
 1250|       |
 1251|       |    #[test]
 1252|       |    fn test_error_cache_theme_independent() {
 1253|       |        // Errors are keyed by code_hash, not theme -- same syntax error in
 1254|       |        // light and dark mode should produce the same error message.
 1255|       |        let invalid = "totally broken pikchr syntax }{}{";
 1256|       |        let err_light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, false).unwrap_err();
 1257|       |        let err_dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, true).unwrap_err();
 1258|       |        assert_eq!(
 1259|       |            err_light, err_dark,
 1260|       |            "Same syntax error should produce same message regardless of theme"
 1261|       |        );
 1262|       |    }
 1263|       |
 1264|       |    #[test]
 1265|       |    fn test_multiple_errors_are_distinct() {
 1266|       |        let err1 =
 1267|       |            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("invalid syntax aaa }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1268|       |        let err2 =
 1269|       |            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("different invalid bbb }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1270|       |        // Different invalid inputs may produce different error messages
 1271|       |        // (at minimum they should both be errors, which we've already asserted
 1272|       |        // by unwrap_err). If they happen to be the same generic error, that's
 1273|       |        // still valid -- but let's verify they're both non-empty.
 1274|       |        assert!(!err1.is_empty(), "Error 1 should not be empty");
 1275|       |        assert!(!err2.is_empty(), "Error 2 should not be empty");
 1276|       |    }
 1277|       |
 1278|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1279|       |    // Dark mode output inspection
 1280|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1281|       |
 1282|       |    #[test]
 1283|       |    fn test_dark_mode_inverts_stroke_colors() {
 1284|       |        let light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1285|       |        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", true).unwrap();
 1286|       |
 1287|       |        // Pikchr uses PIKCHR_DARK_MODE flag to invert colors.
 1288|       |        // Light mode should contain dark strokes (e.g., "black" or rgb(0,0,0))
 1289|       |        // Dark mode should contain light strokes (e.g., "white" or rgb(255,255,255))
 1290|       |        let light_has_black =
 1291|       |            light.contains("black") || light.contains("rgb(0,0,0)") || light.contains("#000");
 1292|       |        let dark_has_white =
 1293|       |            dark.contains("white") || dark.contains("rgb(255,255,255)") || dark.contains("#fff");
 1294|       |
 1295|       |        assert!(
 1296|       |            light_has_black,
 1297|       |            "Light mode SVG should reference dark colors:\n{}",
 1298|       |            light
 1299|       |        );
 1300|       |        assert!(
 1301|       |            dark_has_white,
 1302|       |            "Dark mode SVG should reference light colors:\n{}",
 1303|       |            dark
 1304|       |        );
 1305|       |    }
 1306|       |
 1307|       |    #[test]
 1308|       |    fn test_dark_mode_preserves_labels() {
 1309|       |        let label = "DarkTest";
 1310|       |        let code = format!("box \"{}\" fit", label);
 1311|       |        let light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, false).unwrap();
 1312|       |        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, true).unwrap();
 1313|       |        assert!(
 1314|       |            light.contains(label),
 1315|       |            "Light mode should contain label '{}'",
 1316|       |            label
 1317|       |        );
 1318|       |        assert!(
 1319|       |            dark.contains(label),
 1320|       |            "Dark mode should contain label '{}'",
 1321|       |            label
 1322|       |        );
 1323|       |    }

 1329|       |    #[test]
 1330|       |    fn test_large_diagram_renders() {
 1331|       |        // Build a chain of 50+ boxes connected by arrows
 1332|       |        let mut code = String::from("box \"B0\" fit\n");
 1333|       |        for i in 1..=50 {
 1334|       |            code.push_str(&format!("arrow right\nbox \"B{}\" fit\n", i));
 1335|       |        }
 1336|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, false);
 1337|       |        assert!(
 1338|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1339|       |            "Large diagram (50+ objects) should render: {:?}",
 1340|       |            result.err()
 1341|       |        );
 1342|       |        let svg = result.unwrap();
 1343|       |        assert!(svg.contains("B0"), "First box label missing");
 1344|       |        assert!(svg.contains("B50"), "Last box label missing");
 1345|       |    }
 1346|       |
 1347|       |    #[test]
 1348|       |    fn test_whitespace_only_input() {
 1349|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("   \n  \n  ", false);
 1350|       |        // Whitespace-only should behave like empty input (OK, not error)
 1351|       |        assert!(
 1352|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1353|       |            "Whitespace-only input should not error: {:?}",
 1354|       |            result.err()
 1355|       |        );
 1356|       |    }
 1357|       |
 1358|       |    #[test]
 1359|       |    fn test_comments_in_pikchr() {
 1360|       |        let code = "# This is a comment\nbox \"Visible\" fit\n# Another comment";
 1361|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false);
 1362|       |        assert!(
 1363|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1364|       |            "Comments should not cause errors: {:?}",
 1365|       |            result.err()
 1366|       |        );
 1367|       |        let svg = result.unwrap();
 1368|       |        assert!(
 1369|       |            svg.contains("Visible"),
 1370|       |            "Label should appear despite comments"
 1371|       |        );
 1372|       |        // Comments should NOT appear in SVG output
 1373|       |        assert!(
 1374|       |            !svg.contains("This is a comment"),
 1375|       |            "Comment text should not leak into SVG output"
 1376|       |        );
 1377|       |    }

 1379|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1380|       |    // Output snapshot / characterization test
 1381|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1382|       |
 1383|       |    #[test]
 1384|       |    fn test_svg_output_snapshot() {
 1385|       |        // Render a known diagram and validate specific SVG structural fragments.
 1386|       |        // This acts as a characterization test documenting what the output looks like.
 1387|       |        let code = r#"arrow right 200% "Input" above
 1388|       |box "Process" fit rad 10px
 1389|       |arrow right 200% "Output" above"#;
 1390|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false).unwrap();
 1391|       |
 1392|       |        // Must have proper SVG wrapper
 1393|       |        assert!(svg.starts_with("<svg"), "SVG must start with <svg tag");
 1394|       |        assert!(
 1395|       |            svg.trim_end().ends_with("</svg>"),
 1396|       |            "SVG must end with </svg>"
 1397|       |        );
 1398|       |
 1399|       |        // Must have viewBox for proper scaling
 1400|       |        assert!(svg.contains("viewBox"), "SVG should have viewBox attribute");
 1401|       |
 1402|       |        // Arrow produces polygon elements (arrowheads)
 1403|       |        assert!(
 1404|       |            svg.contains("<polygon"),
 1405|       |            "Arrows should produce <polygon> arrowheads"
 1406|       |        );
 1407|       |
 1408|       |        // Labels appear as text elements
 1409|       |        assert!(
 1410|       |            svg.contains("<text"),
 1411|       |            "Labels should produce <text> elements"
 1412|       |        );
 1413|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Input"), "Label 'Input' must be in SVG");
 1414|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Process"), "Label 'Process' must be in SVG");
 1415|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Output"), "Label 'Output' must be in SVG");
 1416|       |
 1417|       |        // Lines appear as path or line elements
 1418|       |        assert!(
 1419|       |            svg.contains("<line") || svg.contains("<path"),
 1420|       |            "Connectors should produce <line> or <path> elements"
 1421|       |        );
 1422|       |    }

 1429|       |    fn test_inject_svg_font_family_adds_declaration() {
 1430|       |        let svg = "<svg style='font-size:initial;' viewBox='0 0 100 50'></svg>";
 1431|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
 1432|       |        assert!(
 1433|       |            result.contains("font-family:"),
 1434|       |            "Should inject font-family declaration"
 1435|       |        );
 1436|       |        assert!(
 1437|       |            result.contains("sans-serif"),
 1438|       |            "Should include sans-serif fallback"
 1439|       |        );
 1440|       |        // Original content preserved
 1441|       |        assert!(
 1442|       |            result.contains("font-size:initial"),
 1443|       |            "Original style should be preserved"
 1444|       |        );
 1445|       |        assert!(
 1446|       |            result.contains("viewBox"),
 1447|       |            "Other attributes should be preserved"
 1448|       |        );
 1449|       |    }

 1463|       |    fn test_supersample_constant_is_reasonable() {

 1465|       |        const {
 1466|       |            assert!(
 1467|       |                PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE <= 4.0,

 1469|       |            )
 1470|       |        };

 1476|       |
 1477|       |    #[test]
 1478|       |    fn test_parse_width_from_texture_key() {
 1479|       |        assert_eq!(
 1480|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:0000000000003039:w640:s100:dm0"),
 1481|       |            Some(640)
 1482|       |        );
 1483|       |        assert_eq!(
 1484|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:w1024:s150:dm1"),
 1485|       |            Some(1024)
 1486|       |        );
 1487|       |        assert_eq!(
 1488|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:w0:s100:dm0"),
 1489|       |            Some(0)
 1490|       |        );
 1491|       |        // No :w prefix
 1492|       |        assert_eq!(
 1493|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:s100:dm0"),
 1494|       |            None
 1495|       |        );
 1496|       |    }

 1502|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
 1503|       |    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
 1504|       |        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
 1505|       |        db.load_system_fonts();
 1506|       |        Arc::new(db)
 1507|       |    }
 1508|       |
 1509|       |    #[test]
 1510|       |    fn test_render_block_basic_and_pending_placeholder() {
 1511|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 1512|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1513|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1514|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1515|       |        let mut rendered_first = false;
 1516|       |        let mut rendered_second = false;
 1517|       |
 1518|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1519|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1520|       |                rendered_first = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1521|       |                rendered_second = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1522|       |            });
 1523|       |        });
 1524|       |
 1525|       |        assert!(rendered_first);
 1526|       |        assert!(rendered_second);
 1527|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&hash_str(code)));
 1528|       |    }
 1529|       |
 1530|       |    #[test]
 1531|       |    fn test_render_block_reports_cached_error() {
 1532|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1533|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1534|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1535|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1536|       |        renderer
 1537|       |            .errors
 1538|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1539|       |            .insert(code_hash, "boom".to_string());
 1540|       |
 1541|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1542|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1543|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1544|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1545|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1546|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1547|       |            });
 1548|       |        });
 1549|       |
 1550|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1551|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1552|       |    }
 1553|       |
 1554|       |    #[test]
 1555|       |    fn test_render_block_enqueues_cached_svg() {
 1556|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1557|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1558|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1559|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1560|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1561|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1562|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, false),
 1563|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1564|       |        );
 1565|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1566|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, true),
 1567|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1568|       |        );
 1569|       |
 1570|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1571|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1572|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1573|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1574|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1575|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1576|       |            });
 1577|       |        });
 1578|       |
 1579|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1580|       |        let request = job_rx
 1581|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1))
 1582|       |            .expect("request");
 1583|       |        assert!(request.svg.is_some());
 1584|       |        assert!(request.code.is_none());
 1585|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1586|       |    }
 1587|       |
 1588|       |    #[test]
 1589|       |    fn test_render_block_queue_full_keeps_rendering() {
 1590|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1591|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1592|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx.clone()), result_rx);
 1593|       |        job_tx
 1594|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1595|       |                code_hash: 1,
 1596|       |                texture_key: "pikchr:full:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1597|       |                code: Some("box".to_string()),
 1598|       |                svg: None,
 1599|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1600|       |                width_bucket: 224,
 1601|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1602|       |            })
 1603|       |            .expect("fill queue");
 1604|       |
 1605|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1606|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1607|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1608|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1609|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1610|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, "box", 1.0, 14.0);
 1611|       |            });
 1612|       |        });
 1613|       |
 1614|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1615|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1616|       |        drop(job_rx);
 1617|       |    }

 1641|       |    #[test]
 1642|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_and_uses_cached_texture() {
 1643|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(2);
 1644|       |        let (job_tx, _job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1645|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1646|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1647|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1648|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1649|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(999);
 1650|       |        let result_tx = RefCell::new(Some(result_tx));
 1651|       |        let texture_key = RefCell::new(String::new());
 1652|       |
 1653|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1654|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1655|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1656|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1657|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1658|       |                let width_bucket = bucket_width(ui.available_width().max(1.0));
 1659|       |                let key = format!(
 1660|       |                    "pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
 1661|       |                    code_hash,
 1662|       |                    width_bucket,
 1663|       |                    100,
 1664|       |                    ui.visuals().dark_mode as u8
 1665|       |                );
 1666|       |                *texture_key.borrow_mut() = key.clone();
 1667|       |                renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
 1668|       |                if let Some(tx) = result_tx.borrow_mut().take() {
 1669|       |                    tx.send(RasterResult {
 1670|       |                        code_hash,
 1671|       |                        texture_key: key,
 1672|       |                        dark_mode: ui.visuals().dark_mode,
 1673|       |                        svg: Some(
 1674|       |                            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1675|       |                                .to_string(),
 1676|       |                        ),
 1677|       |                        rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1678|       |                        raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1679|       |                        display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1680|       |                        error: None,
 1681|       |                    })
 1682|       |                    .expect("send result");
 1683|       |                }
 1684|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1685|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1686|       |            });
 1687|       |        });
 1688|       |
 1689|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1690|       |        let texture_key = texture_key.into_inner();
 1691|       |        assert!(renderer.textures.borrow_mut().get(&texture_key).is_some());
 1692|       |        assert!(renderer
 1693|       |            .svg_cache
 1694|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1695|       |            .get(&svg_cache_key(code_hash, ctx.style().visuals.dark_mode))
 1696|       |            .is_some());
 1697|       |        assert_eq!(
 1698|       |            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash),
 1699|       |            Some(&texture_key)
 1700|       |        );
 1701|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1702|       |    }

 1885|       |    #[test]
 1886|       |    fn test_render_block_debounce_without_stale_texture_enqueues_job() {
 1887|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1888|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1889|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1890|       |        let code = "box \"A\"\narrow right 50\nbox \"B\"";
 1891|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1892|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1893|       |            code_hash,
 1894|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1895|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1896|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1897|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1898|       |            },
 1899|       |        );
 1900|       |
 1901|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1902|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1903|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1904|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1905|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1906|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1907|       |            });
 1908|       |        });
 1909|       |
 1910|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1911|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 1912|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1913|       |        assert_eq!(
 1914|       |            renderer
 1915|       |                .debounce
 1916|       |                .borrow()
 1917|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1918|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1919|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1920|       |        );
 1921|       |    }

 1923|       |    #[test]
 1924|       |    fn test_render_block_uses_stale_texture_during_debounce() {
 1925|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1926|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1927|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1928|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1929|       |        let stale_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w224:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1930|       |        renderer
 1931|       |            .latest_texture_key
 1932|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1933|       |            .insert(code_hash, stale_key.clone());
 1934|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1935|       |            code_hash,
 1936|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1937|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1938|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1939|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1940|       |            },
 1941|       |        );
 1942|       |
 1943|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1944|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1945|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1946|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1947|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1948|       |                insert_test_texture(ui, &renderer, &stale_key, [320, 160]);
 1949|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1950|       |            });
 1951|       |        });
 1952|       |
 1953|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1954|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1955|       |        assert_eq!(
 1956|       |            renderer
 1957|       |                .debounce
 1958|       |                .borrow()
 1959|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1960|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1961|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1962|       |        );
 1963|       |    }

 2062|       |    #[test]
 2063|       |    fn test_render_block_expired_debounce_enqueues_job() {
 2064|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 2065|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 2066|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 2067|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 2068|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 2069|       |        let width_bucket = bucket_width(96.0);
 2070|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 2071|       |            code_hash,
 2072|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 2073|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 2074|       |                last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
 2075|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(
 2076|       |                    Instant::now() - Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS + 5),
 2077|       |                ),
 2078|       |            },
 2079|       |        );
 2080|       |
 2081|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 2082|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 2083|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 2084|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 2085|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 2086|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 2087|       |            });
 2088|       |        });
 2089|       |
 2090|       |        assert!(rendered);
 2091|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 2092|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 2093|       |    }
