 2199|       |    #[test]
 2200|       |    fn test_worker_processes_job() {
 2201|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 2202|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 2203|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 2204|       |
 2205|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 2206|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 2207|       |        let texture_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 2208|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 2209|       |            code_hash,
 2210|       |            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
 2211|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 2212|       |            svg: None,
 2213|       |            dark_mode: false,
 2214|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 2215|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 2216|       |        };
 2217|       |
 2218|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 2219|       |        // Drop sender so worker_loop exits after processing the one job.
 2220|       |        drop(job_tx);
 2221|       |
 2222|       |        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 2223|       |
 2224|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).unwrap();
 2225|       |        assert!(result.rgba.is_some(), "Worker should produce RGBA data");
 2226|       |        assert!(
 2227|       |            result.raster_size.is_some(),
 2228|       |            "Worker should produce raster_size"
 2229|       |        );
 2230|       |        assert!(
 2231|       |            result.display_size.is_some(),
 2232|       |            "Worker should produce display_size"
 2233|       |        );
 2234|       |        assert!(result.error.is_none(), "Worker should not produce an error");
 2235|       |        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "Worker should produce SVG on success");
 2236|       |    }
 2237|       |

 2240|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 2241|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 2242|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 2243|       |
 2244|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 2245|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);

 2247|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 2248|       |            code_hash,
 2249|       |            texture_key,
 2250|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 2251|       |            svg: None,
 2252|       |            dark_mode: false,
 2253|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 2254|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 2255|       |        };
 2256|       |
 2257|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();

 2259|       |
 2260|       |        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);

 2273|       |    fn test_worker_caches_error() {
 2274|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 2275|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 2276|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 2277|       |
 2278|       |        let code = "invalid syntax }{}{";
 2279|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 2280|       |        let texture_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 2281|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 2282|       |            code_hash,
 2283|       |            texture_key,
 2284|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 2285|       |            svg: None,
 2286|       |            dark_mode: false,
 2287|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 2288|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 2289|       |        };
 2290|       |
 2291|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 2292|       |        drop(job_tx);
 2293|       |
 2294|       |        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 2295|       |
 2296|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).unwrap();

 2298|       |            result.error.is_some(),
 2299|       |            "Worker should produce an error for invalid code"

 2303|       |            "Worker should not produce RGBA for invalid code"
 2304|       |        );

 2307|       |            "Worker should not produce SVG for invalid code"
 2308|       |        );

 2311|       |    #[test]
 2312|       |    fn test_is_stale_result_width_mismatch() {
 2313|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 2314|       |        let code_hash = 12345u64;
 2315|       |
 2316|       |        // Insert wanted_bucket: code_hash -> width 640
 2317|       |        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, 640);
 2318|       |
 2319|       |        // Texture key with w1024 should be stale (mismatch)
 2320|       |        let stale_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w1024:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 2321|       |        assert!(
 2322|       |            renderer.is_stale_result(&stale_key, code_hash),
 2323|       |            "Width 1024 vs wanted 640 should be stale"
 2324|       |        );
 2325|       |
 2326|       |        // Texture key with w640 should be fresh (match)
 2327|       |        let fresh_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 2328|       |        assert!(
 2329|       |            !renderer.is_stale_result(&fresh_key, code_hash),
 2330|       |            "Width 640 vs wanted 640 should be fresh"
 2331|       |        );
 2332|       |    }

 2407|       |            "wanted_bucket should be empty after release_gpu_textures"

 2418|       |
 2419|       |    #[test]
 2420|       |    fn test_queue_full_does_not_block() {
 2421|       |        let (tx, _rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(2);
 2422|       |
 2423|       |        // Fill the channel with 2 dummy jobs
 2424|       |        for i in 0..2 {
 2425|       |            tx.send(RasterJob {
 2426|       |                code_hash: i,
 2427|       |                texture_key: format!("pikchr:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:{}", i),
 2428|       |                code: Some("box".to_string()),
 2429|       |                svg: None,
 2430|       |                dark_mode: false,
 2431|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 2432|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 2433|       |            })
 2434|       |            .unwrap();
 2435|       |        }
 2436|       |
 2437|       |        // Third send should fail with Full, not block
 2438|       |        let result = tx.try_send(RasterJob {
 2439|       |            code_hash: 99,
 2440|       |            texture_key: "pikchr:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:99".to_string(),
 2441|       |            code: Some("box".to_string()),
 2442|       |            svg: None,
 2443|       |            dark_mode: false,
 2444|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 2445|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 2446|       |        });
 2447|       |        assert!(
 2448|       |            matches!(result, Err(TrySendError::Full(_))),
 2449|       |            "try_send on full channel should return Err(Full), got {:?}",
 2450|       |            result.err()
 2451|       |        );
 2452|       |    }
 2453|       |
 2454|       |    #[test]
 2455|       |    fn test_shutdown_joins_worker() {
 2456|       |        let start = Instant::now();
 2457|       |        {
 2458|       |            let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 2459|       |            // Drop renderer — should close channels and join worker
 2460|       |            drop(renderer);
 2461|       |        }
 2462|       |        let elapsed = start.elapsed();
 2463|       |        assert!(
 2464|       |            elapsed < Duration::from_secs(5),
 2465|       |            "Drop should join worker within 5 seconds, took {:?}",
 2466|       |            elapsed
 2467|       |        );
 2468|       |    }
 2469|       |
 2470|       |    #[test]
 2471|       |    fn test_pending_dedup_prevents_double_enqueue() {
 2472|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 2473|       |        let code_hash = 42u64;
 2474|       |
 2475|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 2476|       |
 2477|       |        // The dedup check in render_block uses contains()
 2478|       |        assert!(
 2479|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash),
 2480|       |            "Pending set should contain the inserted code_hash"
 2481|       |        );
 2482|       |
 2483|       |        // Inserting again is a no-op on HashSet
 2484|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 2485|       |        assert_eq!(
 2486|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().len(),
 2487|       |            1,
 2488|       |            "HashSet dedup: inserting same hash twice should keep len at 1"
 2489|       |        );
 2490|       |    }
 2491|       |
 2492|       |    #[test]
 2493|       |    fn test_worker_try_send_full_does_not_block() {
 2494|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 2495|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 2496|       |        // Result channel with capacity 1
 2497|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(1);
 2498|       |
 2499|       |        // Fill the result channel with a dummy result
 2500|       |        result_tx
 2501|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 2502|       |                code_hash: 0,
 2503|       |                texture_key: "dummy".to_string(),
 2504|       |                dark_mode: false,
 2505|       |                svg: None,
 2506|       |                rgba: None,
 2507|       |                raster_size: None,
 2508|       |                display_size: None,
 2509|       |                error: None,
 2510|       |            })
 2511|       |            .unwrap();
 2512|       |
 2513|       |        // Submit a job that will produce a result the worker can't send (channel full)
 2514|       |        let code = "box \"Test\" fit";
 2515|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 2516|       |        job_tx
 2517|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 2518|       |                code_hash,
 2519|       |                texture_key: format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash),
 2520|       |                code: Some(code.to_string()),
 2521|       |                svg: None,
 2522|       |                dark_mode: false,
 2523|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 2524|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 2525|       |            })
 2526|       |            .unwrap();
 2527|       |        // Drop job_tx so worker_loop exits after processing the one job
 2528|       |        drop(job_tx);
 2529|       |
 2530|       |        // Run worker_loop on a spawned thread with a timeout to detect hangs
 2531|       |        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {
 2532|       |            PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 2533|       |        });
 2534|       |
 2535|       |        // Wait up to 5 seconds for the worker to finish
 2536|       |        let start = Instant::now();
 2537|       |        loop {
 2538|       |            if handle.is_finished() {
 2539|       |                break;
 2540|       |            }
 2541|       |            if start.elapsed() > Duration::from_secs(5) {
 2542|       |                panic!("worker_loop blocked for >5s — try_send likely blocked instead of dropping the result");
 2543|       |            }
 2544|       |            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
 2545|       |        }
 2546|       |        handle.join().unwrap();
 2547|       |
 2548|       |        // The result channel should still contain the original dummy result
 2549|       |        let dummy = result_rx.try_recv().unwrap();
 2550|       |        assert_eq!(
 2551|       |            dummy.code_hash, 0,
 2552|       |            "Original dummy result should still be in the channel"
 2553|       |        );
 2554|       |    }
 2555|       |
 2556|       |    #[test]
 2557|       |    fn test_process_job_with_cached_svg() {
 2558|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 2559|       |        let valid_svg = r#"<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 50"><rect width="100" height="50" fill="red"/></svg>"#;
 2560|       |        let code_hash = hash_str("cached_diagram");
 2561|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 2562|       |            code_hash,
 2563|       |            texture_key: format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash),
 2564|       |            code: None, // No source code — using cached SVG
 2565|       |            svg: Some(valid_svg.to_string()),
 2566|       |            dark_mode: false,
 2567|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 2568|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 2569|       |        };
 2570|       |
 2571|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::process_job(&fontdb, job);

C:\language\mdmdview\src\shell_integration.rs:
    1|       |// Shell integration utilities for OS-level file operations.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// Provides cross-platform functions for interacting with the operating system's
    4|       |// file manager and clipboard at the file level:
    5|       |//
    6|       |// - `clipboard_copy_file`: Places a file path on the system clipboard as a
    7|       |//   file copy operation (CF_HDROP on Windows, osascript on macOS, xclip on
    8|       |//   Linux). This allows the user to paste the file into Explorer/Finder/etc.
    9|       |//
   10|       |// - `reveal_in_file_manager`: Opens the native file manager (Explorer, Finder,
   11|       |//   or xdg-open) with the specified file selected/highlighted.
   12|       |//
   13|       |// These functions are used by the application's File menu to provide
   14|       |// shell operations on the currently open file. Each function uses platform-specific
   15|       |// implementations gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` attributes and returns
   16|       |// `anyhow::Result` for consistent error handling.
   17|       |
   18|       |use std::path::Path;
   19|       |

   98|       |pub fn clipboard_copy_file(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   99|       |    #[cfg(test)]
  100|       |    if take_forced_clipboard_copy_error() {
  101|       |        anyhow::bail!("forced clipboard error");
  102|       |    }
  103|       |
  104|       |    let abs_path = path.canonicalize()?;
  105|       |    // Escape backslashes and double-quotes for AppleScript string literal
  106|       |    let escaped = abs_path
  107|       |        .display()
  108|       |        .to_string()

  110|       |        .replace('"', "\\\"");
  111|       |    let script = format!("set the clipboard to (POSIX file \"{}\")", escaped);
  112|       |    let output = std::process::Command::new("osascript")
  113|       |        .arg("-e")
  114|       |        .arg(&script)
  115|       |        .output()?;

  118|       |            "osascript failed: {}",
  119|       |            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr)
  120|       |        );
  121|       |    }
  122|       |    Ok(())

  125|       |/// Place a file on the system clipboard as a file copy (Linux via xclip).
  126|       |#[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
  127|       |pub fn clipboard_copy_file(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
  128|       |    #[cfg(test)]
  129|       |    if take_forced_clipboard_copy_error() {
  130|       |        anyhow::bail!("forced clipboard error");
  131|       |    }
  132|       |
  133|       |    let abs_path = path.canonicalize()?;
  134|       |    let uri = format!("file://{}\n", abs_path.display());
  135|       |    let mut child = std::process::Command::new("xclip")
  136|       |        .args(["-selection", "clipboard", "-t", "text/uri-list"])
  137|       |        .stdin(std::process::Stdio::piped())
  138|       |        .spawn()
  139|       |        .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("xclip not found. Install with: sudo apt install xclip"))?;
  140|       |    if let Some(stdin) = child.stdin.as_mut() {
  141|       |        use std::io::Write;
  142|       |        stdin.write_all(uri.as_bytes())?;
  143|       |    }
  144|       |    let status = child.wait()?;
  145|       |    if !status.success() {
  146|       |        anyhow::bail!("xclip exited with status {}", status);
  147|       |    }
  148|       |    Ok(())
  149|       |}

  484|       |/// Check if a character is emoji-like (for column width estimation).
  485|       |/// Covers common emoji Unicode blocks including:
  486|       |/// - Miscellaneous Symbols (2600-26FF)
  487|       |/// - Dingbats (2700-27BF)
  488|       |/// - Emoticons (1F600-1F64F)
  489|       |/// - Transport and Map (1F680-1F6FF)
  490|       |/// - Miscellaneous Symbols and Pictographs (1F300-1F5FF)
  491|       |/// - Supplemental Symbols and Pictographs (1F900-1F9FF)
  492|       |/// - Symbols and Pictographs Extended-A (1FA00-1FAFF)
  493|  1.27k|fn is_emoji_like(c: char) -> bool {
  494|  1.27k|    let cp = c as u32;
  495|     14|    matches!(
  496|  1.27k|        cp,
  497|     24|        0x2600..=0x26FF     // Miscellaneous Symbols
  498|     22|        | 0x2700..=0x27BF   // Dingbats
  499|     10|        | 0x1F300..=0x1F5FF // Miscellaneous Symbols and Pictographs
  500|     10|        | 0x1F600..=0x1F64F // Emoticons (smileys)
  501|      6|        | 0x1F680..=0x1F6FF // Transport and Map Symbols
  502|      4|        | 0x1F900..=0x1F9FF // Supplemental Symbols and Pictographs
  503|      2|        | 0x1FA00..=0x1FAFF // Symbols and Pictographs Extended-A
  504|       |    )
  505|  1.27k|}

  576|       |mod tests {

  578|       |

  582|       |
  583|       |    #[test]
  584|       |    fn classify_version_column() {
  585|       |        let headers = vec![vec![span("Version")], vec![span("Changes")]];
  586|       |        let rows: Vec<Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>>> = Vec::new();

  591|       |        assert!(matches!(specs[1].policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. }));
  592|       |    }
  593|       |
  594|       |    #[test]
  595|       |    fn classify_author_column() {
  596|       |        let headers = vec![vec![span("Author")], vec![span("Examples")]];
  597|       |        let rows: Vec<Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>>> = Vec::new();

  599|       |        let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(&headers, &rows, &stats, 14.0, 0);
  600|       |        let specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
  601|       |        assert!(matches!(specs[0].policy, ColumnPolicy::Resizable { .. }));
  602|       |        assert!(matches!(specs[1].policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. }));
  603|       |    }

  652|       |            vec![span("Left")],
  653|       |            vec![span("Right")],
  654|       |        ];

  656|       |            vec![span("Centered label with enough words to raise score")],
  657|       |            vec![span("L1")],
  658|       |            vec![span("R1")],
  659|       |        ]];
  660|       |        let stats = compute_column_stats(&headers, &rows, 32);

  662|       |        let specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);

  664|       |        assert!(specs[1..]
  665|       |            .iter()
  666|       |            .any(|spec| matches!(spec.policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. })));
  667|       |    }

  715|       |                    has_link: false,

  717|       |                    has_emoji_like: false,
  718|       |                },

  721|       |                max_graphemes: 5,

  724|       |                    has_link: false,
  725|       |                    has_image: false,
  726|       |                    has_emoji_like: false,
  727|       |                },

  732|       |                rich_content: RichContentFlags {
  733|       |                    has_link: false,
  734|       |                    has_image: false,
  735|       |                    has_emoji_like: false,
  736|       |                },
  737|       |            },
  738|       |        ];

  740|       |        let specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
  741|       |        assert!(matches!(specs[0].policy, ColumnPolicy::Fixed { .. }));

  759|       |    fn multiple_remainder_columns_allowed() {
  760|       |        let headers = vec![
  761|       |            vec![span("Summary")],
  762|       |            vec![span("Details")],
  763|       |            vec![span("Examples")],
  764|       |        ];
  765|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
  766|       |            vec![span("short")],
  767|       |            vec![span(
  768|       |                "Long content that should trigger a remainder column due to its width and words.",
  769|       |            )],
  770|       |            vec![span(
  771|       |                "Another large column with links https://example.com and more text.",
  772|       |            )],
  773|       |        ]];
  774|       |        let stats = compute_column_stats(&headers, &rows, 32);
  775|       |        let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(&headers, &rows, &stats, 14.0, 0);
  776|       |        let specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
  777|       |        let remainder_count = specs
  778|       |            .iter()
  779|       |            .filter(|s| matches!(s.policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. }))
  780|       |            .count();
  781|       |        assert!(remainder_count >= 2);
  782|       |    }

  792|       |    #[test]
  793|       |    fn test_header_text_and_spans_to_text_variants() {
  794|       |        let spans = vec![
  795|       |            InlineSpan::Text(" Alpha ".to_string()),
  796|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("Beta".to_string()),
  797|       |            InlineSpan::Emphasis("Gamma".to_string()),
  798|       |            InlineSpan::Strikethrough("Delta".to_string()),
  799|       |            InlineSpan::Code("Epsilon".to_string()),
  800|       |            InlineSpan::Link {
  801|       |                text: "Zeta".to_string(),
  802|       |                url: "https://example.invalid".to_string(),
  803|       |            },
  804|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
  805|       |                src: "img.png".to_string(),
  806|       |                alt: "Eta".to_string(),
  807|       |                title: None,
  808|       |            },
  809|       |        ];
  810|       |
  811|       |        let header = header_text(&spans);
  812|       |        assert!(header.contains("Alpha"));
  813|       |        assert!(header.contains("Beta"));
  814|       |        assert!(header.contains("Zeta"));
  815|       |        assert!(header.contains("Eta"));
  816|       |
  817|       |        let text = spans_to_text(&spans);
  818|       |        assert!(text.contains("Alpha"));
  819|       |        assert!(text.contains("Zeta"));
  820|       |        assert!(text.contains("Eta"));
  821|       |
  822|       |        assert_eq!(header_text(&[]), "Column");
  823|       |        assert_eq!(
  824|       |            header_text(&[InlineSpan::Text("   ".to_string())]),
  825|       |            "Column"
  826|       |        );
  827|       |    }

  831|       |        let policies = vec![
  832|       |            ColumnPolicy::Auto,
  833|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
  834|       |                width: 120.0,
  835|       |                clip: true,
  836|       |            },
  837|       |            ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false },
  838|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  839|       |                min: 50.0,
  840|       |                preferred: 140.0,
  841|       |                clip: true,
  842|       |            },
  843|       |        ];
  844|       |
  845|       |        for policy in policies {
  846|       |            let _col = policy.to_column();
  847|       |            let tooltip = column_tooltip("Header", &policy).expect("tooltip");
  848|       |            assert!(tooltip.contains("Header"));
  849|       |        }
  850|       |    }

  852|       |    #[test]
  853|       |    fn test_apply_preferred_width_clamps_and_hash_stable_for_resizable() {
  854|       |        let mut spec = ColumnSpec::new(
  855|       |            0,
  856|       |            "body",
  857|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  858|       |                min: 50.0,
  859|       |                preferred: 120.0,
  860|       |                clip: false,
  861|       |            },
  862|       |            None,
  863|       |        );
  864|       |        let original_hash = spec.policy_hash;
  865|       |        spec.apply_preferred_width(10.0);
  866|       |        assert!(matches!(spec.policy, ColumnPolicy::Resizable { .. }));
  867|       |        assert_eq!(spec.policy_hash, original_hash);
  868|       |
  869|       |        let mut fixed = ColumnSpec::new(
  870|       |            1,
  871|       |            "fixed",
  872|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
  873|       |                width: 40.0,
  874|       |                clip: false,
  875|       |            },
  876|       |            None,
  877|       |        );
  878|       |        let fixed_hash = fixed.policy_hash;
  879|       |        fixed.apply_preferred_width(100.0);
  880|       |        assert_eq!(fixed.policy_hash, fixed_hash);
  881|       |    }

  885|       |        let policy = ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  886|       |            min: 40.0,
  887|       |            preferred: 120.0,
  888|       |            clip: false,
  889|       |        };

  891|       |        let second = ColumnSpec::new(1, "Header", policy, None);
  892|       |        assert_ne!(first.policy_hash, second.policy_hash);
  893|       |    }

  978|       |    #[test]
  979|       |    fn test_column_policy_hash_variants() {
  980|       |        let policies = [
  981|       |            ColumnPolicy::Auto,
  982|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
  983|       |                width: 120.0,
  984|       |                clip: true,
  985|       |            },
  986|       |            ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true },
  987|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  988|       |                min: 40.0,
  989|       |                preferred: 120.0,
  990|       |                clip: true,
  991|       |            },
  992|       |        ];
  993|       |        for policy in policies {
  994|       |            let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
  995|       |            policy.hash(&mut hasher);
  996|       |            let _ = hasher.finish();
  997|       |        }
  998|       |    }

 1000|       |    #[test]
 1001|       |    fn test_column_policy_to_column_clipped_variants() {
 1002|       |        let fixed = ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
 1003|       |            width: 120.0,
 1004|       |            clip: true,
 1005|       |        };
 1006|       |        let fixed_unclipped = ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
 1007|       |            width: 120.0,
 1008|       |            clip: false,
 1009|       |        };
 1010|       |        let remainder = ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true };
 1011|       |        let resizable = ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
 1012|       |            min: 40.0,
 1013|       |            preferred: 120.0,
 1014|       |            clip: true,
 1015|       |        };
 1016|       |        let _ = fixed.to_column();
 1017|       |        let _ = fixed_unclipped.to_column();
 1018|       |        let _ = remainder.to_column();
 1019|       |        let _ = resizable.to_column();
 1020|       |    }

 1076|       |    #[test]
 1077|       |    fn test_accumulate_stats_preserves_existing_flags() {
 1078|       |        let mut stat = ColumnStat {
 1079|       |            max_graphemes: 0,
 1080|       |            longest_word: 0,
 1081|       |            rich_content: RichContentFlags {
 1082|       |                has_link: false,
 1083|       |                has_image: true,
 1084|       |                has_emoji_like: false,
 1085|       |            },
 1086|       |        };
 1087|       |        accumulate_stats_for_cell(&[InlineSpan::Text("plain".to_string())], &mut stat);

 1181|       |    fn test_emoji_detection_covers_all_blocks() {
 1182|       |        // Basic emoticons (0x1F600-0x1F64F)
 1183|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F600}')); // grinning face
 1184|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F64F}')); // person with folded hands
 1185|       |
 1186|       |        // Miscellaneous Symbols (0x2600-0x26FF)
 1187|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{2600}')); // sun
 1188|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{26FF}')); // end of block
 1189|       |
 1190|       |        // Dingbats (0x2700-0x27BF)
 1191|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{2700}')); // black scissors
 1192|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{27BF}')); // end of block
 1193|       |
 1194|       |        // Transport and Map (0x1F680-0x1F6FF)
 1195|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F680}')); // rocket
 1196|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F6FF}')); // end of block
 1197|       |
 1198|       |        // Supplemental Symbols (0x1F900-0x1F9FF)
 1199|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F900}')); // start of block
 1200|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F9FF}')); // end of block
 1201|       |
 1202|       |        // Extended-A (0x1FA00-0x1FAFF)
 1203|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1FA00}')); // start of block
 1204|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1FAFF}')); // end of block
 1205|       |
 1206|       |        // Non-emoji should return false
 1207|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('A'));
 1208|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('z'));
 1209|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('0'));
 1210|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like(' '));
 1211|       |    }

  193|       |    #[test]
  194|       |    fn update_widths_detects_large_change() {
  195|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  196|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.0, 120.0], 1), WidthChange::None);
  197|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.2, 120.6], 2), WidthChange::Small);
  198|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 3), WidthChange::Large);
  199|       |    }
  200|       |
  201|       |    #[test]
  202|       |    fn update_widths_preserves_rows_on_width_changes() {
  203|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  204|       |
  205|       |        // Set up some rows
  206|       |        entry.ensure_row(0).max_height = 20.0;
  207|       |        entry.ensure_row(1).max_height = 25.0;
  208|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2);
  209|       |
  210|       |        // First update should preserve rows (uniform row heights approach)
  211|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.0, 120.0], 1), WidthChange::None);
  212|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "first update should preserve rows");
  213|       |
  214|       |        // Small change should preserve rows
  215|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.2, 120.6], 2), WidthChange::Small);
  216|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "small change should preserve rows");
  217|       |
  218|       |        // Large change should preserve rows
  219|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 3), WidthChange::Large);
  220|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "large change should preserve rows");
  221|       |
  222|       |        // No change should preserve rows
  223|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 4), WidthChange::None);
  224|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "no change should preserve rows");
  225|       |    }
  226|       |
  227|       |    #[test]
  228|       |    fn font_size_change_clears_persisted_widths() {
  229|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  230|       |
  231|       |        // Set some persisted widths at font size 14.0
  232|       |        entry.set_persisted_width(1, 100.0);
  233|       |        entry.set_persisted_width(2, 150.0);
  234|       |        assert!(!entry.check_font_size_change(14.0)); // First call sets baseline
  235|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), Some(100.0));
  236|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(2), Some(150.0));
  237|       |
  238|       |        // Small font size change (within epsilon) should NOT clear
  239|       |        assert!(!entry.check_font_size_change(14.3));
  240|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), Some(100.0));
  241|       |
  242|       |        // Large font size change should clear all persisted widths
  243|       |        assert!(entry.check_font_size_change(16.0));
  244|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), None);
  245|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(2), None);
  246|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
  247|       |    }
  248|       |

  547|       |#[cfg(test)]
  548|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  549|       |mod tests {
  550|       |    use super::*;
  551|       |    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
  552|       |    use tempfile::TempDir;
  553|       |
  554|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
  555|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
  556|       |        ENV_LOCK
  557|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
  558|       |            .lock()
  559|       |            .expect("env lock")
  560|       |    }
  561|       |
  562|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  563|       |        key: &'static str,
  564|       |        original: Option<String>,
  565|       |    }
  566|       |
  567|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  568|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  569|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  570|       |            std::env::set_var(key, value);
  571|       |            Self { key, original }
  572|       |        }
  573|       |
  574|       |        fn unset(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  575|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  576|       |            std::env::remove_var(key);
  577|       |            Self { key, original }
  578|       |        }
  579|       |    }
  580|       |
  581|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  582|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
  583|       |            if let Some(value) = &self.original {
  584|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, value);
  585|       |            } else {
  586|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
  587|       |            }
  588|       |        }
  589|       |    }
  590|       |
  591|       |    /// Helper to set platform-appropriate config env var and return expected config subdir.
  592|       |    /// On Windows: sets APPDATA, returns "MarkdownView"
  593|       |    /// On macOS: sets HOME, returns "Library/Application Support/MarkdownView"
  594|       |    /// On Linux: sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME, returns "mdmdview"
  595|       |    fn set_config_env(temp_path: &std::path::Path) -> (EnvGuard, PathBuf) {
  596|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  597|       |        {
  598|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  599|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("MarkdownView");
  600|       |            (guard, subdir)
  601|       |        }
  602|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
  603|       |        {
  604|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  605|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("Library/Application Support/MarkdownView");
  606|       |            (guard, subdir)
  607|       |        }
  608|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
  609|       |        {
  610|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  611|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("mdmdview");
  612|       |            (guard, subdir)
  613|       |        }
  614|       |    }
  615|       |
  616|       |    #[test]
  617|       |    fn test_sanitize_window_state_clamps_and_rejects_invalid() {
  618|       |        let invalid = WindowState {
  619|       |            pos: [f32::NAN, 10.0],
  620|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  621|       |            maximized: false,
  622|       |        };
  623|       |        assert!(sanitize_window_state(invalid).is_none());
  624|       |
  625|       |        let input = WindowState {
  626|       |            pos: [-50.0, 25000.0],
  627|       |            size: [100.0, 200.0],
  628|       |            maximized: true,
  629|       |        };
  630|       |        let sanitized = sanitize_window_state(input).expect("expected sanitized state");
  631|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.pos[0], 0.0);
  632|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.pos[1], 20000.0);
  633|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.size[0], 600.0);
  634|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.size[1], 400.0);
  635|       |        assert!(sanitized.maximized);
  636|       |    }
  637|       |

  662|       |    #[test]
  663|       |    fn test_save_and_load_window_state_from_file() {
  664|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  665|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  666|       |        let (_guard, _config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  667|       |
  668|       |        let state = WindowState {
  669|       |            pos: [120.0, 80.0],
  670|       |            size: [1024.0, 768.0],
  671|       |            maximized: false,
  672|       |        };
  673|       |        save_window_state(&state).expect("save");
  674|       |
  675|       |        let loaded = load_window_state().expect("load");
  676|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.pos, state.pos);
  677|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.size, state.size);
  678|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.maximized, state.maximized);
  679|       |    }
  680|       |
  681|       |    #[test]
  682|       |    fn test_save_window_state_creates_dir_and_writes_file() {
  683|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  684|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  685|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  686|       |
  687|       |        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(&config_dir);
  688|       |
  689|       |        let state = WindowState {
  690|       |            pos: [10.0, 20.0],
  691|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  692|       |            maximized: true,
  693|       |        };
  694|       |        save_window_state(&state).expect("save");
  695|       |
  696|       |        let path = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
  697|       |        let contents = fs::read_to_string(&path).expect("read");
  698|       |        let mut parts = contents.split_whitespace();
  699|       |        let pos_x: f32 = parts.next().expect("pos x").parse().expect("parse pos x");
  700|       |        let pos_y: f32 = parts.next().expect("pos y").parse().expect("parse pos y");
  701|       |        let size_x: f32 = parts.next().expect("size x").parse().expect("parse size x");
  702|       |        let size_y: f32 = parts.next().expect("size y").parse().expect("parse size y");
  703|       |        let max_flag = parts.next().expect("max flag");
  704|       |        assert_eq!(pos_x, state.pos[0]);
  705|       |        assert_eq!(pos_y, state.pos[1]);
  706|       |        assert_eq!(size_x, state.size[0]);
  707|       |        assert_eq!(size_y, state.size[1]);
  708|       |        assert_eq!(max_flag, "1");
  709|       |    }
  710|       |
  711|       |    #[test]
  712|       |    fn test_write_window_state_error_propagates() {
  713|       |        struct FailingWriter;
  714|       |
  715|       |        impl Write for FailingWriter {
  716|       |            fn write(&mut self, _buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {
  717|       |                Err(std::io::Error::other("fail"))
  718|       |            }
  719|       |
  720|       |            fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> {
  721|       |                Ok(())
  722|       |            }
  723|       |        }
  724|       |
  725|       |        let state = WindowState {
  726|       |            pos: [1.0, 2.0],
  727|       |            size: [3.0, 4.0],
  728|       |            maximized: false,
  729|       |        };
  730|       |        let mut writer = FailingWriter;
  731|       |        let err = write_window_state(&mut writer, &state).expect_err("err");
  732|       |        assert_eq!(err.kind(), std::io::ErrorKind::Other);
  733|       |        writer.flush().expect("flush");
  734|       |    }
  735|       |

  743|       |        let state_dir = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
  744|       |        fs::create_dir_all(&state_dir).expect("create state dir");
  745|       |
  746|       |        let state = WindowState {
  747|       |            pos: [1.0, 2.0],
  748|       |            size: [3.0, 4.0],
  749|       |            maximized: false,
  750|       |        };

  752|       |    }

  789|       |    fn test_load_window_state_prefers_registry_when_forced() {
  790|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  791|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  792|       |        let _guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp.path().to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  793|       |
  794|       |        let state = WindowState {
  795|       |            pos: [42.0, 24.0],
  796|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  797|       |            maximized: true,
  798|       |        };
  799|       |        force_registry_load_once(state);
  800|       |
  801|       |        let loaded = load_window_state().expect("load");
  802|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.pos, state.pos);
  803|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.size, state.size);
  804|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.maximized, state.maximized);
  805|       |    }

  854|       |        let _lock = env_lock();

  856|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  857|       |
  858|       |        std::fs::create_dir_all(&config_dir).expect("create config dir");
  859|       |        let config = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
  860|       |

  862|       |        assert!(load_window_state().is_none());
  863|       |

  865|       |        assert!(load_window_state().is_none());
  866|       |
  867|       |        std::fs::write(&config, "10 20 30 h 1").expect("write bad data");

  869|       |    }

  913|       |        let _guard_xdg = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp.path().to_string_lossy().as_ref());

  915|       |        let dir = config_dir().expect("config dir");

 1159|       |    #[test]
 1160|       |    #[cfg(windows)]
 1161|       |    fn test_save_app_settings_registry_error_still_writes_file() {
 1162|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
